task.goal: Create .planning/ scaffold for get-anything-done and register in planning-config.toml.

Migrate gad-01 through gad-15 decisions from root DECISIONS.xml into vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/DECISIONS.xml. Add gad-16 (no subagents until evaluated) and gad-17 (context handoff via planning docs).
Add gad pack command that bundles .planning data into a repo-planner-compatible planning pack format (JSON bundle with state, phases, tasks, decisions, requirements, docRefs).
Create vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md using GAD's own workflow â€” gad CLI commands, planning loop, context re-hydration, skill references.
Create skill stubs: skills/gad-write-intent/SKILL.md, skills/gad-write-feature-doc/SKILL.md, skills/gad-write-tech-doc/SKILL.md.
Update gadConfig.load() to read [[docs.projects]] from planning-config.toml and expose as config.docsProjects. Update gad sink status and gad refs to include docs.projects entries.
Create docs-map-reader.cjs and DOCS-MAP.xml per planning root. Extend gad refs to include docs-map entries. Update gad sink compile to handle DOCS-MAP.xml.

Create eval documentation in sink: apps/portfolio/content/docs/get-anything-done/evals.mdx covering what GAD evals are, how they work, the DEFINITIONS.md model, cli-efficiency results, portfolio-bare concept.
Update AGENTS.md in all vendor project roots to reference XML planning files and gad: commands exclusively.
Run gad:eval-run on GAD itself â€” portfolio-bare with get-anything-done requirements as the project. Measure planning loop fidelity on GAD's own workflow.
Evaluate subagent utility formally: design an eval comparing single-session vs subagent execution on a representative task. Record findings in DECISIONS.xml (may revise gad-16).
Add decisions gad-17 (revised: auto-compact over fresh sessions), gad-18 (canonical loop: snapshot â†’ work â†’ update â†’ commit), gad-19 (GSD is dead, GAD only).
Delete .claude/commands/gsd/ directory entirely (30 command files).

Rebrand .claude/hooks/ for GAD: remove gsd-check-update.js (checks npm for get-shit-done-cc). Rename gsd-context-monitor.js â†’ context-monitor.js, strip GSD references from warning messages. Rename gsd-statusline.js â†’ statusline.js, strip GSD update check and branding. Update .claude/settings.json to reference new hook filenames.

Simplify root AGENTS.md: remove the 15-file mandatory read order. Agents use gad snapshot on session start, not manual file reads. Remove GSD section. Keep build/lint/verify gates, conventions, site copy guide. Reference vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md as the canonical loop definition.

Simplify .planning/AGENTS.md: remove redundant loop definition, trigger phrase table, and pattern examples that duplicate root AGENTS.md and vendor GAD AGENTS.md. Keep XML schema reference and planning-config.toml pointers. Reference gad-18 for the loop.

Update vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md: mark as canonical loop source per gad-18. Update "Current work" section to reference phase 12. Fix gad-17 reference to reflect auto-compact over fresh sessions.

Uninstall GSD globally: delete ~/.claude/get-shit-done/, ~/.claude/commands/gsd/ (56 files), ~/.claude/hooks/gsd-*.js (5 files). Clean ~/.claude/settings.json â€” remove all GSD hooks and statusline, keep permissions and preferences.

Install all 56 GAD commands from vendor/get-anything-done/commands/gad/ as Claude Code slash commands in .claude/commands/gad/. Includes the full planning loop: plan-phase, execute-phase, discuss-phase, progress, check-todos, next, plus project lifecycle, debug, health, verify, stats, and all other commands.

Clean all GSD/get-shit-done references in vendor/get-anything-done/commands/gad/*.md and propagate to .claude/commands/gad/. Replace GSDâ†’GAD, get-shit-doneâ†’get-anything-done, /gsd:â†’/gad: throughout. Note: 115 references to get-shit-done remain as literal directory/file paths (the actual folder is named that).
Extend gad docs command: add `gad docs list --projectid <id>` that shows all docs for a project â€” planning files from sink status, DOCS-MAP entries, and docs.projects entries. Add --all flag.

Create DOCS-MAP.xml for get-anything-done with entries for evals.mdx and any other non-planning feature docs in the sink. Reader (docs-map-reader.cjs) already exists â€” this creates the actual data files.
Create DOCS-MAP.xml for grime-time, repo-planner, and any other roots that have feature docs in their sink paths. Check each root's sink directory for non-planning MDX files.
Flesh out write-intent SKILL.md: add concrete examples, template output, edge cases. The skill should be usable by any agent reading it cold â€” currently too abstract.
Flesh out write-feature-doc SKILL.md: add concrete examples of good feature docs, MDX frontmatter template, sink path resolution logic. Currently steps are correct but thin.
Flesh out write-tech-doc SKILL.md: add concrete examples, mermaid diagram patterns, decision-referencing conventions. Currently steps are correct but thin.
CLI error hardening: audit all gad.cjs commands for gad-12 compliance (missing-arg UX prints choices + rerun hint). Test edge cases: missing .planning/ dirs, malformed XML, empty files.
Run gad sink compile to sync the 7 stale files (get-anything-done and grime-time). Verify sink is fully up to date after phase 12 changes.

Create a gad eval project "escape-the-dungeon" from GAMEPLAY-DESIGN.xml and STAT-AND-BEHAVIOUR-TAXONOMY.md. Convert these source docs into a GAD REQUIREMENTS.xml in the eval project directory (evals/escape-the-dungeon/). The requirements should be structured for an agent to plan phases and implement from â€” not a raw XML dump.

Create bin/gad-tools.cjs â€” the GAD equivalent of gsd-tools.cjs. Provides the core commands that workflows depend on: init (phase-op, execute-phase, plan-phase), commit, config-get, config-set, state (load, update, record-session), phase (find, add, complete), roadmap (get-phase, analyze), generate-slug, current-timestamp, scaffold, agent-skills. Reads from XML planning files instead of MD. This replaces all gsd-tools.cjs references in workflows.

Define the eval trace format for real implementations: track which gad commands were called, which gad skills were triggered, arguments passed, token counts, time-to-completion. Extend TRACE.json schema beyond the current unit-coverage model to include tool_calls[] and skill_triggers[].

Define the eval scoring rubric for real implementations: CLI efficiency (same as v6), skill trigger accuracy (were the right skills triggered at the right time), planning quality (task coverage, state hygiene), and time-to-completion. Human feedback is a separate manual score added after.

Run the escape-the-dungeon eval: fresh agent session, agent reads requirements, plans phases, implements using the GAD loop, traces everything. Produce TRACE.json and SCORE.md. This validates the full loop end-to-end on a real project.
task.resolution: Superseded by 14.5-07 which re-ran the eval with all structural fixes applied. v2 TRACE.json exists with 34 tasks completed. The eval exposed 5 structural problems that 14.5 fixed.

task.goal: Run the portfolio-bare eval with updated tracing. Compare against prior runs. Tighten portfolio-bare based on findings from the escape-the-dungeon eval.

Review CONTEXT.md: what it is, how discuss-phase produces it, how downstream agents (researcher, planner) consume it. Evaluate whether gad CLI should have a `gad context-doc` command or if gad-tools.cjs scaffold is sufficient. Decide if CONTEXT.md should be a GAD skill output or a planning file type. Update skills and CLI to integrate CONTEXT.md into the loop if valuable.
task.resolution: Decision: CONTEXT.md stays as a discuss-phase artifact â€” no CLI command needed. gad-24 established that gad snapshot is the bootstrap mechanism; CONTEXT.md is a phase-specific planning artifact produced by discuss-phase and consumed by researcher/planner subagents. Adding a CLI command would duplicate what snapshot already provides for general context.

task.goal: Create eval template AGENTS.md that enforces the GAD loop. The eval template's .planning/ directory must include an AGENTS.md that tells the agent: (1) run gad snapshot first, (2) plan phases in ROADMAP.xml before coding, (3) update TASK-REGISTRY.xml per task, (4) update STATE.xml after each phase, (5) capture decisions. This file is what the agent reads to know how to work â€” not a prompt instruction it ignores.

Update eval-run skill/command to inject snapshot context INTO the agent prompt. Instead of telling the agent "run gad snapshot", the eval-run workflow should run the snapshot itself and include the output in the agent's initial context. The agent starts with context, not instructions to get context.

Create gad eval trace reconstruct --project <name> command. Parses git log + planning doc state to build TRACE.json after the fact. Detects: which files were created/modified per commit, which planning docs were updated, time between commits (phase duration), task completion count from TASK-REGISTRY.xml diff. This replaces manual log-cmd/log-skill with post-hoc reconstruction.

Add checkpoint enforcement to execute-phase workflow. Before the agent can start the next task, it must have: (1) updated TASK-REGISTRY.xml for the previous task, (2) updated STATE.xml next-action. The workflow checks these preconditions. If not met, it prompts the agent to update before continuing. Not a hook â€” a step in the workflow that reads the planning docs and verifies.

Auto-scaffold CONVENTIONS.md after first implementation phase of greenfield projects. When phase 01 (or first phase with code output) completes in a project with no existing CONVENTIONS.md, run a lightweight codebase scan (file structure, naming patterns, import style, content pack format) and write .planning/CONVENTIONS.md. Integrate with gad:map-codebase skill output.

Define expected_triggers for implementation evals. The TRACE.json skill_accuracy field needs expected_triggers populated before the eval runs â€” defining which skills SHOULD fire and when. Create a template expected_triggers list for implementation evals: discuss-phase before planning, plan-phase before execution, execute-phase per phase, verify-work after phases, map-codebase after first implementation.

Re-run escape-the-dungeon eval with fixes applied (template AGENTS.md, snapshot injection, checkpoint enforcement). Compare v3 TRACE.json against v2. Validate that: gad CLI is actually used, task-level tracking happens, conventions are generated, trace has real data not backfilled.
Move get-shit-done/workflows/ â†’ workflows/ at GAD repo top level. All 58 workflow .md files.
Move get-shit-done/templates/ â†’ templates/ at GAD repo top level. All 34 template files.
Move get-shit-done/references/ â†’ references/ at GAD repo top level. All 15 reference files.

Update all 42 command @-path references from get-shit-done/workflows/ â†’ workflows/, get-shit-done/templates/ â†’ templates/, get-shit-done/references/ â†’ references/. Sync to .claude/commands/gad/.
Delete get-shit-done/ directory entirely (bin/, lib/, workflows/, templates/, references/). gsd-tools.cjs replaced by gad-tools.cjs. Verify no remaining references.
Copy eval v3 game build (dist/) to apps/portfolio/public/evals/escape-the-dungeon/ for docs site hosting. Verify it's servable as static page.

Review RepoPlanner sprint model: read scripts/loop-cli.mjs sprint commands (set-size, show, context), understand how sprintSize groups phases, how context sprint filters to sprint-scoped paths and task counts. Document what RP gives that GAD doesn't.

Add sprint concept to GAD: configurable sprint size in planning-config.toml (default 5 phases). Add gad sprint show (list sprint boundaries) and gad sprint context (sprint-scoped paths + summary) commands to gad.cjs.

Add gad snapshot --sprint mode: collapse done phases to id|title|done (no goal text), inline only active/planned phases within current sprint, show only open tasks (planned/in-progress), omit completed task history. Measure token reduction vs current full dump.

Add file refs to snapshot: for each active/in-progress task, include code and doc files touched (from git log scoped to project path). Add recent git log (last 5-10 commits for the project). These give the agent immediate code context without manual file reads.

Add file refs to TASK-REGISTRY.xml schema: optional <files> block per task listing paths touched during implementation. Update gad-tools.cjs and any task-completion workflows to prompt for file list on task done.
Measure optimized snapshot token count. Target: ~3,000 tokens for get-anything-done (currently ~9,750). Run cli-efficiency eval v7 with sprint-scoped snapshot. Compare composite score vs v6.
Update docs/context-budget.md with new snapshot token counts and sprint context window analysis. Update DEFINITIONS.md if sprint changes the information unit model.
Run gad:reverse-engineer on vendor/repub-builder/src/reader to produce REQUIREMENTS.xml, DECISIONS.xml, CONVENTIONS.md, ROADMAP.xml for the reader-workspace eval.
task.resolution: Completed in prior session. Template directory has all 5 planning artifacts (351 lines total). REQUIREMENTS.xml has 118 lines of structured requirements.

task.goal: Create reader-workspace eval project with REQUIREMENTS.md, template/, and AGENTS.md.
task.resolution: Completed in prior session. Eval project at evals/reader-workspace/ with REQUIREMENTS.md (89 lines, 51 success criteria) and template with AGENTS.md + .planning/ scaffold.

task.goal: Run reader-workspace eval v1: fresh agent builds the EPUB reader from reverse-engineered requirements alone. Produce TRACE.json and SCORE.md. Validates the full reverse-engineer â†’ implement cycle.
task.resolution: Agent completed 12/12 phases, 24/24 tasks, 34 source files (~3300 lines). 89K tokens, 105 tool uses, ~13 min. 47/51 criteria fully met (4 partial: sc-03, sc-10, sc-34, sc-40). All 12 architectural decisions followed. Planning docs maintained per-task. Composite score: 0.821.

task.goal: Score reader-workspace v1: requirement coverage (51 criteria), architecture alignment (12 decisions), state hygiene, convention adherence. Compare output against reference implementation quality.
task.resolution: TRACE.json written. Scores: requirement_coverage=0.961, architecture_alignment=1.0, planning_quality=1.0, skill_accuracy=0.80, time_efficiency=0.973, per_task_discipline=0.50, composite=0.821. Key finding: agent committed per-phase (12 commits for 24 tasks) not per-task â€” discipline gap to address in 18-05.

task.goal: Replace the stub in `gad eval run` with a prompt generator: read the eval template (AGENTS.md, REQUIREMENTS.xml, DECISIONS.xml, CONVENTIONS.md, ROADMAP.xml, STATE.xml), construct a full bootstrap prompt, and output it to stdout + eval-output.txt. The user/agent copies this prompt into their AI tool. Agent-agnostic per gad-01.
task.resolution: buildEvalPrompt() reads all template files and constructs a full bootstrap prompt. `gad eval run --prompt-only` generates without worktree. PROMPT.md written to run directory with char/token counts. Supports source-*.md/xml docs with truncation.

task.goal: Add `gad eval suite` CLI command: lists all eval projects with templates, generates bootstrap prompts for each, outputs them as separate files in a timestamped suite run directory. Shows a summary table of what will be run.
task.resolution: evalSuite command finds all projects with template/ dirs, builds prompts for each, writes to .suite-runs/ timestamped dir with SUITE.json manifest. Supports --projects filter. Tested: 3 projects (escape-the-dungeon 4514 tokens, portfolio-bare 1251 tokens, reader-workspace 9469 tokens).

task.goal: Add `gad eval report` cross-project comparison: reads TRACE.json from latest run of each project, produces a comparison table (phases, tasks, discipline, scores) across all projects. Supports --all flag for all projects or specific --project list.
task.resolution: evalReport command reads latest TRACE.json per project, produces table with phases, tasks, discipline, planning quality, skill accuracy, and composite scores. Shows average composite and lists projects without traces. Tested across 8 projects.

task.goal: Add comprehensive logging: GAD CLI call logger (JSONL per day), PostToolUse hook for tool tracing (Bash/Read/Write/Edit/Agent/Skill/Grep/Glob), `gad log show|stats|clear` commands, `gad eval trace from-log` for definitive trace building from actual logs, GAD_LOG_DIR env for eval-scoped logs.
task.resolution: gad-25 decision. CLI logger in gad.cjs (process.on exit), tool-trace.js hook in .claude/hooks/, registered in settings.json. Log show has --filter (cli|tool|gad|skill|agent). trace from-log builds TRACE.json from actual JSONL. .gad-log/ gitignored for dev, preserved in eval runs.

task.goal: Run the full eval suite: portfolio-bare v3, escape-the-dungeon v4, reader-workspace v2. Launch all three in parallel worktree agents. Reconstruct traces and produce cross-project report.
task.resolution: All 3 evals completed in parallel. portfolio-bare v3: 0.944 composite (1.0 discipline, 12/12 tasks). escape-the-dungeon v4: 0.916 composite (0.89 discipline, 19/19 tasks, 12/12 criteria â€” up from 0.285 in v2). reader-workspace v2: 0.928 composite (0.93 discipline, 40/40 tasks, 51/51 criteria â€” up from 0.821 in v1). Average composite: 0.941.

task.goal: Iterate on findings: review eval results, tighten at least one skill based on patterns, update eval-suite SKILL.md with lessons learned, add any new eval criteria discovered.
task.resolution: Root cause: skill_accuracy 0.80 = verify-work never triggered. Fix: (1) all 3 eval AGENTS.md templates now require VERIFICATION.md after each phase with "verify:" commit prefix, (2) trace reconstruct detects VERIFICATION.md existence OR verify: commits, (3) execute-phase skill now includes verification step before SUMMARY.md, (4) buildEvalPrompt instructions include verification. Per-task discipline gap (0.50â†’0.89-1.0) already addressed by prompt emphasis in 18-04. YAML frontmatter fixed for Codex compatibility.

task.goal: Document tooling eval contract: extend TRACE.json with eval_type tooling|mcp, trace_schema_version, nested tooling or mcp metrics blocks, optional metrics.json for granular histograms. Record gad-26â€“gad-28 in DECISIONS.xml. Add a short section to vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md (or evals/README) describing implementation vs tooling evals and how regressions are compared.
task.resolution: DEFINITIONS.md updated with tooling eval trace format, MCP trace format, scores.json schema, tooling composite formula, and metrics.json guidance. trace_schema_version field added to base schema.

task.goal: Implement `gad dev` or `gad watch planning`: debounced watch on .planning/**/*.xml (configurable), invoke existing refs verify logic, print one JSON summary line per run (ok, missing paths, timing) for scripts and terminal/CI consumers. Optional --poll for unreliable FS watchers. No network by default.
task.resolution: watch-planning.cjs library (startWatch, runVerify, findPlanningDirs). gad dev command with --debounce, --poll, --once flags. JSON output per verify run. Fixed stale refs (quick/SKILL.md deleted, planning-config.tomlâ†’gad-config.toml). 75 XML files verified clean in 1.3s.

task.goal: Add evals/tooling-watch-baseline (or equivalent): fixed fixture repo with known good/bad paths, automated Node harness that starts watcher, performs scripted edits, asserts correctness + latency percentiles, writes evals/<project>/vN/TRACE.json with eval_type tooling. Runnable in CI without an LLM.
task.resolution: evals/tooling-watch/ with fixture/.planning/DECISIONS.xml (1 good path, 1 bad path), harness.cjs (6 tests: detect-missing, accept-good, latency p50/p95/avg, xml-count). v1 results: 6/6 passed, composite 0.989, p50=4ms, p95=14ms. No LLM needed, runs in 64ms.

task.goal: Harden implementation eval scoring automation: generate scores.json alongside SCORE.md from score-generator; change gad eval scores to prefer JSON totals over SCORE.md regex. Prepare same pattern for tooling composite (tooling_composite) so weights can evolve without breaking old runs.
task.resolution: score-generator.cjs now emits scores.json alongside SCORE.md. JSON contains eval name, version, eval_type, trace_schema_version, composite, and all dimension scores. gad eval report updated to prefer scores.json over TRACE.json. Tooling dimensions (correctness, latency, tooling_composite, mcp_composite) supported.

task.goal: Optional MCP server package: thin adapter over the same core as CLI (verify, snapshot summary, session status). Stdio MCP; document Cursor config. Agents may use it; primary user remains human IDE workflows.
task.resolution: gad-mcp.cjs created with 6 tools (gad_snapshot, gad_state, gad_tasks, gad_phases, gad_decisions, gad_verify). Stdio MCP transport with Content-Length framing. initialize + tool calls work. tools/list has a sequencing issue to fix in follow-up.

task.goal: MCP tooling eval: golden tool invocations, cold-start and per-tool latency, error rate on bad args. Produces eval_type mcp TRACE.json; compare across gad releases.
task.resolution: evals/tooling-mcp/harness.cjs with 6 tests (initialize, cold-start, tools/list, gad_verify, gad_state, gad_snapshot). v1 results: 3/6 passed (0.697 composite), cold start 542ms. tools/list timeout is a protocol sequencing issue to fix.

task.goal: IDE integration: document VS Code / Cursor Tasks (background watch), optional problem matcher mapping JSON errors to Problems panel. Update portfolio sink evals.mdx when behavior is stable.
task.resolution: Out of scope for now â€” no IDE/editor integrations until explicitly re-opened. Remaining Phase 20 work is CLI, MCP optional, evals, and metrics only.

task.goal: Extend gad eval report and gad eval diff to include tooling/mcp runs (filter by eval_type, compare tooling.metrics and composite). Ensure suite or follow-up command can run implementation + tooling evals in one report.
task.resolution: eval report now shows TYPE column (impl/tooling/mcp), reads tooling_composite/mcp_composite, prefers scores.json. All 6 scored projects visible in one table: 4 implementation + 1 tooling + 1 MCP. Average composite 0.901.

task.goal: Fix MCP UTF-8 buffer bug: Content-Length counts bytes but handleData used string.length. tools/list responses with em dashes broke the parser. Switch to Buffer throughout MCP harness and server.
task.resolution: Both gad-mcp.cjs and evals/tooling-mcp/harness.cjs now use Buffer.concat instead of string concat. tooling-mcp composite went 0.697 â†’ 0.853.

task.goal: Speed up planning-ref-verify by reading from planning-config roots instead of full tree walk, plus cache fs.statSync results.
task.resolution: walkXmlFromConfig added. Verify time: 2.5s â†’ 0.9s (warm cache 0.2s). 76 XML files covered.

task.goal: Standardize implementation eval composite formula. Replace inconsistent per-eval weights with a single v3 formula: requirement_coverage + planning_quality + per_task_discipline + skill_accuracy + time_efficiency + human_review. Document in DEFINITIONS.md.
task.resolution: Initial v3: (0.20, 0.20, 0.20, 0.15, 0.10, 0.15). Later raised human_review to 0.30 per gad-34. All traces re-scored. auto_composite normalizes when human_review null.

task.goal: Clean skill_accuracy: remove methodology behaviors (decisions-captured, multi-phase-planning, phase-completion, task-lifecycle) from expected_triggers. Only actual /gad:* skill invocations count.
task.resolution: Three TRACE.json files cleaned (escape-the-dungeon v5, portfolio-bare v4, reader-workspace v3). All now show 6/6 real skills triggered. Decision gad-29 notes skill_accuracy no longer discriminates â€” needs quality scoring.

task.goal: Add gad eval review CLI command to record human review scores that get folded into the composite with the v3 weights.
task.resolution: gad eval review <project> <version> --score 0-1 --notes "...". Writes to TRACE.json and scores.json. Applies low-score caps (< 0.20 â†’ 0.40, < 0.10 â†’ 0.25).

task.goal: Add gad eval open CLI command to serve eval build output via local HTTP server (file:// doesn't work for ES module bundles from Vite/KAPLAY).
task.resolution: gad eval open <project> [version]. Spawns npx serve on a random port, opens browser. Version-aware path resolution.

task.goal: Create escape-the-dungeon-bare eval project: same game requirements, no GAD framework, agent creates own workflow. Baseline A/B comparison for the freedom hypothesis.
task.resolution: evals/escape-the-dungeon-bare/ with template/AGENTS.md (research Ralph Wiggum loop, create own workflow). Runs v1 (0.10), v2 (0.50 â€” best), v3 (0.70 â€” highest of any game eval).

task.goal: Create escape-the-dungeon-emergent eval project: no framework, but inherits skills from the previous run. Tests whether self-created systems improve iteratively.
task.resolution: evals/escape-the-dungeon-emergent/ with template/skills/ containing inherited patterns. v1 (0.10 â€” crashed on styled text), v2 (0.50 â€” functional forge).

task.goal: Version game requirements. Document v1â†’v2â†’v3 history inside REQUIREMENTS.xml. Each eval round should reference the requirements version it used.
task.resolution: All 3 escape-the-dungeon* templates use <requirements version="v3" updated="2026-04-08">. Version history in XML comment header. TRACE.json references requirements_version.

task.goal: Build requirements v3: add gate criteria (game loop, spell crafting, UI quality) that override requirement_coverage to 0 if failed. Trim source gameplay design doc from 640 â†’ 120 lines for the vertical slice focus.
task.resolution: REQUIREMENTS.xml v3 with 3 gates (G1 game-loop, G2 spell-crafting, G3 ui-quality). Source gameplay design trimmed. Vertical slice priority mandated.

task.goal: Run round 2 greenfield evals with v2 requirements. Three conditions: GAD v7, Bare v2, Emergent v1.
task.resolution: GAD v7 = 0.30 (stuck after combat), Bare v2 = 0.50 (most playable, ASCII UI), Emergent v1 = 0.10 (styled text crash). Findings: bare beats GAD consistently.

task.goal: Run round 3 greenfield evals with v3 requirements (gates). GAD v8, Bare v3, Emergent v2.
task.resolution: GAD v8 = 0.20 (broken forge, ASCII), Bare v3 = 0.70 (best game overall, most enjoyable), Emergent v2 = 0.50 (functional forge, medium UI). Freedom hypothesis confirmed â€” bare wins.

task.goal: Document the freedom hypothesis finding: GAD framework constraint correlates inversely with creative output quality on greenfield game implementation. Capture in FINDINGS doc and decision.
task.resolution: evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-08-round-3.md with full analysis. Decision gad-36 captures the hypothesis as PRELIMINARY status. Brownfield experiments may invalidate or confirm.

task.goal: Build eval preservation contract: gad eval preserve command that copies code+planning+build+logs to canonical per-version locations. Enforce via tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs.
task.resolution: gad eval preserve and gad eval verify commands. 6-test suite (tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs) that fails the build if any run after the contract cutoff is missing artifacts. Decision gad-38 captures the contract.

task.goal: Fix eval preserve to copy the ENTIRE project (not cherry-picked files). Agent may create files we don't anticipate.
task.resolution: Copies every top-level entry in game/ except node_modules/.git/dist. Re-preserved all 7 round-3 runs. Adds _worktree_root_extras/ for agent-created files at worktree root that aren't part of the main repo.

task.goal: Mandate game/.planning/ as the home for all eval workflow artifacts regardless of framework. Bare and emergent must put workflow files there too, not at project root.
task.resolution: All 3 greenfield AGENTS.md updated with Project Layout section. eval preserve detects and warns on violations. Decision gad-39 captures the contract.

task.goal: Add gad worktree management commands: list, show, clean, prune. User had no visibility into .claude/worktrees/agent-* directories.
task.resolution: gad worktree list/show/clean/prune. Prune --older-than 1d --dry-run safely removes stale. Cleaned 28 worktrees â†’ 10 (only preserved round 3 runs remain).

task.goal: Categorize evals by eval_mode (greenfield|brownfield) and workflow (gad|bare|emergent). Show in gad eval list.
task.resolution: gad.json now has eval_mode and workflow fields. gad eval list shows MODE and WORKFLOW columns. Decision gad-40 captures the categorization.

task.goal: Create three brownfield eval projects (etd-brownfield-gad/bare/emergent) using bare v3 as baseline. Shared extension requirements (_brownfield-extensions-v4.md) describing v4 features (floor progression, respawning encounters, forge affinity, skills system, visual upgrade).
task.resolution: Three eval projects with gad.json baseline references. Template AGENTS.md short and points at baseline. Prompt generator auto-injects baseline copy instructions.

task.goal: Port session tracking into TASK-REGISTRY.xml instead of Claude Code Task tool. Stop using ephemeral session tasks for GAD-tracked work.
task.resolution: Added tasks 21-01 through 21-24 to TASK-REGISTRY.xml documenting phase 21 work. ROADMAP.xml phase 20 marked done, phase 21 added as active. STATE.xml next-action updated to reflect real current state. Going forward, all GAD-tracked work lives in TASK-REGISTRY.xml not ephemeral Claude Task tool.

task.goal: Create an experiment log doc (evals/EXPERIMENT-LOG.md) that tracks every round: conditions, requirements version, results, findings. Running ledger across all rounds.
task.resolution: Created evals/EXPERIMENT-LOG.md with rounds 1-3 documented. Also created evals/REQUIREMENTS-VERSIONS.md tracking v1â†’v2â†’v3 requirements evolution. Both are append-only running ledgers that future sessions can read to re-ground.

task.goal: Build requirements v4: pressure-oriented design (not features checklist). Authored dungeon with floors â†’ 5-8 rooms â†’ boss gate. Room types: Combat, Elite, Forge, Rest, Event. Each floor must introduce a mechanical constraint that can't be brute-forced with default spells. Forge is gate with at least one encounter per floor that favors crafted/adapted spells. Pressure mechanics required from at least 2 of 4 categories. Starter abilities explicitly insufficient. Skills scored but not gate (combat needs non-spell action category only). Sprites: attempt sourcing first, coherent fallback allowed. Traits label in UI, narrativeStats in code. Add ingenuity/adaptation scoring dimension.
task.resolution: Wrote v4 REQUIREMENTS.xml with 4 gates (G1 game-loop, G2 forge-with-ingenuity-payoff, G3 ui-quality, G4 pressure-mechanics), authored dungeon structure (floors â†’ 5-8 rooms â†’ boss), forge integrated with encounter design, starter abilities explicitly insufficient, ingenuity scoring rubric, Traits/narrativeStats split. Committed to all three greenfield templates (escape-the-dungeon, -bare, -emergent). Requirements version history logged in evals/REQUIREMENTS-VERSIONS.md. Eight new decisions logged (gad-41 through gad-48).

task.goal: Create create_skill skill doc (skills/create-skill/SKILL.md) that documents how bare/emergent agents can define their own reusable skills. Only skill bare starts with; emergent starts with create_skill + inherited skills from previous runs.
task.resolution: Created skills/create-skill/SKILL.md with trigger rules, file layout (game/.planning/skills/<name>.md), required section structure (When to use / The pattern / Why / Failure modes / Related), writing rules (lead with trigger, show working code, explain the failure you avoided, write immediately, update-don't-duplicate, delete wrong skills), and separate guidance for emergent (inheritance + CHANGELOG.md) vs bare (starts with only create-skill and find-sprites) conditions. Copied into both eval templates under template/skills/create-skill.md.

task.goal: Rewrite bare and emergent AGENTS.md to be truly minimal. Stop mandating ARCHITECTURE.md, CHANGELOG.md, or any specific artifact. Only mandate: (1) all workflow artifacts in game/.planning/, (2) reference create_skill skill for defining new skills. Everything else is agent-defined. This defeats the previous round's too-prescriptive guidance and lets us observe what emergent workflows actually produce.
task.resolution: Rewrote bare/template/AGENTS.md and emergent/template/AGENTS.md. Both now mandate only: (1) workflow artifacts live under game/.planning/, (2) dist/ build + playable index.html, (3) emergent adds skills/CHANGELOG.md. No more ARCHITECTURE.md mandate, no prescribed phases, no specific tracking format. Gates updated to v4 (G1-G4 including pressure-mechanics). Both reference inherited create-skill and find-sprites as the bootstrap skills. Stale "previous run failure" sections removed â€” lineage is captured in inherited skills instead.

task.goal: Create gad:find-sprites skill (skills/find-sprites/SKILL.md) that documents the asset sourcing workflow: web search for free/open-license sprites and icons, preference order (npm packs â†’ web search â†’ generated â†’ geometric fallback), integration tips, license verification. Referenced by v4 AGENTS.md for all 3 greenfield conditions.
task.resolution: Created skills/find-sprites/SKILL.md with 5-tier preference order: (1) installable icon packs (@iconify-json/game-icons highlighted as single-highest-leverage install), (2) web-searched CC0/CC-BY sprite packs (OpenGameArt, Kenney.nl, itch.io), (3) generated SVG/canvas/emoji, (4) geometric primitives (scored against), (5) raw ASCII = gate failure. Includes license discipline (LICENSES.md pattern), coherence-over-polish guidance (pick one perspective/fidelity/palette before sourcing), and failure modes. Copied into both bare and emergent eval templates.

task.goal: Run round 4 greenfield with v4 requirements. Three conditions: GAD, Bare, Emergent. All with pressure-oriented gates.
task.resolution: Attempt #1 (parallel): rate-limited â€” escape-the-dungeon v9, escape-the-dungeon-bare v4, escape-the-dungeon-emergent v3 all hit shared account rate limit at ~14 min. Preserved partial, decisions gad-62 (serial default) and gad-63 (exclude rate-limited from comparisons). Attempt #2 (serial): Bare v5 + Emergent v4 completed cleanly with green builds, GAD v10 API-interrupted twice (HTTP 529 overloaded) at phases 01+02 completion. Partial GAD preserved with api_interrupted flag + decision gad-64. Round 4 data is two completed + one api-interrupted; freedom hypothesis holds under v4. Remaining: GAD v11 retry pending 529 investigation (task 21-23b).

task.goal: Retry GAD greenfield v11 after HTTP 529 investigation. First attempt at v10 (tool_uses=55) completed phases 01+02 (scaffold + data layer) before Anthropic API returned overloaded_error. Previous attempt crashed earlier at tool_uses=18. Pattern suggests GAD's setup-heavy phases (snapshot + planning XML writes) spend proportionally more time in server-dependent states during peak load periods. Investigation needed: (1) check Anthropic status page for correlation with our crash timestamps; (2) test whether spreading the snapshot + plan calls across shorter API invocations reduces 529 exposure; (3) consider a retry-with-backoff wrapper in the eval runner for 529 specifically (not for rate limits). Only retry GAD v11 after investigation produces a mitigation or explicit "try again, the API was just overloaded that hour" conclusion.
Run round 1 brownfield experiments. Three conditions using bare v3 baseline + v4 extensions. BLOCKED on portfolio documentation (21-28).

Plan the portfolio experiment documentation structure â€” pivoted to a standalone Next.js site at vendor/get-anything-done/site/ instead of apps/portfolio docs. Designed single-page sections (workflow, rounds, projects, results, playable, requirements, catalog, templates) plus dynamic per-run pages.

task.goal: `gad install` CLI subcommand as a local-checkout-friendly wrapper around existing bin/install.js. The underlying install.js already supports --claude --cursor --codex --opencode --gemini --copilot --antigravity --windsurf --augment --all --uninstall and ships the full GSD-pattern cross-runtime install â€” but it's hardcoded for the npm-published package layout (expects to find files under `node_modules/get-anything-done/get-anything-done/`). This task wraps it in a `gad install` subcommand that resolves paths correctly whether running from a git checkout (vendor/get-anything-done/) or an npm install. Related to 22-34 (fix install.js directly) â€” this task may become a no-op if 22-34 fixes the path resolution at the install.js level.
Fix path resolution bug in bin/install.js for local git checkouts.
task.resolution: Two fixes in bin/install.js: (1) added existence check before copying the get-anything-done skill directory â€” local checkouts skip gracefully with a warning instead of crashing on ENOENT. (2) ensure the CHANGELOG/VERSION destination parent directory (targetDir/get-anything-done/) exists via mkdir recursive, since the prior skill copy may have been skipped. Verified by running `node bin/install.js --claude --cursor --codex --local` to clean completion across all three runtimes. Test also merged existing settings hooks successfully.

task.goal: Design trace event envelope + 4 event types as a reusable library in lib/trace-schema.cjs.
task.resolution: lib/trace-schema.cjs exports TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION=4, EVENT_TYPES (tool_use, skill_invocation, subagent_spawn, file_mutation), factory functions for each event type, type guard isTraceEvent, and parseTraceEventsJsonl parser. Each factory takes a plain options object and returns an envelope with ts (ISO8601), seq (monotonic int), type, and type-specific fields. Smoke-tested via node REPL: factories return valid events, type guard round-trips, parser handles invalid lines gracefully.

task.goal: Write the hook handler script at bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs that Claude Code invokes for PreToolUse/PostToolUse.
task.resolution: bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs reads hook payload from stdin, finds project root by walking up for .planning/, reads .planning/.trace-active-skill marker, detects transitions against .planning/.trace-last-skill file, and appends events to .planning/.trace-events.jsonl synchronously. PreToolUse emits only skill_invocation events (on transition); PostToolUse emits tool_use plus conditionally subagent_spawn (for Task tool) and file_mutation (for Write/Edit/NotebookEdit). Monotonic seq persisted in .planning/.trace-seq. Never blocks the main agent â€” all errors logged to stderr and hook exits 0. Verified end-to-end with a mock PostToolUse Bash payload: valid tool_use event appended correctly.

task.goal: 4 KB truncation helper with head+tail split for trace event outputs.
task.resolution: lib/trace-truncate.cjs exports truncateOutput({ value, truncated, originalBytes }) and truncateInputsObject helpers. Default cap 4096 bytes (decision gad-60), split 2048 head + 2048 tail joined by "... [truncated N bytes] ..." marker. Multibyte-safe via Buffer slicing. Returns the truncated flag for the caller to stamp on the event. Smoke-tested: 11-byte input passes through (truncated=false, 11 bytes); 5000-byte input truncates to 4127 bytes (truncated=true, original=5000).

task.goal: `gad install hooks` CLI subcommand that wires the trace hook into Claude Code settings.json.
task.resolution: Added install + uninstall subcommands in bin/gad.cjs. `gad install hooks` (optional --global) merges a PreToolUse + PostToolUse entry pointing at the absolute path to bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs into .claude/settings.json (or ~/.claude/settings.json with --global). Merges into existing hooks â€” preserves all other entries. Idempotent: existing GAD trace entries (detected by "gad-trace-hook" substring) are filtered before re-adding. `gad uninstall hooks` is the reverse. `gad install all --claude --cursor --codex --local` delegates to bin/install.js as a wrapper satisfying task 22-33 via delegation. Verified: installed into a settings.json with 8 existing hook entries, all preserved, new GAD entries added to PreToolUse and PostToolUse.

task.goal: Fix YAML parsing errors in session-authored skill frontmatter (create-skill, objective-eval-design). Descriptions with embedded colons like "methodology:" and "discipline:" were parsed by js-yaml as key-value separators, breaking skill loading in Claude Code. Fix by converting plain scalar descriptions to YAML folded block scalar form (`description: >- ...`) which treats the body as opaque.
task.resolution: Fixed create-skill and objective-eval-design. Updated create-skill/SKILL.md body to document the YAML frontmatter discipline (folded block scalar, colons are safe inside, test with js-yaml). Validated all 5 session-authored skills now parse clean. Claude Code system reminder confirms all GAD skills load with no YAML errors.

Adopt agentskills.io .agents/skills/ convention (gad-80). Extend bin/install.js to copy skills to BOTH .claude/skills/ (native) AND .agents/skills/ (cross-client) for supported runtimes. Migrate the fundamental triumvirate (create-skill, merge-skill, find-skills per gad-73) to the new location once those skills exist. Required for the codex-vs-claude comparison eval (task 89) because Codex uses different discovery conventions â€” if we target .agents/skills/, both runtimes find the same skills. Also honor the excluded-from-default-install:true frontmatter flag I added to the emergent skill â€” the installer must skip those.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-09. Migration steps: (1) `git mv skills/ .agents/skills/` moved all 28 skill directories including the emergent subdir to the agentskills.io canonical location. (2) Prebuild SKILLS_DIR constant updated to read from `.agents/skills/`; LEGACY_SKILLS_DIR kept as a fallback in case of rollback. Generated TS file paths updated to `vendor/get-anything-done/.agents/skills/...`. (3) install.js extended (line 4712+): new workspace-skills-copy section walks `.agents/skills/*`, honors the `excluded-from-default-install: true` frontmatter flag, explicitly skips the `emergent/` subdirectory, and copies each approved skill to BOTH `skills/` (Claude Code native) and `.agents/skills/` (cross-client). Emergent skills are skipped and logged so operators see what was filtered. (4) /standards page + /skills index cite the new convention. (5) create-skill is the only triumvirate member currently present â€” merge-skill and find-skills still need to be authored (gad-73 follow-up).

task.goal: Build a /standards page on the site citing both the Anthropic skills guide (resources.anthropic.com/hubfs/The-Complete-Guide-to-Building-Skill-for-Claude.pdf) and agentskills.io. Link from /skills, /security, /methodology, /gad. The page must summarize: (a) SKILL.md format + required frontmatter, (b) progressive disclosure three-tier loading, (c) name collision handling, (d) the per-skill evaluation methodology (with_skill vs without_skill baseline per agentskills.io/skill-creation/evaluating-skills). This becomes the reference page every skill-related section of the site cites instead of repeating the content inline.

Worktree isolation audit script (gad-82, open question worktree-isolation-verification). Write scripts/audit-worktree-isolation.mjs that walks a fresh eval worktree and reports: which files from the parent repo are reachable (by relative path traversal), what settings from .claude/settings.json inherit, what env vars are set, what's on PATH, which workspace skills at vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ could be picked up by a scan that walks parent dirs. Output structured report. ROUND 5 IS BLOCKED on either passing this audit or documenting which parent-repo state is explicitly allowed (e.g. the trace hook handler is allowed because it's the instrumentation layer per gad-50; workspace skills are NOT allowed because bare condition requires zero framework affordances).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-10 as .planning/docs/ISOLATION-AUDIT-2026-04-10.md. PASSES with explicit allowlist. Git worktrees have separate working trees so .agents/skills/ and .planning/ do NOT inherit. .claude/settings.json IS inherited but the only settings are the trace hook handler (instrumentation, not framework assistance â€” explicitly allowed per gad-82). gad eval run creates worktrees at os.tmpdir() (entirely outside the repo â€” even cleaner). Secondary vector: globally-installed user skills at ~/.claude/skills/ are mitigated by adding a bare-eval-prompt instruction. ROUND 5 IS UNBLOCKED. Open question worktree-isolation-verification resolved.

task.goal: Skill collision detection at prebuild (gad-81). Scan every skills/*/SKILL.md description for overlapping trigger keywords. Compute a keyword profile per skill, find overlaps above a threshold, emit a collision report to data/skill-collisions.json. Add a "collision candidates" section to the /skills site page showing any detected overlaps with a merge-skill suggestion link (merge-skill itself not yet built â€” link falls back to a manual-review prompt). Also: a keyword index for every skill so users searching can see what terms trigger each.

Extract thinking blocks from session.jsonl into TRACE.json derived.reasoning_trace (gad-84, GAPS G13). Post-hoc offline enrichment â€” no new hooks required. Walk a run's session.jsonl, pull every thinking block and inter-tool message text, attach to TRACE.json with timestamps tying thoughts to subsequent tool uses. First exploratory analysis: thought-to-action interval, thought length distribution, keyword presence (exploration vs execution), thought-before-action vs thought-after-action ordering. This is the highest-leverage way to get "agent reasoning" signal without Claude Code exposing new hook surfaces.

Scaffold new eval domain evals/skill-evaluation-app/ per decision gad-88. Third task-domain alongside escape-the-dungeon (game dev) and gad-explainer-video (video composition). Tests the same hypotheses (freedom / CSH / emergent-evolution) on GUI app development. The GUI, once built, is also part of the GAD eval framework (dogfood).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-09. evals/skill-evaluation-app/gad.json with 5-dimension rubric (ui_usability 0.30, requirements_ergonomics 0.20, harness_integration 0.20, visualization_quality 0.15, stability 0.15), workflow:gad, compare_to bare + emergent siblings. evals/skill-evaluation-app/REQUIREMENTS.md with full scope, 4 mandatory gate frames (G1 requirements load, G2 pressure preview live, G3 harness viewer live, G4 mobile responsive), pre-run checklist. Siblings (skill-evaluation-app-bare, -emergent) still to be scaffolded in a follow-up.

task.goal: Build the GAD-native per-skill evaluation harness: gad eval skill <name> CLI subcommand. Reads evals/evals.json inside the skill directory, spawns clean-context subagent runs (with_skill + without_skill) via gad worktree, captures trace events from v4 schema (gad-50) as the observable signal, grades assertions by querying trace events + file mutations + commit log (NOT LLM self-report â€” that's what skill-creator's Python harness does and it didn't work for us), emits benchmark.json compatible with agentskills.io format. This is the load-bearing piece that lets every GAD skill graduate from experimental to canonical per gad-86. Directly answers programmatic-eval GAPS G2 + the hygiene half of G11.

Author merge-skill + find-skills SKILL.md files as workspace skills. find-skills is a thin methodology wrapper around npx skills (vercel-labs/skills CLI per gad-85). merge-skill is a pure methodology doc for fusing overlapping skills. Both start as experimental (status: experimental) because no evaluation harness has been run against them yet.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-09. .agents/skills/merge-skill/SKILL.md authored with full procedure (6 steps: identify merge â†’ draft merged skill â†’ deprecate originals â†’ update CHANGELOG â†’ run harness â†’ notify inheriting runs). .agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md authored as thin wrapper around npx skills find, with 4-step procedure, common-searches table, external-source suggestions (MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done, anthropics/skills, skills.sh). Both marked status:experimental pending the harness from 22-52. Completes 2 of 3 triumvirate members â€” create-skill was already present.

task.goal: Define the executable packaging target for gad: Windows, macOS, and Linux standalone binaries with no Node requirement. Choose and document the packaging toolchain, output artifact names, signing/notarization assumptions, and which runtime files (CLI bundle, hooks, templates, skills) must ship alongside the executable.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-11. Implemented standalone packaging with Node SEA + postject via scripts/build-release.mjs, scripts/build-release-support.mjs, and scripts/sea-bootstrap.cjs. Packaged builds extract a versioned support tree, route installer execution through the executable, and emit gad-v<version>-windows|linux|macos-<arch> artifact names. Verified a local Windows x64 build and packaged install-all flow.

task.goal: Implement the official GitHub-first install flow in docs and CLI messaging. Primary path: GitHub Release executable. Secondary path: direct GitHub repo/Node install. Remove or rewrite remaining npm-first copy in quick start, README, starter READMEs, and site pages.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-11. Rewrote README.md, docs/quick-start.md, docs/eval-guide.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/release-guide.md, and workflows/help.md so GitHub Release executables are the primary install path, GitHub checkout + Node is the secondary path, and Windows packaged install/self-install behavior is documented explicitly.

task.goal: Publish installable GAD skills through skills.sh / `npx skills add` using the GitHub repo as the source of truth. Document the exact command(s), verify the repo is listable/installable from skills.sh, and make skill installation distinct from CLI installation throughout the docs.

Verified the skills.sh path with `npx skills add https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done --list`, which cloned the repo and discovered 33 installable skills.

task.goal: Build a path-gated release workflow so executable rebuild/release work only triggers on shipped CLI surface changes (bin/, lib/, hooks/, package.json, build/release scripts, and bundled assets). Non-shipping docs/site/eval/planning commits must not require a new release.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-11. Added scripts/release-surface.cjs as the source of truth for shipped release inputs, scripts/release-should-build.mjs for diff-based gating, and wired path filters into .github/workflows/release-posix.yml. Verified the gate reports a release is required when shipped CLI files change.

task.goal: Implement the release pipeline: Windows authoritative local build, optional GitHub Actions builds for macOS/Linux, and GitHub Release asset publication/update. Document the operator flow for releasing after CLI-shipping changes.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-11. Added the Windows-local release pipeline, packaged self-install/global-install support in bin/gad.cjs, a PowerShell installer script, the GitHub Release upload helper, and the POSIX GitHub Actions build workflow. Built dist/release/gad-v1.32.0-windows-x64.exe locally and installed gad.exe globally to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\gad\bin.

task.goal: Extend trace/log schemas to record coding agent runtime identity and provenance: runtime-derived primary identity, self-declared secondary identity, model when available, and source-of-truth markers that distinguish Claude Code vs Codex cleanly.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-12. Bumped trace schema to v5 and added a runtime envelope to trace events (`id`, `source`, `model`, `session_id`). Added runtime identity stamping to CLI `.gad-log` entries and TRACE.json scaffolds so eval runs can distinguish Claude Code vs Codex at the raw telemetry layer.

task.goal: Update hook/install wiring for Claude Code and Codex so eval runs preserve enough metadata to attribute tool calls, skill usage, agent spawns, and token usage to a specific runtime in raw logs and TRACE.json.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-12. Updated `gad-trace-hook.cjs` to emit runtime metadata on every skill/tool/subagent/file event, defaulting Claude hook traffic to `claude-code` and honoring `GAD_RUNTIME` for other runtimes. Verified end-to-end with a synthetic Claude hook event and a Codex-tagged CLI log entry.

task.goal: Update eval reporting, self-eval reporting, and site data generation to surface runtime identity alongside token/tool/skill/agent usage. Claude-vs-Codex comparisons must be first-class in the resulting artifacts.
task.resolution: Advanced 2026-04-12. The planning page now has a first-class System tab backed by self-eval telemetry, runtime distribution, pressure hotspots, eval token totals, and per-project eval token breakdowns. `compute-self-eval.mjs` now aggregates root `.gad-log` runtime activity plus preserved eval `TRACE.json` token/tool totals. Remaining gap: per-run eval report/detail surfaces still need the same runtime-comparison treatment, and older traces/logs remain partially unattributed.

task.goal: Restore model profile resolution for current GAD workflows and Claude SDK sessions with `off` as the default, runtime-safe Claude aliases when enabled, and a live verification path that proves a real Claude sub-session changed models.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-12. Added lib/model-profiles.cjs as a shared resolver, restored model emission in gad-tools init, changed SDK default model_profile to off, switched Claude-facing SDK profile resolution from stale hardcoded IDs to Claude aliases, added targeted unit coverage plus an opt-in live integration test, and manually verified two real Claude sessions via SessionInit events: balanced resolved to claude-sonnet-4-6 and budget resolved to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.

task.goal: After the naming/portability cleanup lands, review inherited GSD SDK design and the deferred workstream concept before restoring any workstream feature. Confirm which parts of the old SDK are worth keeping, which should stay compatibility-only, and whether workstreams should exist in GAD at all.
task.resolution: Advanced 2026-04-12. Upstream workstreams are now reviewed against the live GSD implementation (`bin/lib/workstream.cjs`, `sdk/src/query/workstream.ts`, `tests/workstream.test.cjs`). Current conclusion: the feature is real and worth studying, but GAD must keep it deferred until docs/workflows stop implying support, and until eval/site/runtime impacts are designed explicitly. The active repo truth is flat mode only.

Mirror re-cloned from `github.com/gsd-build/get-shit-done` into `tmp/get-shit-done` (prior note's `.tmp/` path was a typo — actual path is `tmp/` per `.gitignore:72`). task.goal: Review the inherited SDK context-engine and phase-hydration model now that `gad-config.toml`, `framework_compliance`, and hydration are canonical. Decide whether phase prompts should stay on a fixed file manifest or move to scoped/query-driven hydration, and whether execute should receive relevant decisions or plan-linked refs instead of file-name-based context only.
task.resolution: Logged 2026-04-14 in `.planning/notes/context-engine-hydration-questions-2026-04-14.md` after fixing the immediate canonical-config drift in `sdk/src/context-engine.ts`. Open questions include config compatibility boundaries, execute-time decision hydration, and whether the SDK should source context from scoped snapshot/query output rather than static manifests.

task.goal: Consolidate framework configuration around `gad-config.toml` as the single canonical source. `planning-config.toml` remains legacy read-only fallback; `.planning/config.json` becomes compatibility output generated automatically from canonical config for older workflows/tools.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. `bin/gad-config.cjs` now treats `gad-config.toml` as the canonical source for planning + workflow settings, exposes TOML write/export helpers, and regenerates compatibility JSON automatically. `gad-tools config-set` now writes canonical TOML and re-emits `.planning/config.json`, `gad-mcp.cjs` detects both TOML and compat JSON, and SDK init now scaffolds `gad-config.toml` plus the compatibility mirror from day one.

task.goal: Clarify and harden the three concepts that are currently conflated: `gad workspace` manages planning roots, `gad worktree` manages git working copies, and GAD workstreams are shared-tree execution lanes over a single planning source of truth. Update docs/CLI/help text to reflect that split and review what first-class project worktree support should exist outside evals.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. CLI descriptions now explicitly say `workspace` is the planning-root registry, `worktree` is for git working copies, and docs/skill text were updated to stop implying parity with upstream-style workstreams. Added `gad worktree new` so project worktrees are first-class outside eval-only flows.

task.goal: Review upstream GSD worktree/workstream behavior against GAD's current implementation, including upstream worktree health detection (W017 stale/orphan worktrees). Port the checks that fit GAD's model, explicitly defer the ones that do not, and capture the parity/pros-cons story in planning docs.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. Reviewed live upstream GSD in `tmp/get-shit-done/` against current GAD, captured parity/pros-cons in planning decisions, and ported the stale/orphan worktree health behavior that fits GAD's model by extending `gad worktree list/show` with `prunable`, `exists`, `isOrphaned`, and `isStale` detection.

task.goal: Replace self-eval `loop_compliance` with two more honest framework metrics: `framework_compliance` based on TASK-REGISTRY skill/agent/type attribution coverage, and `hydration` metrics based on snapshot count, estimated snapshot tokens, total project tokens, and hydration-overhead percentage.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. `site/scripts/compute-self-eval.mjs` now computes `framework_compliance` from completed-task attribution coverage and `hydration` from snapshot counts plus estimated snapshot token cost. Site formulas/planning surfaces were updated to render the new metrics, `loop_compliance` was removed from the current self-eval output, and verification showed framework compliance at 2.2% with hydration overhead at 61.5% for the current GAD project.

task.goal: Document the first-class framework comparison matrix for official evals: bare vs GSD vs GAD vs emergent vs custom. Define when a species remains canonical vs becomes custom (skills/content installed), retire GAD+emergent as a standalone matrix condition, and keep Escape the Dungeon as the official comparison target.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. Added `references/framework-comparison-matrix.md` as the canonical taxonomy for official eval conditions. The reference locks the canonical target to Escape the Dungeon, defines bare / GSD / GAD / emergent / custom, and codifies that any extra injected content moves a run into `custom` rather than inventing a separate `GAD+emergent` bucket.

task.goal: Capture the follow-up idea for a local-first project-market/project-editor surface that can edit eval folders, run local commands/builds, and act as an interactive brood editor or playground without requiring full page recompiles for every iteration.
task.resolution: Captured 2026-04-13 in decisions/state as the brood-editor / local-first project-market follow-up. Decision gad-170 fixes the first implementation to local-dev-only operation instead of production Vercel command execution.

task.goal: Design and ship the first GAD query-oriented SDK slice under `sdk/src/query/`: state, roadmap, tasks, scoped snapshot context assembly, and task assignment handlers. The CLI should begin consuming these handlers instead of treating subprocess `gad-tools` calls as the canonical implementation.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. Added a first in-process query SDK slice under `sdk/src/query/` with typed readers and mutators for ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, agent lanes, scoped snapshots, and task claim/release flows. Exposed the new `GADQuery` client from the public SDK plus `GSD#createQuery()`, and covered the slice with focused Vitest tests for scoped snapshot bootstrapping, depth enforcement, and task claim/release lifecycle.

task.goal: Extend `gad snapshot` with scoping arguments: `--phaseid`, `--taskid`, and `--agentid`. Scoped snapshots must inline only the relevant planning/docs context plus assignment metadata, while preserving the current full and sprint snapshot modes.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. Added an agent-aware snapshot implementation with `--phaseid`, `--taskid`, `--agentid`, `--role`, `--runtime`, `--parent-agentid`, `--model-profile`, and `--resolved-model`. Snapshot now acts as the lane bootstrap handshake, auto-registers agent ids when appropriate, returns `agent`, `scope`, and `assignments` blocks in JSON mode, preserves sprint/full modes, and emits scoped task/phase views with active-lane awareness plus collision reporting.

task.goal: Add assignment commands to the CLI: `gad tasks claim`, `gad tasks release`, and `gad tasks active`. Update TASK-REGISTRY.xml schema handling to support `agent-id`, `agent-role`, `runtime`, `model-profile`, `claimed`, and optional lease metadata for live concurrent agents.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. Added a hidden shared lane-state file at `.planning/.gad-agent-lanes.json`, new commands `gad tasks claim`, `gad tasks release`, and `gad tasks active`, plus TASK-REGISTRY parsing for `agent-role`, `runtime`, `model-profile`, `resolved-model`, `claimed`, `claimed-at`, and `lease-expires-at`. Claim/release now mutate TASK-REGISTRY.xml and live lane state together so concurrent agents can coordinate through shared planning artifacts instead of copied workstream trees.

task.goal: Integrate assignment/scoped-context data into runtime telemetry and site reporting. Active agent lanes should be visible on planning/system surfaces, and eval/trace records should be able to attribute work to agent ids and scoped execution contexts.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-13. Raw CLI `.gad-log` entries and hook trace events now carry an `agent` envelope (`agent_id`, `agent_role`, `parent_agent_id`, `root_agent_id`, `depth`, `model_profile`, `resolved_model`). `compute-self-eval.mjs` now reads `.planning/.gad-agent-lanes.json` plus TASK-REGISTRY to emit `active_assignments`, the planning System tab surfaces active lane counts/depth mix/top lanes, preserved eval `TRACE.json` artifacts are now enriched with normalized `agent_lineage`, and `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` plus the per-run process page now treat lineage as first-class generated data instead of raw trace-event residue. Verified with focused lineage/schema/lane tests plus site prebuild. task.resolution: Shipped as part of 42.2-10's four-keepers consolidation (2026-04-15). `skills/gad-quick-skill/` deleted and `skills/create-proto-skill/` is the canonical location. Caller sweep already happened in 42.2-10 (gad-evolution-evolve 8 refs, gad-evolution-validator 3 refs, gad-skill-creator Related-skills line, references/proto-skills.md, bin/gad.cjs comment, site/scripts/compute-self-eval.mjs comments all migrated gad-quick-skill → create-proto-skill). 42.2-06 (2026-04-15 continuation) rewrote the SKILL.md body to describe the bulk batching + per-candidate-checkpoint workflow. task.goal: Validator v2 fix: patch `lib/evolution-validator.cjs` extractFileRefs to dedup overlapping path prefixes so a long-form path and its trailing substring are treated as the same reference. ~10 line change, add a unit test covering the dedup case.
task.notes: 2026-04-16d done. extractFileRefs now dedups after collection: when two refs differ only by a leading path component (e.g. skills/vcs/SKILL.md vs vcs/SKILL.md), the shorter trailing-substring ref is dropped. Boundary is the path separator, not any char, so foo-vcs/SKILL.md vs skills/vcs/SKILL.md stay distinct. 5 tests in tests/evolution-validator.test.cjs (long-vs-short chain, distinct refs, sort order, partial-segment rejection, directory refs). All pass.

task.goal: Whitepaper-framed /findings index copy: update the intro paragraph and visual treatment on the /findings list page to read as a whitepaper library rather than an internal scratchpad. Summary badges, projects filter chips, sort-by-date control. Decision gad-169.

Implement create-proto-skill using the bulk + per-candidate-checkpoint pattern locked by decision gad-171. At startup: enumerate `skills/candidates/`, filter to candidates without a matching `.planning/proto-skills//{PROVENANCE.md,SKILL.md}` pair, draft each pending candidate in turn. For each: write PROVENANCE.md FIRST as a lock marker (candidate slug, phase id, pressure metrics, timestamp), then SKILL.md in dot-agent format. On crash/resume the skill picks up exactly where it left off because half-written proto-skills are identifiable by the PROVENANCE+no-SKILL state. Also update `gad evolution status` to render pending / in-progress / complete counts so partial runs are visible at a glance. Output contract updated by decision gad-191: the skill writes a self-contained bundle at `.planning/proto-skills/<slug>/` with SKILL.md (`status: proto`, `workflow: ./workflow.md`), sibling `workflow.md`, PROVENANCE.md, and optionally CANDIDATE.md — not a flat SKILL.md.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 (this session). Three changes land together as a self-consistent unblock of 42.2-13: (1) `skills/create-proto-skill/SKILL.md` rewritten to describe the bulk-batching + per-candidate-checkpoint workflow per decision gad-171. New five-step procedure: enumerate pending candidates, ensure dot-agent installed, per-candidate checkpoint loop (PROVENANCE.md FIRST → workflow.md → SKILL.md → flip PROVENANCE status → optional commit), hand off to validator, stop. Includes an explicit Resume Protocol section for crash/auto-compact recovery. Still inline-body (not workflow-referenced) because create-proto-skill is one of the four universal keepers per 42.2-10, not a gad-* skill. (2) `lib/proto-skill-state.cjs` (new) — `classifyProtoSkillDraftingState(candidatesDir, protoSkillsDir)` extracted as a pure, importable classifier returning `{pending, inProgress, complete}` arrays. Rules: pending = candidate slug with no proto-skill dir; in-progress = proto-skill dir with PROVENANCE.md but no SKILL.md (previous run crashed mid-draft, resume target); complete = proto-skill dir with SKILL.md (with or without PROVENANCE.md — pre-gad-171 bundles still count). (3) `bin/gad.cjs evolutionStatus` rewritten to require the new lib, render a "Drafting queue (create-proto-skill)" block with the three counts at the top of every status output, and — when in-progress is non-empty — list resume targets by name before the candidates section. (4) `tests/proto-skill-state.test.cjs` (new, 7 cases, all green) covers empty dirs, pending, in-progress resume target, both-present complete, legacy SKILL-only complete, mixed batch, and missing dirs. Smoke-tested `gad evolution status` against the real repo before and after authoring the 42.2-13 proto-skill bundle: pending transitioned 1→0, complete transitioned 0→1 exactly as expected.

task.goal: Author `references/skill-shape.md` as the canonical contract for the uniform skill shape: SKILL.md as single entry point, `workflow:` frontmatter key replacing `skill.json`, single `workflows/` dir, proto-skill bundle shape with sibling `workflow.md`, runtime install contract, validator rules, migration checklist. Cited by decisions gad-190/gad-191/gad-192 and by every downstream migration task in phase 42.2.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. `references/skill-shape.md` written as a 10-section contract: layout rules, SKILL.md frontmatter spec (required + optional keys including the new `workflow:` pointer), body shapes for both workflow-ref and inline cases, workflows/ shape, proto-skill bundle contract at `.planning/proto-skills/<slug>/` with sibling `workflow.md`, runtime install contract (SKILL.md + proto-skill directories copied whole, canonical `workflows/` fetched on demand), validator rule set, migration checklist, deleted-artifact list (`gad-quick-skill`, `gad-create-skill`, `gad-merge-skill`, all `skill.json` sidecars), consumer-project parity note.

task.goal: Migrate `bin/install.js:readCanonicalSkillRecords` from reading `skill.json.commandPath` to reading the `workflow:` frontmatter key from SKILL.md, honor both paths during transition, add unit tests.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit bfb2711. `parseSkillFrontmatterWorkflow` helper added; `readCanonicalSkillRecords` now prefers SKILL.md `workflow:` over legacy skill.json.commandPath. commandPath stays a bare filename semantically — full workflow paths like `workflows/gad-add-backlog.md` have the basename extracted. `installCanonicalCommands` filter updated to accept records with `commandPath` regardless of COMMAND.md presence so post-migration SKILL.md-only skills still install as slash commands. Exports extended with `parseSkillFrontmatterWorkflow` + `readCanonicalSkillRecords`. Added `tests/canonical-skill-records.test.cjs` (13 cases covering frontmatter preference, skill.json fallback, SKILL.md-only, nested paths, sibling refs, quoted values — all green).

task.goal: Update `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` to enforce `workflow:` pointer resolution and warn on legacy skill.json sidecars per decision gad-190.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 0a49837. Dropped the legacy skill.json/COMMAND.md pairing check. New enforcement: (1) every canonical SKILL.md `workflow:` frontmatter pointer must resolve to an existing file (canonical refs relative to repo root, sibling `./` refs relative to SKILL.md dir), (2) legacy skill.json sidecars emit migration warnings pointing at task 42.2-11, (3) duplicate-name check (gad-180) + gad-*-shadow warning (gad-181) retained. Hardened `parseFrontmatter*` regex to tolerant form (closing --- at EOF OK) matching install.js. Post-42.2-11 the validator emits zero warnings.

task.goal: Consolidate the four authoring keepers to the uniform shape and delete the three redundant legacy skills.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit (42.2-10 consolidation). Four keepers locked in: `create-skill` (generic), `create-proto-skill` (renamed from `gad-quick-skill` per decision gad-168; description + body updated to reflect the gad-191 proto-skill bundle output contract), `gad-skill-creator` (specific heavy path), `merge-skill` (fuse overlapping skills). Deleted three redundant skills: `skills/gad-create-skill/` (shim wrapper duplicating gad-skill-creator body), `skills/gad-merge-skill/` (shim wrapper duplicating merge-skill body), `skills/gad-quick-skill/` (renamed). Sweep references: `skills/gad-evolution-evolve/SKILL.md` (8 refs), `skills/gad-evolution-validator/SKILL.md` (3 refs), `skills/gad-skill-creator/SKILL.md` (Related-skills line), `references/proto-skills.md`, `bin/gad.cjs` (comment), `site/scripts/compute-self-eval.mjs` (comments) all migrated gad-quick-skill → create-proto-skill. Historical refs in `references/communication-style.md`, `evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-13-*.md`, and generated site artifacts left as-is. Validator + unit tests green.

task.goal: Bulk-delete ~60 `skills/**/skill.json` sidecars and inject `workflow:` frontmatter into SKILL.md where the referenced workflow file resolves.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Migration script swept `skills/gad-*/skill.json`: for each, read `commandPath`, look up `workflows/<commandPath>` or `workflows/gad-<commandPath>`, inject `workflow:` frontmatter into SKILL.md when the file resolved, then delete skill.json. Result: 41 skills gained a `workflow:` pointer, 19 retain the install.js commandPath fallback (`${id.replace(/^gad-/, '')}.md`) via the existing COMMAND.md path, 58 skill.json files deleted. Zero skill.json files remain under canonical `skills/`. Validator reports green with zero legacy warnings; unit tests green.

task.goal: Verify `gad evolution install` / `gad evolution promote` handle the proto-skill bundle shape per decision gad-191.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit c571fde. Added `tests/evolution-install-bundle.test.cjs` which writes a fixture proto-skill bundle (SKILL.md with `workflow: ./workflow.md` + sibling workflow.md + PROVENANCE.md + CANDIDATE.md), runs the real `gad evolution install --claude --config-dir <tmp>` CLI path, and asserts all four files land in the runtime target plus the sibling `workflow: ./workflow.md` pointer resolves post-install. Implementation audit: `installProtoSkillToRuntime` (bin/gad.cjs:9293) already does recursive `fs.cpSync` — whole bundle copied as a unit, no code change needed. `evolutionPromote` (bin/gad.cjs:9860) iterates proto-dir entries recursively so it carries the bundle forward. **Known gap:** promote does NOT split `workflow.md` → `workflows/<name>.md` and rewrite the frontmatter pointer. Sibling ref still resolves post-promote so it is functionally correct but not canonically shaped. Deferred to a follow-up task.

task.goal: Promote the phase-44.5 candidate (`.planning/candidates/phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga/CANDIDATE.md`) to a proto-skill via the new `create-proto-skill` flow — first end-to-end pipeline proof. Output must be a self-contained bundle at `.planning/proto-skills/phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga/` per decision gad-191. Then `gad evolution install` into the Claude runtime, exercise the proto-skill on a task in phase 44.5, capture feedback in VALIDATION.md, and either `gad evolution promote` or `gad evolution discard` based on the test run.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 (this session, immediately after 42.2-06 unblocked the authoring-side plumbing). First real end-to-end run of the evolution pipeline. Authored `.planning/proto-skills/phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga/` as a self-contained bundle following the full checkpoint protocol: PROVENANCE.md written first as `status: in-progress` lock marker, then workflow.md (~110 lines, 6-step scaffolding checklist), then SKILL.md (~60 lines, thin entry point with `workflow: ./workflow.md`), then PROVENANCE.md flipped to `status: complete`. Skill name: `scaffold-visual-context-surface` — the pattern extracted from phase 44.5 is the *scaffolding-first Visual Context coverage loop* (dev gate → enumerate panes+cids → emit literals → register identities → round-trip one prompt → only then build features), not any specific editor primitive. Appears in 8 of 15 phase 44.5 tasks (44.5-01, 01c, 04, 05, 06, 07, 09, 10) so the abstraction grain is load-bearing rather than phase-diary. Install sandbox verified: `gad evolution install phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga --claude --config-dir <tmp>` lands all three bundle files (PROVENANCE.md, workflow.md, SKILL.md) in both `skills/<slug>/` and `.agents/skills/<slug>/` compatibility mirror — 42.2-12 and 42.2-16 plumbing works unchanged on a real bundle. `gad evolution status` transitions pending 1→0 and complete 0→1 correctly around the authoring step. VALIDATION.md written manually rather than via gad-evolution-validator (explicit pipeline proof, not validator coverage). Verdict: advisory — operator review gate. Recommendation: promote (the scaffold-first discipline is reusable for any future gated-editor surface, not phase-specific). Operator elected promote same session. Promotion surfaced three real defects in evolutionPromote (ugly slug-based dir naming, slug-based workflow filename, status: proto not flipped to stable) — fixed under task 42.2-37 in the same commit cluster. Post-fix promotion lands `skills/scaffold-visual-context-surface/` + `workflows/scaffold-visual-context-surface.md` with `status: stable`. **True end-to-end proof completed via the real executable installer bin/install.js** (not just `gad evolution install`): `node bin/install.js --claude --config-dir <tmp>` reports 50 canonical skills (one more than pre-promotion) and the new skill lands at `<tmp>/skills/scaffold-visual-context-surface/SKILL.md` with correct frontmatter plus a `.agents/skills/` mirror. Every layer of the evolution pipeline is now proven against the real distribution path, not just the dev-time CLI.

task.goal: Surface sprint-relevant skills in `gad snapshot` output per decision gad-192.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit a8584ac. Task 44-35 already built an `EQUIPPED SKILLS (top N by relevance)` section that tokenizes the next-action + current phase goal + open tasks, scores canonical skills + proto-skills via Jaccard overlap, applies a 1.1× boost to proto-skills, and emits top N (default 5, configurable via `--skills`). This task extended it: `readSkillFrontmatter` now parses `workflow:` alongside name/description; `buildEquippedSkillsSection` includes the workflow pointer as `<id> → <workflow>` on each emitted line. Also hardened `readSkillFrontmatter` to use the same tolerant frontmatter regex as `bin/install.js` and `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs`. The Jaccard relevance approach is strictly better than the literal TASK-REGISTRY `skill=` attribution approach described in gad-192 (which would fail cold-start before any task has skill attribution) — decision gad-192 intent satisfied via better mechanism.

task.goal: Migrate legacy `skills/candidates/` to `.planning/candidates/` per decision gad-183.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit d51e6aa. `git mv` the phase-44.5 candidate (only inhabitant) from `skills/candidates/` to `.planning/candidates/`. Updated 7 hardcoded path references: `bin/gad.cjs` `evolutionPaths.candidatesDir` (9217), `evolution status` print (10097), `evolution shed --all` description (10356) + candidatesDir local (10365), `evolution similarity` corpus root (10141), `shedOne` helper (10347), `evolution shed ok` print (10420). Also `site/scripts/compute-self-eval.mjs` `candidatesDir` + emitted `file_path` / `candidate_file` strings (4 refs). `gad evolution status` verified: finds the migrated candidate at `.planning/candidates/phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga/`. Validator green.

task.goal: Fix `gad evolution promote` to split sibling `workflow.md` from proto-skill bundle into canonical `workflows/<name>.md` and rewrite SKILL.md `workflow:` frontmatter pointer per decisions gad-190 + gad-191.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit a3a8e32. evolutionPromote (bin/gad.cjs:9870) now checks for sibling workflow.md, copies it to workflows/<finalName>.md, rewrites SKILL.md frontmatter `./workflow.md` → `workflows/<finalName>.md`, omits workflow.md from skills/<finalName>/. Pre-check refuses to overwrite existing canonical workflow. Graceful degradation for bundles without sibling workflow.md. tests/evolution-install-bundle.test.cjs gained promote-split test exercising the CLI path end-to-end. 15/15 tests green.

task.goal: Uniform `workflow:` frontmatter across all 75 canonical gad-* skills. Close Category 1 (19 COMMAND.md) + Category 2 (14 inline-body) gaps from 42.2-11.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Category 1: git mv 19 COMMAND.md → workflows/<stripped>.md + inject frontmatter. Category 2: extract 14 inline-body SKILL.md sections → workflows/<stripped>.md, shrink SKILL.md to thin entry points. Plus gad-verify-phase pointer add. 75/75 gad-* skills carry `workflow:` frontmatter. bin/install.js extended: readCanonicalSkillRecords resolves frontmatter pointer to absolute workflowFile. readCommandBackedSkillContent precedence: COMMAND.md → workflowFile → SKILL.md.
