Validator green.

task.goal: Document the canonical standard flow for creating new command/workflow skills in references/skill-shape.md.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit f9b9f19. Added §10 "Standard flow: creating a new command/workflow skill" — six ordered steps (identity → workflow → thin SKILL.md → verification → attribution → optional eval). Added §11 "Skill lifecycle — candidate → proto-skill → canonical" with CLI verbs and arrow diagram. Renumbered consumer-project parity from §10 to §12.

task.goal: Shrink 18 Category 1 SKILL.md duplicates to thin pointer shape after discovering their bodies matched workflows/<stripped>.md byte-for-byte post-42.2-17.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 07bfc83. Body-equality audit found 18 of 19 Category 1 SKILL.md files had identical content to their new workflows/<stripped>.md counterpart. Script rewrote those 18 to the canonical thin pointer shape. gad-eval-spawn intentionally skipped — its SKILL.md and workflows/eval-spawn.md genuinely differ.

task.goal: Add `gad skill show <id>` + `gad skill list --paths` to close the P0 discoverability gap surfaced by the subagent test battery in task 42.2-21. Also update stale references.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit ce53f94. New `gad skill show <id> [--body]` prints name, description, resolved SKILL.md path, resolved workflow path; with --body also prints contents. Accepts id, public name, or gad-stripped form. New `--paths` flag on `gad skill list` prints absolute SKILL.md + workflow paths for every skill, flagging MISSING inline. references/skill-shape.md §1a added to clarify workflows/ vs .planning/workflows/ duality. references/proto-skills.md stale skills/candidates/ refs fixed to .planning/candidates/.

task.goal: Run subagent discovery test battery: 5 parallel cold agents, tool access restricted to gad CLI + Read-for-CLI-surfaced-paths, measure how findable the skill catalog is.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 85d4282. Five cold subagents (A: plan phase, B: evolution cycle, C: debug, D: fresh session + create skill, E: candidate → canonical). Mean confidence 7.3/10. Agent A scored 5/10 (could not reach skills/gad-plan-phase/SKILL.md under strict CLI-only rules). Four P0/P1 gaps identified; P0 fixes shipped in 42.2-20. Full findings at .planning/notes/subagent-discovery-findings-2026-04-15.md including read-chain flow map + testing cadence proposal. Adjacent: .planning/notes/workflow-flow-control-dsl-exploration-2026-04-15.md recommends Position B (lightweight structured Markdown tags: <workflow>, <step>, <branch>, <loop>, <parallel>, <output>) for workflow flow control DSL, proposes a one-workflow retrofit experiment.

task.goal: Add `gad startup` command — one-shot session-start contract, closes the chicken-and-egg gap Agent D1 surfaced (no skill governed session start, gad snapshot was only discoverable via CLAUDE.md or from inside its own output).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 585237a. New `gad startup [--projectid]` prints the session-start contract, snapshot content summary, three-step "after snapshot" flow, cross-session continuity notes, and rehydration cost caveat. Optional --projectid passthrough runs snapshot after the contract print. Enumerates available project ids via gad-config.

task.goal: Add authoring-skill disambiguation footer to `gad skill list` output per Agent D2 finding — four overlapping authoring skills with no CLI-surfaced ordering guidance.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 585237a. `gad skill list` now always emits a footer with: (a) which authoring skill to fire (create-skill / create-proto-skill / gad-skill-creator / merge-skill), (b) the candidate → proto-skill → canonical lifecycle with the CLI verb at each transition, (c) the three discovery helpers (skill show, skill show --body, skill list --paths). Agents reading `gad skill list` now see the full lifecycle without needing to read references/skill-shape.md §11 separately.

task.goal: Turn the subagent discovery test battery into a reusable skill + workflow + CLI helper so it can be rerun as a regression test after changes to the skill catalog or CLI discovery surface.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 591a9c7. skills/gad-discovery-test/SKILL.md (thin entry) + workflows/discovery-test.md (seven-step Position B tagged workflow: gate → task-inventory → spawn-agents → aggregate → write-findings → write-site-data → commit) + `gad discovery-test` CLI helper (prints battery instructions, points at the skill, direct CLI invocation not possible since the battery spawns Agent subagents). Target mean confidence 8.5/10. Rerun after any catalog change.

task.goal: Implement the Position B workflow flow-control DSL v0 per `.planning/notes/workflow-flow-control-dsl-exploration-2026-04-15.md`: seven structural tags (workflow, step, branch, else, loop, parallel, output) layered on top of existing free-form Markdown as advisory annotations.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit dcb7512. lib/workflow-tag-parser.cjs — regex-based parser, tolerant of whitespace, respects fenced code blocks, handles quoted attribute values containing '>'. Two APIs: parse(source) returns a tree, flatten(tree) returns {steps, edges}. tests/workflow-tag-parser.test.cjs — 10 cases covering tag recognition, attributes, fenced-block exclusion, linear sequence, branch+condition, loop+for, parallel, skill attribute, anonymous auto-id. All green. Retrofit experiment: workflows/evolution-evolve.md prepended with a tagged structural skeleton (16 nodes: gate → branch pending → else → 6 mainline steps with 2 nested loops → register-review-task). Existing 352-line prose body preserved verbatim below the skeleton. Parser extracts the expected graph correctly.

task.goal: Design an A/B experiment to test whether repeated snapshot rehydration after auto-compact is actually reinforcing agent behavior or wasting tokens (operator concern 2026-04-15).
task.resolution: Design shipped 2026-04-15 commit 2084d21 but experiment CANCELLED 2026-04-15 per operator confirmation: "I confirmed that when the context is already loaded, any of the same context is useless and wasteful. so it needs to be clear when to get static vs active info." Answer known without the run — H2 (rehydration is wasteful) confirmed. Design doc at `.planning/notes/rehydration-cost-experiment-2026-04-15.md` marked SUPERSEDED at the top and retained as history. The insight is shipped as task 42.2-29 (`gad snapshot --mode=active`) + decision gad-195.

task.goal: Populate `site/data/discovery-findings.json` from the 2026-04-15 battery run + seed the cli-captures buffer for the planning site discovery tab.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 0aa449d (part of 27/28 bundle). discovery-findings.json — structured schema matching the gad:discovery-test workflow step 6: mean/min/max confidence, agent array with cli_commands + skills_discovered + skill_order + gaps, flow_map with nodes (depth-graded) + edges (workflow-pointer / reference / prints / dead-end), aggregate_cli_frequency, unreached_paths list, P0/P1 gap tracker with fix status. cli-captures.json seeded empty — populated at runtime by the /api/dev/capture-cli route.

task.goal: Add /planning Discovery tab rendering (a) live CLI terminal capture with rerun buttons, (b) read-chain flow map from discovery-findings.json, (c) findings summary with P0/P1 gap status.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 0aa449d. site/app/api/dev/capture-cli/route.ts — dev-only API spawning `node bin/gad.cjs <cmd>` with NO_COLOR=1, strips ANSI codes, appends to site/data/cli-captures.json (5 per cmd, 20 total cap). Allowed commands: startup, snapshot, skill-list, skill-show, discovery-test. GET returns the current captures without triggering a new run. site/app/planning/PlanningDiscoveryTab.tsx — three-panel client component: TerminalPanel (buttons run the API, render latest capture per command as monospace block), FlowMapPanel (nodes grouped by depth, outbound edges rendered inline, unreached nodes highlighted red, entry points blue), FindingsSummaryPanel (confidence badges, regression flag, per-agent table, P0/P1 gap lists with fix status, unreached paths). Wired into PlanningTabbedContent as the "Discovery" tab. Type check clean.

task.goal: Ship the session-scoped static/active snapshot split per decision gad-195. `gad snapshot --mode=active` emits only STATE.xml + ROADMAP (sprint) + TASKS, skipping the static catalog/references/decisions that a mid-session agent already has in working memory.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. `bin/gad.cjs snapshotV2Cmd` gained `--mode={full|active}` flag. Active mode skips buildDecisionsSection, buildFileRefsSection, buildConventionsSection, buildEquippedSkillsSection, and DOCS-MAP emission. Measured: active mode ≈ 3900 tokens vs full mode ≈ 7500 tokens (~50% reduction). Decision gad-195 codifies the contract: startup = one-shot full, mid-session = active-only re-pulls, never auto-rehydrate after compact. Rehydration experiment (42.2-26) cancelled — operator confirmed H2 directly. Subagent discovery battery is unaffected (cold agents = fresh-session scenario, always full).

task.goal: Add `gad skill find <keyword>` to eliminate the "guess the slug" problem cold agents hit when EQUIPPED SKILLS top-5 doesn't surface a task-relevant skill. Also fix the stale `skills/candidates/` narrative Agent B caught in the rerun.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit ffd3aed. `gad skill find <query>` — substring match (+10) + token overlap (+2 exact, +1 partial), ranked across canonical + proto skills, prints name + description + workflow pointer + MISSING flag. Verified: `gad skill find debug` returns gad-debug + find-sprites; `gad skill find "plan phase"` returns 10 relevant matches. Hooked into skillCmd alongside list/show/promote. Also fixed `workflows/evolution-evolve.md` narrative: 3 lines still said `skills/candidates/` while the outputs block and CLI both said `.planning/candidates/` — sed'd to consistent `.planning/candidates/`. CLAUDE.md updated to prefer `gad startup` over direct `gad snapshot` and to document the static/active split per decision gad-195.

task.goal: Rerun the subagent discovery test battery after P0/P1 fixes (42.2-20/22/23/30) to measure confidence delta.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 3f2b3bd. Five cold subagents rerun with identical task set. Mean confidence 7.3 → 7.9 (+0.6). Agent A: 5 → 6 (+1, used `gad skill show` for the first time). Agent D: 8 → 9.5 (+1.5, combined d1=10, d2=9). Agent E: 8 → 9 (+1). Agents B and C held (remaining gaps are conceptual or shipped in 42.2-30 after the rerun). Target 8.5 not reached — remaining 0.6 gap is (a) Agent C needs `gad skill find` test, (b) EQUIPPED SKILLS top-5 still misses task-relevant skills, (c) Agent A didn't follow transitive `@references/` pointers. Agent E incident: actually executed the full candidate-to-canonical pipeline including promote, operator reverted, workflows/discovery-test.md agent prompt template hardened to forbid write-side CLI verbs. Full writeup at `.planning/notes/subagent-discovery-findings-2026-04-15-rerun.md`. Site data updated: `site/data/discovery-findings.json` with delta, previous_run linkage, p0 all fixed, p1 3/4 fixed.

task.goal: Ship `gad skill promote-folder` — generalized promote that accepts any skill-shaped folder source (not just `.planning/proto-skills/<slug>/`) and installs it into the canonical get-anything-done framework tree. Also canonicalize the VCS skill.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 6498414 with an initial scope bug (`--project <id>` flag suggesting cross-project install), fixed 2026-04-15 commit 2326bc6 (task 42.2-34) after operator correction: promotion is framework-only. `gad skill promote-folder <source> [--name] [--dry-run] [--force]` — validates SKILL.md frontmatter, parses workflow: pointer, performs sibling-workflow split per decision gad-191, deposits into canonical skills/<name>/ + workflows/<name>.md, sentinel-gated to the canonical get-anything-done repo. Pre-checks collisions, refuses without --force. Smoke tested end-to-end with a /tmp fixture. VCS canonicalization: `skills/gad-visual-context-system/SKILL.md` status flipped from proto → stable with canonicalized date + rationale ("framework staple — every UI-based consumer project starts with this methodology"). Since VCS already lives at its canonical destination, the promotion was a metadata flip rather than a promote-folder run; promote-folder is for future skills drafted outside .planning/proto-skills/.

task.goal: Ship gad:proto-skill-battery — per-proto discoverability + supersession test, wired as automatic step 9 of workflows/evolution-evolve.md per operator direction.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 03497e0; objective block corrected 2026-04-15 commit 2326bc6 (task 42.2-34). Skills/gad-proto-skill-battery/SKILL.md (thin entry point) + workflows/proto-skill-battery.md (8-step Position B tagged workflow). Runs PER PROJECT, scoped to the project you're in (proto-skills auto-register in the project tree, no cross-project install). Per-proto measurement: findability 0-10, execution_sufficiency 0-10, shed_score 0.0-1.0 from proto-vs-proto supersession ranking within the same project. NOT proto-vs-canonical — canonical is framework-scope, consumer projects have their own ecosystems. Shed thresholds: 0.5 flags for review, 0.75 recommends eviction. Battery never mutates canonical state. Evolution-evolve workflow step 9 auto-runs the battery. Framework note: get-anything-done itself is both a consumer project and the framework — when the battery runs in this repo it tests get-anything-done's OWN proto-skills, not framework canonical skills (those are evaluated by gad:discovery-test).

task.goal: Fix `gad skill promote-folder` scope: remove the `--project <id>` flag — promotion is framework-only, consumer projects don't promote.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 2326bc6. Operator correction: "promote-folder doesn't make sense. promotion is only for get-anything-done framework and not for anyone else. proto-skills automatically register with the consumer project. our get-anything-done project is a consumer project and the framework. you are getting confused." Removed --project flag and its branching. promote-folder now always targets canonical get-anything-done skills/ + workflows/ and refuses unless isCanonicalGadRepo. Updated meta description, help text, error messages, and the skillCmd parent description. Also corrected workflows/proto-skill-battery.md objective block to emphasize "runs PER PROJECT", "proto-skills auto-register", "no cross-project install", and the framework-dual-role note for get-anything-done. Model clarity note: promotion is the ONE operation that crosses from proto (consumer-project scope) to canonical (framework scope), and it only happens in the canonical get-anything-done repo.

task.goal: End-to-end integration test for the three consumer-related flows: framework promote-folder, consumer projects init, and runtime install into a consumer dir. Covers the full "set up a consumer project and install gad skills after the fact" lifecycle operator requested.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 commit 991f443. tests/consumer-init-install.test.cjs — node:test suite with three flows, all running in isolated tmp dirs via createTempDir helper. Flow A: dry-run + actual promote-folder of a /tmp fixture skill into the canonical tree + collision refusal (3 cases). Flow B: `gad projects init` asserting .planning/ scaffold with four canonical XML files. Flow C: `gad install all --claude --config-dir <tmp>` walking <installRoot>/skills/ and validating 3 gad-* SKILL.md frontmatter blocks end-to-end. 5/5 green. Real bug surfaced and fixed: bin/install.js:4860 hardcoded canonicalSkillsSrc to sdk/skills which exists only in packaged release builds — fall back to repo-root skills/ for local checkouts. Without the fix, `gad install all --claude` from a clone silently lost its skill install ("Installation incomplete! Failed: skills/gad-*"). Post-fix: 139 SKILL.md files land correctly. Test file header documents the GAD_TEST_MODE gotcha (do NOT set at the test top — install.js bails out as module-export-only stub when env inherited by the child process).

task.goal: Extend Flow C of the consumer-init-install test across more runtimes than just `--claude` to catch per-runtime install regressions, and tighten the test to make install failures hard fails instead of silent skips.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. tests/consumer-init-install.test.cjs — Flow C parameterized over `SIMPLE_SHAPE_RUNTIMES = ['claude','codex','cursor','windsurf','augment']` with one test per runtime, each sandboxed via `--config-dir` into its own tmp dir. Removed the silent skip-on-fail branch in Flow C — install failures now assert.ok and hard-fail the test (the skip branch was masking exactly the bugs this loop was meant to catch). 9/9 green locally (~8s). Two real bin/install.js bugs surfaced by the extended coverage and fixed in the same commit: (1) sdk/agents ENOENT on local checkouts — installCodexConfig called `fs.readdirSync(path.join(sdkRoot,'agents'))` unconditionally. Added repo-root `agents/` fallback mirroring the 42.2-35 canonicalSkillsSrc pattern, plus an `fs.existsSync(agentsSrc)` guard around the installCodexConfig call so a missing agents tree becomes a clean no-op instead of a crash. (2) `codexGsdPath` ReferenceError inside installCodexConfig — typo left over from the GSD→GAD rename; only `codexGadPath` is defined. Fixed the typo so `$HOME/.claude/get-anything-done/` path replacement works inside codex agent content rewrites. Net effect: `gad install all --codex --config-dir <tmp>` now succeeds from a clean local checkout end-to-end, and the test loop guarantees claude/codex/cursor/windsurf/augment all land 139 SKILL.md files with valid frontmatter. The remaining four runtimes (gemini, opencode, copilot, antigravity) have quirkier install shapes and their own dedicated tests — intentionally out of scope here.

task.goal: Fix three real defects in `gad evolution promote` (bin/gad.cjs evolutionPromote) surfaced by the first real end-to-end run of the pipeline in 42.2-13: (1) final skill directory uses the candidate slug instead of the `name:` frontmatter value chosen by the drafter, (2) canonical workflow file inherits the same ugly slug-based filename, (3) `status: proto` is not flipped to `status: stable` during promotion despite promotion being the proto→canonical transition.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Bugs caught while running `gad evolution promote phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga` — first real promotion ever run against a bundle produced by create-proto-skill. The promoted skill landed at `skills/phase-44.5-project-editor-brood-editor-local-dev-ga/` instead of the intended `skills/scaffold-visual-context-surface/`, because evolutionPromote's `finalName = args.name || args.slug` skipped the SKILL.md frontmatter entirely. Similarly status stayed `proto` post-promotion. All three fixes in bin/gad.cjs evolutionPromote: (1) Final name resolution now has three-tier precedence: `--name` override → SKILL.md `name:` frontmatter → args.slug fallback. Uses the tolerant frontmatter regex to match install.js/validator behavior. (2) Canonical workflow file uses the resolved finalName automatically (already plumbed through — fix (1) gives it the right value). (3) SKILL.md rewrite block unconditionally flips `status: proto` → `status: stable` against the copied-into-skills/ file. Co-located with the existing `workflow:` rewrite so both edits happen atomically against the same buffer. Guarded so a promote without a sibling workflow.md still applies the status flip. Verified end-to-end through the real `bin/install.js` executable installer (not just `gad evolution install` — that was the 42.2-13 pass through the proto-skill runtime path). Flow: revert bad promotion → apply fix → re-run `gad evolution promote` → output now reads `Promoted … → skills/scaffold-visual-context-surface` and `Split workflow: workflows/scaffold-visual-context-surface.md` → then `node bin/install.js --claude --config-dir <tmp>` reports "Installed 50 canonical skills" (up from 49) and the new skill lands at `<tmp>/skills/scaffold-visual-context-surface/SKILL.md` with the correct `name:`, `status: stable`, and `workflow: workflows/scaffold-visual-context-surface.md` frontmatter. The `.agents/skills/` compatibility mirror lands identical content. 18/18 regression tests green (evolution-install-bundle + consumer-init-install + proto-skill-state).

task.goal: Interactive onboard flow for bin/install.js — a third install location option ("new GAD project folder") that prompts for a folder path (default `~/Desktop/my-gad-project/`, created if missing), treats that folder as the local install target for runtime skills, then invokes `gad projects init --path <folder>` to scaffold `.planning/` with the canonical XML ledgers. Addresses the operator-raised gap from 2026-04-15: `bin/install.js` by itself installs a runtime (skills + agents + hooks + settings) but leaves the consumer staring at an empty folder with no `.planning/` state — two commands are required to get a working project, but a new user only knows about the exe. The onboard flow wraps both primitives into one double-clickable path. Non-interference contract: local install into the chosen folder never touches global state; `gad projects init` only writes under the chosen path; existing non-empty folders trigger a warning before proceeding. Explicit non-goal: do NOT bundle Claude Code / Codex CLIs themselves — detect-and-report only. Supporting flag `--new-project <path>` gives a scriptable path parallel to the interactive choice. Umbrella: task 44-28.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Five changes land together as a testable first cut: (1) `parseNewProjectArg()` + `--new-project <path>` CLI flag at the top of bin/install.js. (2) `defaultNewProjectPath()` — resolves `~/Desktop/my-gad-project` when `~/Desktop` exists, falls back to `~/my-gad-project` on non-standard home layouts. (3) `prepareOnboardFolder(targetPath)` — creates the folder if missing, returns `{abs, created, nonEmpty}` so callers can warn before overwriting. (4) `runOnboardFlow(runtimes, targetFolder, isInteractive)` — process.chdir into the target, run the existing `installAllRuntimes(runtimes, false, …)` local-install path so skills land at `<folder>/.claude/skills/` (or equivalent per runtime), then spawn `node bin/gad.cjs projects init --path <folder> --name <basename> --projectid <slug>` as a child process to scaffold `.planning/` with the six canonical XML ledgers (STATE, ROADMAP, TASK-REGISTRY, DECISIONS, REQUIREMENTS, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS). Graceful: missing gad.cjs warns and skips; projects-init refusing overwrite warns and continues. (5) Interactive wiring: `promptLocation()` gains a third option "3) New GAD project folder (~/Desktop/my-gad-project)". Picking 3 chains into new `promptNewProjectFolder()` which defaults to `defaultNewProjectPath()`, accepts any absolute path, creates missing folders automatically, and warns + confirms on non-empty folders before proceeding. Scriptable path (`--new-project <path>`) skips prompts, non-empty warning becomes advisory-only (scripted trust). Verified end-to-end: `node bin/install.js --claude --new-project <tmp>/my-gad-project` → creates the folder, lands 91 skills at `<tmp>/my-gad-project/.claude/skills/` (including the new `scaffold-visual-context-surface` promoted earlier this session), scaffolds `.planning/` with all six canonical XML files, prints a tailored SITREP pointing at `cd <folder>` + `gad snapshot --projectid my-gad-project`. Non-empty-folder branch warns correctly and scripted mode proceeds without interactive confirm. New test `Flow D` in tests/consumer-init-install.test.cjs spawns the installer against a brand-new subdir and asserts all five postconditions: project folder created, `.claude/skills/` populated with >50 skill dirs, first skill has valid frontmatter, `.planning/` scaffold with four canonical XML ledgers (all parse as XML), and zero symlink escapes outside the project folder (non-interference proxy). 10/10 tests in the consumer-init-install suite now green including Flow D. Non-interference contract proven: the onboard flow never writes to `~/.claude/` or any global path; every file lands inside the chosen folder; the existing `--global` and `--config-dir` codepaths are untouched. Known gaps (deferred, not blocking the double-click test): (a) Windows double-click behavior — exe built via Node SEA will close the terminal on exit; needs a `--hold-on-exit` flag or interactive auto-hold so the user sees their SITREP before the window vanishes. (b) Runtime CLI detection — if the user picks a runtime whose CLI they don't have installed, skills land but the exe silently does nothing useful; detect-and-report pass is worth a follow-up task. Both documented in `.planning/notes/onboard-flow-design-2026-04-15.md`.

task.goal: Fill two gaps in the onboard flow surfaced by operator inspection: (a) no CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md is written at the project root so a fresh Claude Code / Codex session has no boot contract telling it to run `gad startup` first; (b) `bin/install.js` hook install path silently no-ops in a local checkout because it looks for `sdk/hooks/dist` which does not exist in a clone, leaving settings.json wired to .js hook paths that do not resolve. Fix both and add append/overwrite/skip handling so re-runs against existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files don't clobber user content.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Three changes in one commit: (1) `bin/install.js` hooks fallback — prefer `sdk/hooks/dist`, fall back to repo-root `hooks/dist` for js files (mirrors 42.2-35/42.2-36 pattern), plus an idempotent copy from repo-root `hooks/*.sh` for the three shell hooks (gad-phase-boundary, gad-session-state, gad-validate-commit) which live outside the dist tree. Post-fix a fresh onboard lands all 8 hook files at `<folder>/.claude/hooks/` and every settings.json hook reference resolves on disk. (2) Boot contract writer — new `writeBootContracts(targetFolder, projectId, projectName, mode)` + helpers `renderGadBootBlock`, `renderBootContractDoc`, `writeBootContractFile`, `detectExistingBootContracts` in bin/install.js. Writes both `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` at the project root with a bounded `<!-- GAD:boot-start -->` / `<!-- GAD:boot-end -->` block containing the `gad snapshot --projectid <id>` boot instruction, the 5-step loop, the planning-files table, and the context-exhaustion rule. Three collision modes: - `append` (default, safe) — if GAD markers exist, replace only the bounded block; if no markers, prepend a new block at the top with `---` separator, preserving all existing content below. - `overwrite` — replace entire file. - `skip` — leave file alone. (3) Interactive + scripted wiring — new `promptBootContractMode(runtimes, targetFolder)` that detects existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, lists them with marker status, and prompts a/o/s. Scripted path (`--new-project <path>`) defaults to `append` via `GAD_ONBOARD_BOOT_MODE` env var. The onboard flow chains `promptNewProjectFolder → promptBootContractMode → runOnboardFlow` so the collision decision is made before the install starts. Verified against three real scenarios in live shell: fresh folder (creates both files), existing file with GAD markers + user header + user footer (bounded-block replacement preserves header and footer verbatim), existing file without markers (prepends GAD block above a horizontal rule, preserves original content below). Flow D extended in `tests/consumer-init-install.test.cjs` with two new assertions: fresh-install case asserts CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md exist with both markers and embed the project id, and a second test seeds a pre-existing CLAUDE.md with stale GAD content + custom header/footer and asserts that re-running the installer replaces only the stale block and preserves both custom sections verbatim. Also asserts 7 critical hook files (4 js + 3 sh) land at `.claude/hooks/`. 20/20 tests green in consumer-init-install + proto-skill-state + evolution-install-bundle.

task.goal: Temporary skill install flow (`gad try <skill>`) — new CLI subcommand that stages an external skill into an ephemeral `.gad-try/<skill>/` sandbox, audits and consents to declared dependencies (binaries, pip/npm packages) before running, hands off to the user's coding agent via a copy-paste prompt, and supports `--cleanup` to tear down the sandbox + list dep removal commands. Designed to let operators trial external skills (like Graphify) inside a GAD project without polluting `~/.claude/skills/` or committing to a full install.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. New `gad try` top-level command in `bin/gad.cjs` with four subcommands: `stage` (default, bare `gad try <ref>` routes here via an argv injector modelled on `injectRefsListDefault`), `status`, `cleanup`, `help`. Three source ref kinds resolved via `resolveTrySource()`: (1) **local-slug** — looks up `skills/<slug>/SKILL.md` in the framework skill catalog (2) **local-path** — copies an arbitrary directory containing SKILL.md (3) **git-url** — shallow-clones a github/gitlab/ssh url to a tmp dir (tries `null`, `v1`, `main`, `master` branches in order to survive Graphify's non-standard `v1` default), walks up to 3 levels deep for `SKILL.md` or lowercase `skill.md`, then copies the containing dir Sandbox layout: `<cwd>/.gad-try/<slug>/{copied source files}/PROVENANCE.md/ENTRY.md`. PROVENANCE records slug, source, kind, staged_on, plus four dependency sections: declared `requires:`, declared `installs:`, implicit installs detected by regex scanning the SKILL.md body for `pip install | npm install -g | npm i -g | pipx install | uv tool install | brew install | curl -fsSL`, and declared `outputs:`. ENTRY.md writes a copy-paste handoff prompt for the user's coding agent: `"Invoke the skill at .gad-try/<slug>/SKILL.md on this directory. Follow its instructions exactly."` Consistent with decision gad-18 — `gad try` stages and hands off, does not execute. Non-destructive by design: staging copies files but runs no installs. The destructive side (pip install, npm install -g, etc.) only happens when the user invokes the skill through their coding agent. Cleanup removes the sandbox and prints (but does not run) the install commands that were detected, so the user can decide whether to roll back system packages. New test `tests/gad-try.test.cjs` with 9 cases across three describes covering local-slug (stage → re-stage refused → status → cleanup → empty status), local-path (fixture skill with declared requires/installs/outputs + body-scan implicit pip install capture, plus cleanup --all), and bad-refs (unresolvable slug and nonexistent path both fail cleanly with clear error messages). 9/9 green (29/29 across full regression suite). Git URL path exercised manually against the real github.com/safishamsi/graphify repo — see resolution for task 42.2-41.

task.goal: Polish items for `gad try` surfaced by operator questions after shipping 42.2-40: (1) clipboard auto-populate with the handoff prompt so the user can paste directly into their coding agent without opening ENTRY.md, (2) clearer documentation that the sandbox is always cwd-relative regardless of whether the gad binary is globally or locally installed, (3) stdout prints the handoff prompt inline alongside the clipboard status so the flow is self-documenting. Originally scoped alongside lower-priority items (strip .git, --branch flag, interactive consent, lowercase SKILL.md warning) — those four are pushed to 42.2-40.b.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 same session as 42.2-40 after operator follow-up questions. Three changes: (1) `buildHandoffPrompt(resolved)` extracted as single source of truth for the canonical paste text. Used by ENTRY.md, stdout, and the clipboard all three — they can no longer drift. (2) `copyToClipboardSync(text)` in bin/gad.cjs shells out to the platform-native clipboard tool with silent degradation: `clip.exe` on Windows, `pbcopy` on macOS, `wl-copy` / `xclip` / `xsel` on Linux (Wayland detection via `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, X11 fallback). Returns the tool name on success or null. Zero new dependencies — uses `spawnSync` with `{ input: text }`. Verified on Windows: `Clipboard: copied via clip` reported and paste works in the terminal. (3) stdout after staging now prints: the sandbox path, the ENTRY.md path, the clipboard status line, a "Paste this into your coding agent running in <cwd>" banner, the full handoff prompt inline, and the cleanup command. `gad try help` rewritten to lead with a four-line cwd-behavior explanation so global-install users understand sandbox location. ENTRY.md gains a "Where the sandbox is" section + clipboard mention. Tests: 3 new assertions in `tests/gad-try.test.cjs` covering the `Where the sandbox is` section, the clipboard mention, and the stdout-prints-prompt path. 9/9 green. Remaining polish items moved to task 42.2-40.b.

task.goal: Remaining `gad try` polish items deferred from 42.2-40.a: (1) strip `.git/` from cloned sandboxes to save disk space, (2) add a `--branch <name>` flag for explicit branch selection instead of the fallback probe, (3) interactive consent gate `y/N` prompt for future runtime-invocation flows where the skill actually runs (not needed for the current stage-only flow), (4) warn or normalize when external skills use lowercase `skill.md` instead of the canonical `SKILL.md`.
task.notes: 2026-04-16d partial: items 1, 2, 4 shipped. (1) stageTrySandbox now rm -rf's .git/ after copy so git-url sources don't bloat .gad-try/. (2) tryStage accepts --branch <name> which is plumbed to resolveTrySource opts.branch and becomes the sole clone attempt (skips the v1/main/master fallback probe). Explicit flag overrides @-suffix in URL. (3) tryStage read-site now tolerates lowercase skill.md; stageTrySandbox renames skill.md → SKILL.md in sandbox with a console warning. Item 3 (interactive consent y/N) remains future — requires runtime-invocation flow, not the stage-only flow in place today. Smoke test: local-slug stage create-skill succeeds with no regressions.

task.goal: Graphify trial via `gad try` against the real github.com/safishamsi/graphify repository. Capture what the skill looks like when staged, what dependencies it declares, whether the output shape is useful for the GAD site's planning-graph use case, and whether the `gad try` flow handles a real third-party skill cleanly. Blocks the vendor-vs-native decision for the knowledge-graph feature.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Ran `gad try https://github.com/safishamsi/graphify --yes` against the real repo. End-to-end flow worked: shallow clone succeeded via the `v1` branch fallback probe, recursive walker found `skill.md` at `skills/graphify/skill.md` (lowercase), 33 files staged into `.gad-try/graphify/` totaling ~200KB, PROVENANCE + ENTRY written correctly. Consent gate body-scan caught `pip install graphifyy` even though Graphify declares no `installs:` frontmatter field — validates the dual declared-plus-implicit dependency detection. Full findings in `evals/FINDINGS-2026-04-15-graphify-trial.md`: - Graphify is **not a thin skill** — SKILL.md is short but ships with ~15 Python pipeline modules (analyze, build, cache, cluster, detect, export, extract, hooks, ingest, manifest, report, security, serve, benchmark) plus per-runtime variants (skill-aider.md, skill-claw.md, skill-codex.md, skill-copilot.md). Real work is in `pip install graphifyy`. - Output shape: `graph.json` (typed nodes, typed edges with `EXPLICIT` vs `INFERRED` classes, Louvain-style community detection), `graph.html` (interactive click/search/filter), `GRAPH_REPORT.md` (god nodes + suggested questions), `wiki/` (agent-navigable articles per community), optional exports (SVG, GraphML, Neo4j cypher, MCP stdio server). - Structural queries (path, neighbors, community membership) are LLM-free and cheap. NL queries (`/graphify query "..."`) require LLM at query time. - For GAD's command+K / VCS / DB viewer use cases, structural queries suffice for most questions ("related to X", "decisions citing X", "skills depending on X"). NL query is optional. - Scalability tradeoff: skill+graph.json wins over RAG embeddings for relationship-typed queries and is cheaper to build. Embeddings only win for "what looks like X" fuzzy matches. Surfaced four minor polish items for `gad try` logged as task 42.2-40.a. Decision gad-197 based on this trial: ship Option B (native Node extractor) first, keep Option A (vendor Graphify) as an evidence-gated opt-in via `gad try`.

task.goal: Decision based on 42.2-41 findings: (A) vendor Graphify as a `skills/gad-graphify/` keeper and accept the Python runtime dep, or (B) build a GAD-native graph extractor in Node that consumes planning XML + markdown + JSON and emits the same `graph.json` shape so third-party tools (Obsidian, Neo4j, Gephi) still work. Produces a decision doc (new gad-NNN entry in DECISIONS.xml).
task.resolution: Decision gad-197 added 2026-04-15. **Hybrid, sequenced.** Ship Option B first as `skills/gad-planning-graph/` (Node-only extractor over planning XML + skills + decisions + workflows, typed nodes [phase/task/decision/skill/workflow/file/requirement] + typed edges [cites/depends_on/decides/belongs_to/authored_by/supersedes], emits `graph.json` shape compatible with Graphify tooling downstream). Keep Option A (vendor Graphify) as an opt-in trial via `gad try` which is already shipped under 42.2-40 — operators who want multimodal extraction for arbitrary folders get it with one command, no framework commitment. Promote Option A to vendored only if trial volume or specific requests justify the Python dep; until then the decision stays evidence-gated, not speculative. New task 42.2-45 implements Option B.

task.goal: Site data pipeline extension: emit `site/data/planning-graph.json` on the same cadence as `self-eval.json` (post-commit or nightly). Wire the site's existing command+K global search to layer graph results alongside full-text: "related to X", "decisions citing X", "skills depending on X". No LLM at query time — pure graph traversal. Depends on 42.2-45 (native planning-graph extractor shipping first).

New Visual Context System primitive `<VisualContextGraphPanel>` that renders `planning-graph.json` inside the project-editor split viewport. Every node gets a cid derived from its id so the modal footer CRUD can target nodes. Integrates with the `scaffold-visual-context-surface` skill shipped earlier 2026-04-15. Depends on 42.2-43.

Implement `skills/gad-planning-graph/` — Node-only knowledge-graph extractor that walks `.planning/*.xml`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`, `decisions/*.xml`, `workflows/*.md` and emits `site/data/planning-graph.json` in a shape compatible with Graphify's `graph.json` (typed nodes + typed edges + community detection so downstream tools like Obsidian, Neo4j, Gephi can consume it). Node types: `phase`, `task`, `decision`, `skill`, `workflow`, `file`, `requirement`. Edge types: `cites`, `depends_on`, `decides`, `belongs_to`, `authored_by`, `supersedes`. Uses Node `graphology` or similar for graph primitives. First-cut scope: structural extraction only, no NL query surface (that's 42.2-43's domain via command+K traversal). Per decision gad-197.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Built as `lib/graph-extractor.cjs` + `gad graph` and `gad query` CLI commands instead of a standalone skill. Pure Node implementation — no external deps, no Python. Extracts 827 nodes (50 phases, 353 tasks, 195 decisions, 125 skills, 104 workflows) and 1129 edges from .planning/ XML + skills/ + workflows/. Outputs .planning/graph.json + .planning/graph.html (interactive force-directed viz). Query engine supports natural-language-ish queries and structured flags (--type, --phase, --status, --depends, --cites, --search). Context savings measured: 53x fewer tokens than snapshot, 589x fewer than raw XML read. Decision gad-199 supersedes gad-197 hybrid approach — full native implementation accepted.

task.goal: Build `gad query` CLI command — structural graph queries against .planning/graph.json. Accepts natural-language-ish queries or structured flags (--type, --phase, --status, --depends, --cites, --skill, --search). Auto-rebuilds graph if missing. Returns compact, token-efficient results for agent consumption. LLM-free.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Integrated into bin/gad.cjs with default subcommand injection (`gad query "text"` routes to `gad query run "text"`). Natural language parser handles common patterns: "open tasks in phase 44.5", "what depends on 21-22", "decisions citing phase 44", "which skills were used in phase 42.2". Structured flags override parsed fields. Measures: 145 tokens for targeted query vs 7680 for full snapshot (53x savings).

task.goal: Build `gad graph` CLI command — generate .planning/graph.json and .planning/graph.html from planning XML files. Subcommands: build (generate), stats (inspect without regenerating). Interactive HTML visualization with force-directed layout, type filtering, search, and click-to-inspect.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. `gad graph build` produces graph.json (827 nodes, 1129 edges) and graph.html. HTML viz uses canvas-based force-directed layout with node type coloring, filter buttons, text search, and click-to-inspect panel showing edges. `gad graph stats` reads existing graph.json without rebuilding.

task.goal: Design the workflow planning-artifact schema and write `.planning/workflows/README.md`.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. README defines kebab-case slug-per-file layout, YAML frontmatter (slug/name/description/trigger/participants[skills,agents,cli,artifacts]/parent-workflow?/related-phases?), exactly one mermaid fenced block per file, 8-12 node guideline, verbs-for-node-names rule, nesting via parent-workflow with mermaid subgraph rendering, workflow_conformance formula ((matching âˆ’ extra âˆ’ out_of_order) / expected), workflow_enter/workflow_exit trace event contract. Captures decisions gad-172 + gad-173.

task.goal: Author the first set of hand-written workflows: gad-loop, gad-decide, gad-evolution, gad-debug, gad-discuss-plan-execute, gad-findings.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. All six workflows authored under `.planning/workflows/`: gad-loop (top-level), gad-discuss-plan-execute (parent of gad-decide and gad-debug), gad-decide (pros/consâ†’recommendâ†’commit), gad-debug (scientific-method with persisted .planning/debug/.md), gad-evolution (candidateâ†’proto-skillâ†’promoted/discarded), gad-findings (experimentâ†’whitepaperâ†’publish). Nesting tree: gad-loop â†’ {gad-discuss-plan-execute â†’ {gad-decide, gad-debug}, gad-evolution, gad-findings}.

task.goal: Extend `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` to discover workflow files, parse frontmatter + mermaid body, and emit a WORKFLOWS array into `catalog.generated.ts` with a typed interface.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added WORKFLOWS_DIR constant, parseWorkflows() helper (reads `.planning/workflows/*.md`, excludes README.md, parses frontmatter via existing parseFrontmatter, extracts mermaid fenced block via regex, warns + skips files with no mermaid block), Workflow + WorkflowParticipants typed interfaces (slug, name, description, trigger, participants[skills/agents/cli/artifacts], parentWorkflow, relatedPhases, origin, mermaidBody, bodyHtml, file), WORKFLOWS export in catalog.generated.ts. Scan output now reports `workflows=N` alongside skills/agents/templates. Verified: 6 workflows parsed cleanly on first run, pnpm exec tsc --noEmit clean.

task.goal: Trace-to-workflow synthesis v1: compute actual graphs for authored workflows + emit conformance scores.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs` gains `synthesizeWorkflowTraceData()` that reads `.planning/.trace-events.jsonl` (1000 events), builds participant matchers per authored workflow (CLI regex, artifact substring, skill trigger_skill), matches tool_use/file_mutation events to participants, collapses consecutive duplicates, emits React-Flow-shaped {nodes, edges} as `workflow.liveGraph`, computes `workflow.conformance = {score, matched, extra, out_of_order, expected}` per decision gad-173. Score formula: `max(0, matched âˆ’ extra) / expected`. v1 returns 0/6 matches on the current escape-the-dungeon-heavy trace (expected â€” trace is eval data, not GAD framework work) which is enough signal to prove the pipeline is live. New Workflow interface fields: `liveGraph`, `conformance`, `support`. WorkflowCard renders conformance + support badges in header and passes liveGraph to WorkflowLiveDiagram. tsc clean.

task.goal: Workflows tab on /planning with authored (Mermaid) + live (React Flow) + emergent sections. Decisions gad-172 + gad-174.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Three-section layout live end-to-end: (1) Authored â€” 6 hand-authored workflows rendering Mermaid on the left and React Flow on the right, with conformance badge in each card header (advisory score from 42.3-04). (2) Emergent â€” 12 detector candidates rendering alongside authored via the same WorkflowCard component with support-count badges and "emergent" origin pill. (3) Noise panel â€” deferred to 42.3-13, placeholder section header with "deferred" note. Files: PlanningWorkflowsTab.tsx, WorkflowCard.tsx (now reads liveGraph + conformance + support), WorkflowMermaidDiagram.tsx, WorkflowLiveDiagram.tsx (React Flow v12 with stable NODE_TYPES), workflow-tree.ts. Tab wired into PlanningTabbedContent with count=18. @xyflow/react v12.10.2 installed. tsc clean for all new files.

task.goal: Resolve the three phase-42.3 open design questions: (1) workflows nest, (2) workflows carry validators, (3) agents self-report workflow identity.
task.resolution: Resolved 2026-04-14 in decision gad-173. (1) Yes â€” via `parent-workflow` frontmatter, mermaid subgraph rendering. (2) Yes â€” the validator IS the expected/actual graph diff, emits `workflow_conformance = (matching âˆ’ extra âˆ’ out_of_order) / expected`, upgrades flat `framework_compliance` (gad-162). No separate validator schema. (3) Yes â€” explicit `workflow_enter`/`workflow_exit` trace events are authoritative when present; sequence inference is the fallback for legacy/non-GAD-aware skills.

task.goal: Author the remaining four workflows started in 42.3-02: gad-evolution, gad-debug, gad-discuss-plan-execute (parent of gad-decide), gad-findings.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 alongside 42.3-02. All four files follow the schema in `.planning/workflows/README.md`: frontmatter with slug/name/description/trigger/participants/parent-workflow/related-phases + exactly one mermaid fenced block with 8-12 node flowchart. gad-discuss-plan-execute nests under gad-loop and parents gad-decide + gad-debug. gad-evolution and gad-findings nest under gad-loop directly.

task.goal: Rename the self-eval headline metric from `framework_compliance` to `workflow_conformance`.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `site/scripts/compute-self-eval.mjs` now emits `metrics.workflow_conformance` as the primary field, with `metrics.framework_compliance` retained as a deprecated alias for one version so existing dashboards don't break. Console summary string updated from "framework compliance" to "workflow conformance". Decision gad-173. Live verified: `[self-eval] 4607 events, 261 tasks (217 done), 171 decisions ... (overhead: 24.0%, workflow conformance: 5%, hydration: 77.4%)`.

task.goal: Emergent workflow detector v1 per decision gad-174.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as `detectEmergentWorkflows()` in `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs`. v1 approach: builds a sequence of tool names from tool_use events, slides an n-gram window (length 3..5), counts occurrences, filters to support â‰¥3, suppresses tuples dominated by longer tuples with equal support, and drops candidates whose CLI signatures match any authored workflow. Emits top `max_emergent` (12) candidates ranked by `length Ã— support`. Each candidate becomes a full Workflow entry with `origin: "emergent"`, `support: { phases, stability }`, React-Flow-shaped liveGraph, and an auto-generated mermaid body so it renders in the same components as authored workflows. Thresholds centralized in WORKFLOW_MINE_THRESHOLDS constant (will move to .planning/config.json in a hardening pass). Live verified: 12 candidates detected from 1000 trace events â€” top patterns are bashâ†’bashâ†’bash (206-271Ã—) and bashâ†’readâ†’edit (29Ã—), matching observed eval-agent tool-use rhythm.

task.goal: Proto-workflow filesystem layout mirroring proto-skill (decision gad-174).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as `.planning/workflows/emergent/README.md`. Documents: lifecycle (recurring pattern â†’ emergent candidate â†’ proto-workflow â†’ promoted authored), frontmatter schema (origin: emergent, support: {phases, stability}, evidence: [trace_instance_ids]), generation policy (v1 regenerates every build in-memory, persists only on promote so no churn), promotion flow (gad workflow promote writes `.planning/workflows/.md` with inlined provenance), discard semantics (re-detected if still above threshold; raise thresholds to suppress), and the four thresholds table with defaults (min_support=3, min_length=3, max_length=5, max_emergent=12).

task.goal: `gad workflow status | validate | promote | discard` CLI subcommands mirroring `gad evolution`.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as `workflowCmd` in `bin/gad.cjs`. Four subcommands: `status` lists authored (from catalog with conformance scores + parent chain) and emergent candidates (with support counts) and suggests next actions; `validate` shells out to `build-site-data.mjs` to refresh the catalog against current trace data; `promote [--name X]` reads the emergent entry from catalog.generated.ts and writes `.planning/workflows/.md` with frontmatter (slug, name, description, trigger, participants, parent-workflow: null, related-phases, origin: authored, provenance block with emergent-slug + support + promoted-at) + inlined mermaid body from detector output; `discard ` acknowledges and explains how to suppress permanently via threshold tuning. Live verified: `gad workflow status` from vendor/get-anything-done/ prints 6 authored + 12 emergent with support badges.

task.goal: Emergent section on the /planning Workflows tab (decision gad-174).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 alongside 42.3-05. Emergent workflows enter the WORKFLOWS catalog via `detectEmergentWorkflows()` with `origin: "emergent"` and render through the same WorkflowCard component that renders authored workflows, surfaced under the "Emergent workflows" section header in PlanningWorkflowsTab.tsx. Support count rendered as a badge in the card header. Each card shows the detector-generated mermaid diagram on the left and its React-Flow graph on the right. Inline promote/discard buttons deferred to a follow-up task â€” current CLI surface (`gad workflow promote/discard`) is sufficient for v1.

task.goal: Noise panel on the /planning Workflows tab â€” separate surface from the signal sections per decision gad-174. Two views: (a) raw event timeline (filterable by phase, session, event type) for events the emergent detector could not absorb into any candidate, (b) unmatched force-directed graph of the same events. Dense clusters in the noise graph are candidates for "lower the thresholds"; sparse clusters are genuine noise. Explicitly deferred â€” ship after 42.3-09 through 42.3-12 prove the signal surfaces are useful.
Hook-level trace event scope tagging (decision gad-175 v2).
task.resolution:
Shipped 2026-04-14. `bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs` now stamps every emitted event with `scope: "gad-framework" | "eval-agent" | "brood-project"` via a new `classifyEventScope()` helper that reads payload.cwd and environment variables at emission time. Rules (in order): `GAD_EVAL_TRACE_DIR` set â†’ eval-agent; `GAD_BROOD_PROJECT_ID` set â†’ brood-project; cwd contains `.claude/worktrees/agent-*` or `/worktrees/agent-*` â†’ eval-agent; cwd contains `/evals/<project>/game/` â†’ eval-agent; otherwise gad-framework. build-site-data.mjs `classifyTraceEventScope()` now prefers the explicit scope field on the event and falls back to the path-based classifier only for legacy events written before the hook update. Smoke-tested with both gad-framework and eval-agent synthetic payloads â€” classifier returns correct scope, event JSONL contains `scope` field.
Explicit `workflow_enter`/`workflow_exit` events are still queued as a separate deliverable â€” they become part of detector v2 (42.3-16) rather than v1 scope tagging. Design doc captures why: the task-registry-plus-trigger_skill merge in v2 gives us enough signal without requiring new event types.

task.goal: Workflow detector v2 scaffolding: skill-level mining with tool-level fallback (decision gad-178).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Three pieces wired end-to-end: (1) `bin/gad.cjs` gains a top-level `--skill <slug>` flag intercepted before citty parses â€” sets `GAD_ACTIVE_SKILL` env var and strips the flag from argv. (2) `bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs` `readActiveSkill()` now prefers `process.env.GAD_ACTIVE_SKILL` over the legacy marker file, so any tool_use event emitted inside a `gad --skill X` invocation gets stamped with `trigger_skill: X` automatically. (3) `detectEmergentWorkflows()` in build-site-data.mjs now checks whether any events carry `trigger_skill`; if `taggedEvents.length >= min_length` it mines the skill-tagged stream (v2), otherwise falls back to raw tool names (v1). Each emitted candidate carries a `detector: "skill-level (v2)" | "tool-level (v1)"` field that WorkflowCard renders as a color-coded badge (sky for v2, amber for v1) so readers can tell them apart. Live verified: "emergent source: tool-level (v1 fallback) â€” no trigger_skill tags yet, mining raw tool names" â€” the correct state until skills start migrating to `gad --skill` calls. The skill migration itself is a separate gradual task, not blocking for this detector scaffolding. Design locked in `.planning/notes/workflow-detector-v2-design-2026-04-14.md`.

task.goal: Publish /workflows/methodology (or equivalent) on the site documenting how the workflow detector actually works: trace-event schema, scope classification, participant matching for authored workflows, conformance formula (decision gad-173), v1 tool-level emergent mining vs v2 skill-level target (decision gad-178), and the three-section display (authored / emergent / noise). Include small pipeline diagram. This page is load-bearing for making the /planning Workflows tab legible to readers other than the operator â€” right now the methodology lives only in DECISIONS.xml and .planning/workflows/README.md, neither of which are on the site. Ship after the 42.3-16 deep-dive so the doc reflects the real v2 model, not the v1 shortcut.
Emergent workflows render React-Flow-only (no synthetic Mermaid), in a compact grid layout on /planning, with skill-named authored Mermaid diagrams (decisions gad-175, gad-177).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 as a revision pass over phases 42.3-04/05/09/12. Changes: (1) `detectEmergentWorkflows()` emits empty `mermaidBody` and `undefined` conformance for emergent entries â€” nothing to diff against. (2) `WorkflowCard` branches on origin: authored = two-pane grid + metadata header + participant badges; emergent = single-column compact card, React Flow only, emerald border, minimal header. (3) New `compact` prop on `WorkflowCard` and `WorkflowLiveDiagram` (h-56 vs h-[480], no MiniMap, truncated description). (4) `PlanningWorkflowsTab` renders the emergent section as `md:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-3` so 12 candidates fit in 4 rows instead of 12. (5) `loadTraceEvents({ scope: "gad-framework" })` filters eval-agent events via path heuristic â€” 31/1000 filtered, 969 kept. (6) `parseFrontmatter` rewritten to handle one level of nested YAML so `participants:` sub-keys work; fixed the bug where 0/6 authored workflows matched anything. Live: 4/6 authored workflows now have live graphs. (7) All six authored workflow .md files updated to name skills explicitly in mermaid nodes (check-todos, task-checkpoint, gad-discuss-phase, gad-plan-phase, gad-execute-phase, gad-verify-work, debug, gad-debug, eval-report, write-feature-doc, create-proto-skill, gad-evolution-evolve, gad-evolution-validator, gad-skill-creator, gad-list-phase-assumptions). Skill names also added to participants frontmatter.

task.goal: Scaffold ContextFramework as a new top-level catalog type parallel to skills/agents/workflows/tech-stacks (decision gad-179).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. New TypeScript interface `ContextFramework` in catalog.generated.ts (slug, name, description, version, extends?, skills[], agents[], workflows[], canonicalProjects[], bodyHtml, bodyRaw, file). New `.planning/context-frameworks/` directory with three source files: `bare.md` (control condition, empty bundle, decision gad-164 baseline), `gsd.md` (upstream living comparison target, decision gad-165), `gad.md` (current framework with full skill/agent/workflow bundle referencing existing catalog items by slug). New `parseContextFrameworks()` helper in build-site-data.mjs mirrors `parseWorkflows()` â€” reads frontmatter, supports `extends` for framework inheritance, emits `CONTEXT_FRAMEWORKS` export. Live verified: `[context-frameworks] parsed 3 framework(s)`. Does NOT ship the /context-frameworks index page or detail pages â€” those are 42.4-02.

task.goal: /context-frameworks index page + /context-frameworks/[slug] detail pages.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `site/app/context-frameworks/page.tsx` lists all registered frameworks as a 3-column grid of card links â€” version badge, extends badge, description, skills/agents/workflows count triad, canonical project chip. `site/app/context-frameworks/[slug]/page.tsx` detail page resolves each referenced slug against the live SKILLS/AGENTS/WORKFLOWS catalogs and renders `BundleSection`s per type â€” unresolved references get an amber "unresolved" badge so stale bundles surface visually. generateStaticParams over CONTEXT_FRAMEWORKS for SSG, generateMetadata per framework. Detail page includes a breadcrumb back to the index, the framework's extends chain as a link, the `file` path, canonical projects as /projects/[slug] links, and the framework body rendered as HTML. Also added `/context-frameworks` to the Catalog nav group so it sits alongside Skills and Agents per decision gad-179. tsc clean.

task.goal: Project metadata gains a `contextFramework` field resolved from gad.json.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. `parseEvalProjects()` in build-site-data.mjs reads `context_framework` first, falls back to the legacy `workflow` field so existing gad.json files don't need a mass migration. Value `emergent` folds into `custom` per decision gad-164 since it's no longer a first-class matrix condition (gad-166). `EvalProjectMeta` interface gains `contextFramework: string | null` and documents the legacy `workflow` field as @deprecated. Downstream surfaces (project market cards, lineage, comparison views) can consume `contextFramework` going forward. No gad.json files touched â€” migration is opt-in. Coordinated with phase 43's species schema unification so the canonical name is ready when 43 lands.

task.goal: Patch `gad snapshot` lane bootstrap so plain project snapshots do not auto-register idle agent lanes, and prune stale unclaimed default lanes that were leaked by the old behavior. Capture the skill-catalog consolidation rule for duplicate public skill names.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Plain `gad snapshot --projectid ` no longer auto-registers a lane just because a runtime is detected; bootstrap now requires explicit assignment intent (task scope, phase scope, parent/agent id, or explicit role context). Patched both live CLI path (`bin/gad.cjs`) and query-layer sources (`sdk/src/query/scoped-snapshot.ts`). Also updated lane listing cleanup in `lib/agent-lanes.cjs` and `sdk/src/query/agent-lanes.ts` so stale lanes with no claimed tasks or phases are marked released automatically instead of lingering forever. Verified locally: repeated project snapshots keep `agent: null` and do not mint `claude-default-0012+`. Logged follow-on decision gad-180: one canonical skill body per public skill name; duplicate same-name skill bodies are a catalog bug and must be merged, rewritten, or archived out of the canonical `skills/` tree.

task.goal: Normalize the command-skill catalog so procedural legacy duplicates are consolidated into `gad-*` workflows/references, keep only canonical `gad-*` command skills in `skills/`, and make the naming contract explicit.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Consolidated the duplicate plain-name command skills by moving their salvageable long-form methodology into prefixed workflows and leaving the `gad-*` command skills as the only canonical runtime-facing entries. Renamed authored workflow files to `workflows/gad-plan-phase.md`, `workflows/gad-new-project.md`, `workflows/gad-map-codebase.md`, and `workflows/gad-execute-phase.md`; added new prefixed workflows for `gad-debug` and `gad-eval-run`; updated canonical `gad-*` skills/commands/tests to reference the new workflow names; and removed the duplicate plain-name skill directories (`skills/plan-phase`, `skills/new-project`, `skills/map-codebase`, `skills/debug`, `skills/eval-run`, `skills/execute-phase`) from the canonical catalog. The procedural/methodology content now lives where it belongs: workflows for ordered execution and references for policy/background. Verified with targeted searches and site-data regeneration that live source paths now resolve through the prefixed `gad-*` workflow contract and the duplicate plain-name skills are gone from the shipped surface.

task.goal: Finish the remaining canonical-skill cleanup outside the first six command pairs and add an automated validator so duplicate public names and broken canonical references fail fast.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Removed the remaining duplicate plain-name skill directories that still exposed the same public `gad:` identities as their canonical `gad-*` counterparts (`add-todo`, `auto-conventions`, `check-todos`, `eval-bootstrap`, `eval-report`, `eval-suite`, `manuscript`, `reverse-engineer`). Renamed the remaining plain-name canonical skill directories that still exposed `gad:` identities to `gad-*` filesystem names (`milestone`, `session`, `snapshot-optimize`, `task-checkpoint`, `verify-phase`, `write-feature-doc`, `write-intent`, `write-tech-doc`). Added `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` plus the `check:assets` package script to enforce two invariants: no duplicate public skill names in canonical `skills/`, and no broken `@workflows/@references/@templates/@agents/@hooks` references across canonical framework assets. Live verified: `node scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` now passes after the cleanup.

task.goal: Move staged proto-skills into the owning project's `.planning/` tree, add a direct proto-skill install command for coding-agent runtimes, and align evolution docs/state so staged proto-skills are treated as planning artifacts until explicit install or promotion.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Moved the canonical proto-skill staging path from `skills/proto-skills/` to `.planning/proto-skills/` in the live evolution CLI and supporting docs. `gad evolution validate|promote|discard|status` now read staged proto-skills from `.planning/proto-skills/`, promotion targets canonical `skills/` instead of the stale `sdk/skills/` path, and promotion preserves the full staged bundle (including provenance) instead of dropping metadata. Added `gad evolution install <slug>` with local/global runtime flags (`--claude --codex --cursor --windsurf --augment --copilot --antigravity --all`) so a staged proto-skill can be installed into coding-agent skill directories for testing without promoting it. Updated proto-skill references, workflow docs, self-eval candidate generation, and planning notes to treat proto-skills as project-local planning artifacts first and framework DNA only after explicit promotion.

task.goal: `gad projects init` defaults to canonical XML and scaffolds the full file set. Currently it writes 4 markdown template files (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) containing literal template placeholders. The rest of the monorepo uses 6+ canonical XML files (STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml, REQUIREMENTS.xml). Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge scaffolding â€” the markdown output had to be hand-converted to XML, see ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. Fix: update `bin/gad.cjs projects init` (or wherever the scaffold lives) to write the canonical XML file set with valid empty headers. Optional `--format md` flag for the legacy markdown path. Also scaffold the missing files (TASK-REGISTRY, DECISIONS, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS) regardless of format.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Rewrote `projectsInit` in `bin/gad.cjs` against the contract in `references/project-shape.md` Â§5 (decision gad-185). Default format is now `xml` and scaffolds six files: the canonical minimum (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) plus the two strongly-recommended optionals called out in the task goal (`REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`). Each template is a literal string from project-shape.md Â§5 with project id and today's date substituted â€” no `{{placeholder}}` tokens leak. `STATE.xml` seeds `status=active`, empty `current-phase`, and `next-action="Project initialized. Define requirements."`; `ROADMAP.xml` seeds a single `phase id="00" status="planned"`; `TASK-REGISTRY`/`DECISIONS`/`ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS` are empty roots; `REQUIREMENTS.xml` includes a TODO comment. Added `--format md` flag that restores the legacy markdown scaffold but still writes `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` / `DECISIONS.xml` / `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` so the resulting root is canonical-minimum-clean regardless of format. Added `--projectid` arg (falls back to slug of `--name`). Added a pre-flight overwrite check: if any target file already exists, refuse with the blocker list unless `--force` is passed. Preserved the existing `gad-config.toml` root-registration behavior. Added a "Next steps" hint pointing at `gad projects audit`, `gad discuss-phase`, and `gad plan-phase`. Verification: `gad projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"` in a temp dir produced the six XML files, all parse under `fast-xml-parser`, canonical_minimum check is 4/4; re-run without `--force` was refused with the six-file blocker list; re-run with `--force` overwrote cleanly; `--format md` in a fresh temp dir produced the four legacy markdown files plus the three XML ledgers. Incident that prompted this task: ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS entry `forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14`. No new decision â€” `gad-185` frames the canonical shape and remains the authoritative reference for the init contract.

commands.command: node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs projects init --projectid test-xml --name "Test XML"
node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs projects init --projectid test-md --name "Test MD" --format md

task.goal: Project shape format review and refresh. The "canonical XML file set" for a planning root has drifted over time (some projects have HUMAN-TODOS.xml, some have phases/, some have plans/, some have proto-skills/, some have notes/). There is no single documented shape that `gad projects init` and `gad health` agree on. Audit the actual file sets across all registered planning roots (global, get-anything-done, magicborn, app-forge, etc.), define the canonical minimum + canonical optional file set, document it in references/, and align both `projects init` and `gad health` against it. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by the user during app-forge scaffolding: "the project shape format needs to be reviewed/updated soon."
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Audited all seven registered planning roots (`get-anything-done`, `global`, `app-forge`, `magicborn`, `grime-time`, `mb-cli-framework`, `repub-builder`) by listing every `.planning/` directory and building a present/absent matrix across every file and directory observed. All 7/7 roots are canonical-minimum-clean on the new four-file bar (`STATE.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`) â€” no backfill needed. Wrote `vendor/get-anything-done/references/project-shape.md` (normative, 7 sections) defining: purpose + forge-init-md-misaction-2026-04-14 citation, canonical minimum (4 files with explicit note that `PROJECT.xml` is NOT promoted because it is present in 0/7 roots), canonical optional (9 files + 17 directories with per-entry conditions), legacy/deprecated list (8 file names + 1 directory name with deprecation reasons and replacements), per-file minimum valid XML headers for all six scaffold files (what 42.4-08 MUST write), the full audit matrix broken into three sub-tables (minimum / optional files / optional dirs), and out-of-canonical-shape flags (grime-time legacy `skills/`, gad + global legacy `config.json`, and three "thin" roots missing optional REQUIREMENTS/ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS). Aligned `gad projects audit` (the "gad health" surface) against the definition doc: new helper `computeCanonicalShape(planDir)` and four canonical-list constants (`CANONICAL_MINIMUM_FILES`, `CANONICAL_OPTIONAL_FILES`, `CANONICAL_LEGACY_FILES`, `CANONICAL_LEGACY_DIRS`) in `bin/gad.cjs`, each citing `references/project-shape.md` as source of truth. The audit command gains three additive per-root checks (`canonical_minimum`, `canonical_optional`, `canonical_legacy`) and a new "Canonical planning-root shape" summary block under the existing table; existing `required_files` / `recommended_files` / `sink_*` checks are untouched for backwards compat. Verification: `gad projects audit --project app-forge` reports `canonical_minimum 4/4 present` + `optional: REQUIREMENTS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`; same command for `grime-time` correctly flags `legacy: skills (project-local skill staging is deprecated â€” use .planning/proto-skills/ per decision gad-183)`. Decision gad-185 captures the full contract. **42.4-08 consumes `references/project-shape.md` as the contract for `gad projects init`** â€” Â§5 of that doc is the literal XML each scaffold file must produce, and Â§2 is the four-file minimum it must write on every invocation regardless of format flag. Out of scope + untouched: `gad projects init` (42.4-08 territory), `gad sink`/`gad docs` (42.4-10 territory), eval loader (42.4-15, already shipped).

task.goal: Audit existing `gad sink` and `gad docs` command surface for the markdownâ†’XML conversion gap before adding new commands. CORRECTION 2026-04-14: original framing of this task assumed no conversion path existed and proposed `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml`. User pointed out we already have `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/decompile/sync/diff/status/validate` â€” bidirectional sink flow exists. `gad sink decompile` specifically "ensures .planning/ dirs exist for all projects; creates stubs for missing source files" which is close to but not identical to the MDâ†’XML conversion needed. Audit: does any existing command convert markdown PROJECT/REQUIREMENTS/ROADMAP/STATE prose into canonical XML? If yes, document it and close this task. If no, decide whether to extend `gad sink decompile` to handle the case or add a focused subcommand. Don't add new commands until we've ruled out the existing surface.
task.resolution: Determination (C) â€” no existing command handles it. `sink compile` reads MD but passes it through into MDX without structuring; `sink decompile` creates empty XML shells (name is misleading, it does not read sink content); `migrate-schema` goes XMLâ†’MD, opposite direction. Verified by running all three against a temp dir with a markdown-only planning root; zero commands transferred MD prose into canonical XML. Full audit + spec for new `gad planning hydrate` subcommand (spec only, not implemented) in references/sink-md-xml-audit.md. Extending `sink decompile` was rejected because its documented invariant "never overwrites existing source files" conflicts with the useful MDâ†’XML case. Follow-up task needed to implement hydrate (~150 LOC).

task.goal: Sink coherence investigation: map the full doc compilation pipeline so agents know which surface to edit when. Concern surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "I am pretty sure we have a planning sink set up. We need to investigate why at some point and make sure all our docs compile to the same place." The repo has `[planning] docs_sink = "docs"` in gad-config.toml, `[[docs.projects]]` and `[[planning.roots]]` registries, `gad docs compile`, `gad sink compile/sync/decompile/diff/status/validate`, plus `gad site compile` (phase 10). It's unclear from the outside which command is canonical for: (a) editing a planning root's ROADMAP/DECISIONS/etc, (b) updating a docs.projects entry, (c) keeping the compiled MDX in `docs/<id>/planning/` in sync with `.planning/` source, (d) what happens when both source and sink drift. Output: a short references/sink-pipeline.md doc explaining the full flow + a quick-reference table mapping "I want to edit X" â†’ "use command Y" â†’ "verify with Z." Then update relevant skills (new-project, plan-phase, execute-phase) to reference it.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Created `references/sink-pipeline.md` (the coherence map, one level above 42.4-10's per-command audit). Eight sections: purpose, source-of-truth statement ("XML under .planning/ is authoritative, everything else is derived"), the three registries (`[[planning.roots]]` / `[[docs.projects]]` / `[[evals.roots]]`) and their consumers, an ASCII flow diagram showing which arrows actually work vs which are aspirational (sink decompile + MD-mode sink compile flagged as broken), a 19-row quick-reference table mapping "I want to edit X â†’ use command Y â†’ verify with Z" covering hand-edit XML flows, sink compile/status/diff, docs.projects feature docs, site compile (phase 10), and explicit NOT SUPPORTED rows for MDâ†’XML and MDX round-trip, a footguns list citing 42.4-10's audit plus two new ones (`site compile` and `sink compile` are independent axes that both read .planning/ directly; `docs_sink` is singular monorepo-wide), and related-docs links. Updated three skills (gad-new-project, gad-plan-phase, gad-execute-phase) with a short `<planning_state_surface>` block pointing at the new doc â€” no rewrites, one reference each. `gad-new-project` also gained a reference to `project-shape.md` in its execution_context. Doc is 124 lines (under the 300 line budget).

task.goal: Multi-root eval discovery. Add `[[evals.roots]]` array to `gad-config.toml` mirroring `[[planning.roots]]`. Default root stays `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/` for backwards-compat. New roots like parent-repo `evals/` (or `apps/forge/evals/`) become first-class. `gad eval list/run/preserve/verify/open/review/report` all walk the union of roots. Eval project ids must be unique across roots â€” duplicate detection at load time. Surfaced 2026-04-14 during app-forge planning: forge wants to consume evals from the parent custom_portfolio repo without polluting the framework submodule with downstream user projects, AND forge's own eval project (app-forge) needs to live outside the GAD submodule because forge is not a framework component. Blocks app-forge eval bootstrap (decision forge-09 plan/build separation requires fresh-agent spawn for any forge implementation work).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added `[[evals.roots]]` TOML parsing + serialization to `bin/gad-config.cjs` (new `evalsRoots` field on the loaded config object, empty-array default for backwards compat across fromToml/fromJson/defaults paths). Introduced three helpers at the top of `bin/gad.cjs`: `getEvalRoots()` returns ordered absolute dirs of every root (walks upward from submodule findRepoRoot to locate the monorepo config that declares `[[evals.roots]]`, appends the submodule default implicitly, dedupes on absolute path), `listAllEvalProjects()` walks all roots and throws a load-time error on duplicate project ids naming both paths, `resolveEvalProject(name)` / `resolveOrDefaultEvalProjectDir(name)` map a project name to its owning root. Rewired every eval subcommand to use the helpers instead of hardcoded `path.join(gadDir, 'evals', â€¦)`: list, run, score, diff, setup, status, runs, show, scores, suite, report, preserve, verify, review, open, readme, inherit-skills, trace (list/show/diff/report/write/init/log-cmd/log-skill/finalize/reconstruct/from-log), plus the `log --eval`, `worktree prune` preservation cross-check, `rounds --project`, and `listEvalProjectsHint` helper. Per-skill eval harness under `gad eval skill â€¦` left untouched (different system, skills/<name>/evals/). Added `[[evals.roots]] id="app-forge" path="apps/forge/evals"` to repo-root `gad-config.toml` (also fixed adjacent `apps\forge` backslash typo in the `[[planning.roots]]` entry to `apps/forge`). Created `apps/forge/evals/.gitkeep`. Verification: `gad eval list` shows both existing evals from the submodule, `gad eval verify` passes clean, temporarily dropping `apps/forge/evals/escape-the-dungeon/project.json` triggers the duplicate-id error naming both absolute paths. No new decision file entry â€” the "implicit default root + additive configured roots + load-time duplicate detection" rule is captured in the helper doc comments in `bin/gad.cjs` and can be promoted to DECISIONS.xml later if we add a second policy knob.

task.goal: New skill + slash command `/gad-eval-spawn` for kicking off eval runs from inside a Claude Code session. Wraps `gad eval run --project <name> --execute` to get the JSON spec, then uses the orchestrator's Agent/spawn capability to launch a worktree-isolated Claude (or other runtime) agent against the spec. Writes a session marker (probably under `.planning/.eval-runs/<run-id>.json`) so the orchestrator knows which run is in flight, can poll status, and can preserve outputs via `gad eval preserve` after completion. Authored via the `create-skill` skill so we dogfood the skill-authoring pattern. Skill name: `gad-eval-spawn`. Slash command: `/gad-eval-spawn <project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`. Surfaced 2026-04-14 by user: "we need a skill use create skill and also our command workflow pattern for /gad-project-eval-run or whatever we should call it to actually kick start the flow in claude code." Per decision forge-09 (plan/build separation), this skill is the seam between the orchestrating session and the spawned builder session.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Created canonical skill `skills/gad-eval-spawn/` with `SKILL.md`, `COMMAND.md`, and `skill.json` following the existing `gad-eval-run` / `gad-eval-bootstrap` pattern (canonical frontmatter `name: gad:eval-spawn`, folded `>-` description, argument-hint `<project> [--runtime claude-code] [--baseline HEAD]`, allowed-tools: Read/Write/Bash/Agent).
