Procedure documents the full plan/build seam: resolve project via `gad eval list`, call `gad eval run --project <name> --runtime <r> --baseline <b> --execute` to produce EXEC.json (the CLI already emits this between `--- EXEC_JSON_START/END ---` markers â€” no CLI change needed), refuse if `.planning/.eval-runs/<project>-<version>.json` marker already exists, create worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<project>-<version>` from `--baseline`, write session marker with `status: "running"` + runId/project/version/species/generation/worktreePath/runDir/agentId/startedAt, spawn `general-purpose` subagent with `isolation: "worktree"`, cwd + prompt + env pulled verbatim from EXEC.json (never rewrap the prompt â€” it's already fully hydrated), on completion update marker â†’ `agent-complete`, run `gad eval preserve <project> <version> --from <worktree>` (mandatory per gad-38) â†’ `preserved`, then `gad worktree clean` â†’ `cleaned`. Failure modes: spawn-fail removes marker, agent-fail leaves worktree for forensics, preserve-fail blocks cleanup, duplicate marker refuses. `.planning/.eval-runs/` added to submodule .gitignore as transient runtime state. `scripts/validate-canonical-assets.mjs` passes. Cited decisions forge-09 (plan/build seam) and gad-38 (preservation contract). Dogfood note: the `create-skill` skill's YAML frontmatter rules (folded `>-`, colons-safe, indentation) guided the SKILL.md shape cleanly â€” no friction. `gad eval run --execute` already existed at bin/gad.cjs:1932+ and emits the EXEC.json shape used by this skill (runId is derived as `<project>-<version>` since the CLI keys on version rather than a separate run-id field), so no CLI addition was needed.

task.goal: Audit and finish phase 43 species.json migration. Phase 43 unified the eval schema around `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json`, but escape-the-dungeon's existing species directories still use `gad.json` instead of `species.json`. Decide canonical name (recommend: `species.json` per phase 43 spec) and either: (a) rename `gad.json` â†’ `species.json` across all existing species in `vendor/get-anything-done/evals/`, OR (b) update phase 43 prose + the CLI loader to treat `gad.json` as the canonical name and abandon `species.json`. User direction 2026-04-14: do the rename to species.json. Update CLI loaders, build-site-data.mjs, eval-preservation tests, and any references in skills/workflows/docs. Pick one shape so app-forge's species file isn't ahead of reality and so the project âŠ‡ species inheritance contract (42.4-15) has a stable file shape to read from.

Project âŠ‡ Species inheritance contract in the CLI eval loader. `project.json` declares defaults: `techStack`, `contextFramework`, `installedSkills`, `defaultContent` (paths or refs to default assets/files/scaffolding). `species.json` may override any of those + adds species-only fields: `inherits_from` (parent species for cross-species lineage), `dna` (workflow + skill manifest summary), species-specific `description`. The CLI's eval loaders (used by `gad eval list/run/preserve` and by build-site-data.mjs) MUST merge project + species at load time and return the merged shape â€” readers should never manually combine them. Shipped: new `lib/eval-loader.cjs` exporting `loadProject`, `loadSpecies`, `loadAllSpeciesRaw`, `loadResolvedSpecies`, `loadAllResolvedSpecies`, `mergeProjectSpecies`, `resolveInheritanceChain`. Merge semantics per decision gad-184: project defaults â†’ inherits_from chain (deepest ancestor first) â†’ target species; last write wins; arrays REPLACE not merge; species-only fields (inherits_from, dna, description) do not back-propagate to project; cycles throw. Wired `bin/gad.cjs eval list` to the loader and swapped `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalProjects` to use `loadResolvedSpecies` so the site sees the merged shape. Hermetic tests in `tests/eval-loader-inheritance.test.cjs` cover (a)-(e) plus loadAllResolvedSpecies + pure-function spot check (7/7 pass via `node --test`). Other legacy `gad.json` reads in bin/gad.cjs (eval run, eval verify, eval review, eval open/report) are orthogonal rename fallout and left for a separate surgical pass.

Forge proving-ground spec: formalize "forge produces a passing escape-the-dungeon generation from its existing REQUIREMENTS.md via the editor's spawn flow" as a measurable acceptance criterion. Decision forge-11 captures the why. Output: checklist doc at `apps/forge/.planning/plans/forge-proving-ground.md` defining PG-01..PG-09 (build preserved, clean headless load, zero console.error, game root mounts, player renders, tilemap renders, input moves player, no unhandled rejections, probe runtime < 30s). Historical comparison table retroactively scores all 12 ETD gad-species generations (v1..v12): 4 have preserved builds (v7, v8, v9, v10), exactly 1 (v8) has an explicit SCORE.md "Build requirement gate PASSED" annotation, and PG-02..PG-08 are `?` across the board because no mechanical probe has ever run against an ETD build. GAPs flagged: no Playwright harness in repo (phase 08 scope), no canonical ETD root selector / player hook, input scheme varies arrow vs WASD. DOG-02 in `apps/forge/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` and phase 08 success criteria in `apps/forge/.planning/ROADMAP.xml` both updated to cite the checklist. Last of the 42.4-12..16 framework gates blocking app-forge phase 01.

Implement `gad planning hydrate` â€” the MD â†’ XML inverse of `gad docs compile`. Closes the follow-up flagged by task 42.4-10's audit (references/sink-md-xml-audit.md Â§6). Subcommand walks configured planning roots (or `--projectid`/`--all`), reads sibling markdown for each canonical slot (STATE, ROADMAP, DECISIONS, TASK-REGISTRY, REQUIREMENTS), and writes canonical XML into the root's `.planning/` directory. Supports `--from <dir>` to read MD from an external source dir (default is the planning dir itself), `--dry-run` to print the generated XML without writing, and `--force` to overwrite existing XML (prior XML is archived under `.planning/archive/xml/<ts>/` first). Non-force runs skip slots where the XML already exists, preserving the "XML is authoritative" invariant from decision 42.4-10.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added five inverse parsers (`stateMdToXml`, `roadmapMdToXml`, `decisionsMdToXml`, `taskRegistryMdToXml`, `requirementsMdToXml`) plus a `hydrateFromMd(planDir, fromDir, options)` orchestrator to `lib/docs-compiler.cjs` â€” each parser mirrors its `*XmlToMd` sibling field-for-field and reuses the same slot shape. Hydrator walks a new `HYDRATE_MAP` (parallel to `SOURCE_MAP`), honours `force`/`dryRun`, and returns `{ written, skipped, archived, results }` for CLI rendering. Added `planning` top-level subcommand group to `bin/gad.cjs` with a single `hydrate` subcommand (args: `--projectid`, `--all`, `--from`, `--dry-run`, `--force`); registered in main subCommands map between `docs` and `site`. Smoke-tested all five parsers against hand-crafted MD input (every canonical field round-trips), end-to-end hydrator run through a temp dir verified write + skip-when-exists + force-with-archive behavior, and CLI `--help` surface confirmed. LOC: lib/docs-compiler.cjs +267, bin/gad.cjs +59 (~326 total, close to the audit's ~150 LOC estimate â€” overshoot is from the inverse parsers being heavier than the forward ones because of paragraph/heading splitting). Closes 42.4-10's follow-up; the audit doc's open design questions (bidirectional-safety, `projects init` integration) are deliberately left as separate follow-ups.

task.goal: Delete dead project-level `gad.json` fallthrough reads in `bin/gad.cjs` (decision gad-184). Task 42.4-14 renamed `evals/<project>/gad.json` to species-level `evals/<project>/species/<sp>/species.json`, but five call sites in `bin/gad.cjs` (buildEvalPrompt / eval run metadata + traceScaffold / eval verify / eval review --rubric / eval readme) still read from the old project-level path. Because the file no longer exists, every read silently fell back to `{}` and every downstream field rendered as a default or dash â€” legacy fallback fallout flagged in task 42.4-15's note. Delete the reads; inline defaults where a field must remain; rewire `eval review --rubric` and `eval readme` through `lib/eval-loader.cjs::loadAllResolvedSpecies` so they pull `humanReviewRubric` / description / domain / tech stack / build requirement from the resolved-species shape instead.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Five sites edited in `bin/gad.cjs`: (1) `buildEvalPrompt` â€” removed the `gad.json` cfg load and the brownfield-baseline block that depended on it (block was already unreachable). (2) `eval run` traceScaffold â€” removed the `gadJson` variable and inlined `eval_type: 'greenfield'`, `workflow: 'unknown'`, `domain/tech_stack/build_requirement: null`. (3) `eval verify` audit â€” dropped the `cfg.type`/`cfg.constraints.planning_only` branch and hardcoded `evalType = 'implementation'` (the only value the block ever produced post-rename). (4) `eval review --rubric` â€” rewired to call `loadAllResolvedSpecies(projectDir)` and walk resolved species looking for `humanReviewRubric` (camelCase canonical per 42.4-15) with a legacy `human_review_rubric` fallback so the command keeps working before task 42.4-19 converts the species.json files. (5) `eval readme` â€” rewired the project-metadata header table to use `loadEvalProject` + first-resolved-species for description/domain/mode/workflow/tech-stack/build-requirement. Every remaining `gad.json` literal in the file is either the canonical legacy-files list entry (line 985) or a "this was removed in 42.4-18" comment. Syntax check + `gad eval list` smoke test pass. Coordinated with parallel 42.4-17 planning-hydrate work by staying clear of `planningCmd` / `planningHydrateCmd` (lines ~2005-2060).

task.goal: Normalize species.json keys to camelCase (decision gad-184, follow-up to task 42.4-15). The project âŠ‡ species inheritance loader (`lib/eval-loader.cjs`) does literal key matching via `applyLayer`, so when `project.json` declares `techStack` (camelCase) and a species declares `tech_stack` (snake_case), the species override silently fails and both keys end up on the merged shape. Per gad-184 contract, camelCase is canonical. Rename `tech_stack`, `build_requirement`, `human_review_rubric`, and `context_framework` to camelCase across every species.json under `evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/*/` and `evals/gad-explainer-video/species/*/`; confirm the eval loader needs no special-casing (it does not â€” literal-match is the point); update all consumers reading those snake_case keys.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. (1) All 6 species.json files updated â€” tech_stack â†’ techStack, build_requirement â†’ buildRequirement, human_review_rubric â†’ humanReviewRubric, context_framework â†’ contextFramework. Species-only `inherits_from` left alone because it is the loader's documented API contract. Nested rubric dimension keys (ui_polish, mechanics_implementation, etc.) left alone because they are rubric data values, not inheritance-level object keys. `constraints.uses_gad_framework` etc. similarly left alone â€” constraints are replaced wholesale, no key-clash issue. (2) Consumer updates: `bin/gad.cjs eval list` (dropped `|| projectCfg.tech_stack` fallbacks on the list loop and the first-species fallback), `bin/gad.cjs eval review --rubric` (dropped the legacy `human_review_rubric` fallback I wired temporarily in 42.4-18), `bin/gad.cjs eval readme` (dropped the `tech_stack` / `build_requirement` fallbacks in the pick() chain), `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalSpecies` (reads `data.humanReviewRubric`, `data.buildRequirement`, `data.contextFramework` directly; dropped snake_case fallbacks on lines 512/529/531/532), `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs scanEvalParents` (dropped `|| p.meta.tech_stack`), `site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs inferWorkflow` (reads `data.contextFramework` instead of `data.context_framework`). (3) `lib/score-generator.cjs` also carried a dead `gad.json` read for `build_requirement`; rewired through `loadAllResolvedSpecies` + `buildRequirement` with a `trace.build_requirement` fallback (TRACE.json stays snake_case â€” that's a schema field on already-written artifacts, out of scope). (4) `lib/eval-loader.cjs` JSDoc updated (`human_review_rubric` â†’ `humanReviewRubric`). Verification: hermetic inheritance check via `loadAllResolvedSpecies` confirms the `techStack: "kaplay"` (escape-the-dungeon) and `techStack: "remotion"` (gad-explainer-video) project defaults now correctly propagate through to every species, and `humanReviewRubric` resolves with the expected dimension counts (bare 5, emergent 6, gad 5 for ETD; gad 5 for explainer video). `gad eval list` smoke test passes.

task.goal: Disambiguate `gad site serve` (planning/landing Next static extract) from `gad play` / `gad generation open` (preserved generation HTML). Unify generation artifact serving on `lib/site-compile.cjs` `serveStatic` instead of `npx serve`; update CLI help strings; record decision gad-225.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. `serveStatic` accepts `logPrefix` and optional `onListening` callback. `gad generation open` / `gad eval open` / `gad play` resolve preserved `index.html`, then call `serveStatic` with `[gad play]` prefix and browser open on listen. Removed `npx serve` subprocess. Updated meta descriptions for `generation`, `site`, `play`, `site serve`, and `generation open`. Added DECISIONS.xml gad-225. Documented split in vendor/get-anything-done/AGENTS.md.

task.goal: TRACK 3 formula display remainder: refine /how-it-works pressure section (Section 3) to show current values, not just the abstract formula. Previous session delivered formula + dimension table + Shannon entropy decomposition; this task adds a "Live example" card between the dimensions table and entropy cards that computes the top-pressure phase from `site/data/self-eval.json` and renders each pressure term with its raw count, weight, and contribution — plus H_d / H_l side-by-side — so a reader sees the formula evaluated end-to-end against real planning data.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Added `PressureRow` local type + `pickTopPressurePhase()` selector + `PRESSURE_WEIGHTS` constant to `site/app/how-it-works/page.tsx`. New Card with `data-cid="pressure-live-example"` rendered between the 5-row dimensions block and the Shannon entropy decomposition. Table columns: Term / Raw / Weight / Contribution, one row per T, C_a, C_l, D, D/T, plus a total-pressure footer. H_d and H_l rendered as two labeled sub-cards underneath. Data source: `self-eval.json.latest.phases_pressure` — regenerated on every site build from `.planning/` XML, so the live example always tracks the current highest-pressure phase in our own planning (currently 42.2, P = 173.41). Typecheck + `npm run build` clean; rendered HTML verified via dev server probe. No new dependencies, no component changes, no new routes.

task.goal: Artifact preview must not load `site-compile`: extract `lib/static-http-serve.cjs`, wire `gad generation open` / `gad play` through shared helper; add `--no-browser` for iframe URLs; vocabulary “build artifact” not “site”.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. New `static-http-serve.cjs`; `site-compile` delegates with `[gad site]` default. `servePreservedGenerationBuildArtifact` in `bin/gad.cjs`; log prefixes `[gad generation open]` vs `[gad play]`. gad-225 summary/impact updated; AGENTS, quick-start, root CLAUDE, CHANGELOG.

task.goal: `gad site serve` distinct default ports: dev/monorepo 3456 vs packaged consumer 3780 via `--consumer`; env `GAD_SITE_SERVE_PORT` / `GAD_SITE_PORT`; explicit `--port` wins.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-17. `resolveGadSiteServePortDetailed` in `bin/gad.cjs`; `--consumer` flag; port arg default empty; log line explains source. AGENTS + quick-start + CHANGELOG.

task.goal: Tree-format output for gad snapshot refs section per decision gad-241. Replace flat '- path' list with indented tree that groups shared prefixes (e.g. .planning/workflows/*, references/*, site/{lib,scripts,app}/*). Implementation: lib/snapshot-compact.cjs gains compactRefsTree() with single-child dir-chain collapse and single-leaf fold. Threshold REFS_TREE_THRESHOLD=5 keeps small lists flat. Tests: tests/snapshot-compact-refs-tree.test.cjs covers empty/below-threshold/tree-mode/collapse/fold/annotations/duplicates/dir-suffix/token-savings/compactStateXml-wiring (11 tests). Measured: full snapshot 17813B → 17622B (−191B, ~1%); refs section alone ~9% reduction; BPE token savings higher than byte due to shared-prefix coalescing. Follow-ups: apply same tree form to gad startup contract output; investigate deeper compression on DECISIONS section (title-only already compact).

Delete the conditions being dropped from the eval matrix: escape-the-dungeon-gad-emergent (D-20), escape-the-dungeon-planning-only (D-21), all etd-brownfield-* (D-22, drops greenfield/brownfield mode framing entirely), and all etd-{babylonjs,phaser,pixijs,threejs} tech-stack variants (D-23, not testing tech-stack variation under the new schema). Also delete corresponding preserved builds under apps/portfolio/public/evals/ if present. Single commit.

Physical merge of escape-the-dungeon family (gad/bare/emergent) into one parent dir with species/ subdirs (D-09, D-26). Same merge for gad-explainer-video family (D-25). Use git mv via temp-rename intermediate so directory history is preserved. After this lands, evals/escape-the-dungeon/species/{gad,bare,emergent}/ contains the old sibling generations.

Define the new gad.json schema (project.json + species.json split) and migrate every project to it. Drop eval_mode field everywhere (D-22). Stub cardImage/tagline/heroImage fields for phase 44 to populate (D-14). Drop compare_to references to deleted projects. Per follow-up: single-variant projects (cli-efficiency, reader-workspace, etc.) deleted entirely â€” only escape-the-dungeon and gad-explainer-video remain.

Migrate preserved build artifact paths under apps/portfolio/public/evals/ from flat-name to project/species nesting. escape-the-dungeon-bare/v3/ becomes escape-the-dungeon/bare/v3/ etc. Use git mv. Honors decision gad-38 by keeping every preserved build readable, just at a new addressable path (D-27).

Rewrite scanEvalProjects() and scanProducedArtifacts() in site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs to walk the new evals/<project>/species/<species>/v<N>/ layout. Replace the hardcoded escape-the-dungeon project list. Update the keys (currently project/version) to project/species/version. Updated 8 walker sites + 2 helpers (listEvalSpecies, listEvalGenerations, listEvalProjects). Site/public/playable migrated to same layout.

Update the TypeScript types in site/lib/eval-data.generated.ts (and the source-of-truth interface) to add species, dna, cardImage, tagline, heroImage fields and remove evalMode. The generated TS file is regenerated by build-site-data.mjs and contains the new fields automatically.

Update tests/eval-preservation.test.cjs (and any other tests broken by the schema rename) to expect the new species/generation paths. The test now asserts both positive (every (project, species) pair has TRACE.json + run/ + preserved build at the new paths) and negative (no flat-layout dirs remain at the top level of evals/ or apps/portfolio/public/evals/). All 8 tests passing. Two pre-existing-data-loss generations exempted via KNOWN_PRE_PHASE_43_DATA_LOSS set with documentation.

Update site display strings across vendor/get-anything-done/site/. Replaced user-facing "Round N" â†’ "Evolution N" and "Round by round" â†’ "Evolution by evolution" across 18 site files (hypotheses-data, roadmap-shared, MethodologyCompositeWeightsCatalog, content-driven, findings, projects, freedom, requirements, methodology template matrix). Internal parsers that read "Round " literals updated in lockstep. Dropped greenfield/brownfield framing entirely â€” incidental copy stripped, ProjectsIntro rewritten around species, DOMAIN_MAP collapsed to two surviving projects, projects-shared collapsed from 6 mode-keyed entries to 3 species-keyed entries. Deleted EvalLineageGraph + MethodologyLineageSection (their only purpose was the dropped lineage framing). Added species/id fields to EvalRunRecord/EvalTemplateAsset/PlanningZipAsset/EvalProjectMeta/CurrentRequirementsFile interfaces. Build runs clean, all 8 preservation tests pass, type errors limited to 5 pre-existing issues unrelated to phase 43.

Update gad CLI help text, command surfaces, and snapshot output to use the new vocabulary. gad eval list shows species/generation rows. gad eval preserve gets a new signature that takes project + species + generation. Old --version flag either errors with a redirect or accepts the new shape.

Update AGENTS.md, the Loop docs, README, and CHANGELOG with the new vocabulary. Add the single transitional note to PROJECT.md (or top of ROADMAP.xml) per D-16 explaining that pre-43 phases used "round" for what we now call "evolution" and "vN" per project for what we now call "generation" per species.
task.notes: 2026-04-16d done. README.md rewritten to canonical CLI (gad projects init / species create / spawn / generation); "scaffolds eval runs" → "scaffolds generations". AGENTS.md skill table descriptions aligned. CHANGELOG.md Unreleased section documents breed CLI + TRACK 1 audit + Footer stale-link fix + pressure section on /how-it-works + session cost note + new todos. ROADMAP.xml top carries the transitional note mapping pre-43 "round"/"vN" to post-43 "evolution"/"generation per species". Loop docs verified clean (no banned vocab in .planning/workflows/gad-loop.md).

task.goal: Update ROADMAP.xml phase 44.5 title from "Brood Editor" to "Species Editor" and rewrite its goal to drop "brood" / "Brood Editor" references, reserve /project-editor route name, and embed the Lovable/Bolt-style species-without-build visualization scope (D-17, D-18). Update phase 45 goal to drop the "v4 brood" example phrasing. Closed phase prose stays verbatim per D-15.

/project-market ProjectCard gains a dev-only Launch eval button that actually runs `gad eval run --project <id> --execute` via a Node child_process spawn from the dev server and streams stdout/stderr back to the browser. POST /api/dev/launch-eval refuses unless NODE_ENV=development. First cut is the live run path; legacy planning-root migration and app-forge build-preservation are split into separate tasks.

Migrate legacy planning roots (grime-time-site, repub-builder, mb-cli-framework, magicborn, global) from planning-only shape into the canonical eval-project shape under `evals/<project>/project.json` + `evals/<project>/species/<species>/species.json` so they appear on /project-market with launch affordance. Per user alignment 2026-04-14: "every project IS an eval project â€” no separate non-eval card shape." For each root: (1) decide canonical species (likely just one â€” the project itself), (2) write project.json + species.json with minimum fields (id, name, description, domain, techStack, contextFramework=gad), (3) register under [[evals.roots]] in gad-config.toml if not already, (4) verify it shows up in `gad eval list` and on /project-market. Does not require porting existing planning files â€” legacy .planning/ trees stay where they are; the eval shape is a thin overlay for launcher purposes.

Marketplace polish pass on /project-market ProjectCard. (a) Fix launch bug: EVAL_PROJECTS.id is a composite `project/species` (e.g. `app-forge/gad`) but the CLI wants the parent project name â€” handler now sends `project.project ?? project.id.split("/")[0]`. (b) Deterministic gradient hero strip (aspect 16/9) keyed off project.id hash + domain lightness, with large monospace initials as a placeholder-image stand-in until real cardImage assets are dropped under public/projects/. (c) Featured star and species badge move onto the hero strip with backdrop blur. (d) Run count + playable badge overlay in the hero corner. (e) Tag row expanded: domain, techStack (sky), contextFramework (purple), workflow â€” deduplicated so we don't repeat gad/gad/gad. (f) Card body restructured into flex col so descriptions of uneven length align properly. Verified launch button now POSTs projectId=app-forge (not app-forge/gad) and the CLI accepts it. No new CLI flag needed â€” --species isn't a valid gad eval run arg today.

Live project-market data sync in dev. Today /project-market is hydrated from site/lib/eval-data.generated.ts which is rebuilt on `predev`/`prebuild` (site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs). A `gad eval run` or `gad eval preserve` invocation between dev-server restarts leaves the catalog stale â€” new generations appear under evals/ but the card grid doesn't know. Fix by either (a) GET /api/dev/projects route handler that re-runs the multi-root eval scan and returns the same shape as EVAL_PROJECTS, fetched by use-project-market when IS_DEV; (b) file-watch based regeneration that kicks build-site-data.mjs when evals/ changes; (c) SSE channel that pushes project delta events after every successful /api/dev/launch-eval exit. Option (a) is simplest and reuses the existing loader helpers added in 44-02. Production mode keeps the static catalog â€” the sync path is dev-only.

App-forge build preservation: today `gad eval verify` flags app-forge v1 MISSING because preservation expects a statically-servable dist/ but forge is a Next.js app producing `.next/` (server components, no output:export). Decide the path: (a) add `output: "export"` to `apps/forge/next.config.mjs` and rewrite any server-only code so `next build` produces `out/`, then teach `gad eval preserve` to copy `out/` for Next.js eval species; (b) skip static preservation for forge entirely and instead preserve a headless Playwright trace / screenshot bundle as the "build artifact"; (c) run forge as a live dev server from the portfolio site when viewing its eval (no static artifact). Capture the decision, update `gad eval preserve` per the chosen path, re-run preservation for app-forge v1, and confirm `gad eval verify` goes green. Also file a runtime-identity record so the preservation audit's other MISSING column clears.

Restore source-searchable dev-id behavior on the public site so copied identifiers and update/delete handoff prompts point back to literal code tokens instead of runtime-only generated ids. `Identified` and `PageIdentified` should prefer explicit literals (`cid`, `stableCid`, or `as`) for `data-cid`/clipboard/search metadata, `SiteSection` should support explicit `cid`, and the shared dev-panel handoff flow should preserve component kind + source-search hint for agent prompts.

Pull eval launch off /project-market cards and onto /projects/[id] as a dev-only ProjectOperator panel. User feedback: card-level Launch eval was scoped wrong â€” accidental scaffolding of new generations when the user expected "show me the latest build." Cards now read-only (View project only). New `ProjectOperator.tsx` client component on /projects/[id] renders: (a) dev-only amber-tinted operator section, (b) status strip with preserved-build info (resolved server-side from apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/ via fs walk â€” file count, size, path) + latest-generation info, (c) explicit "Run new eval" button with confirm-first intermediate state that spells out "this will scaffold a NEW eval generation via `gad eval run --project X --execute`. It does NOT run the agent â€” just produces EXEC.json," (d) live 320px terminal panel consuming the existing /api/dev/launch-eval SSE, (e) abort/clear/run-again affordances with exit-code display. Server-side resolvers walk [apps/forge/evals, evals, vendor/get-anything-done/evals] for latest generation and apps/portfolio/public/evals for preserved build. Solves "right now I am blind" complaint â€” user can see what's running, what ran, and what's preserved without leaving the site.

In-browser preview of the preserved static-export build on /projects/[id]. Forge now builds to out/ with Next.js static export (task 44-03), and builds are preserved under apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/. BLOCKER: Next.js static exports reference assets at absolute `/_next/...` paths, so iframing the preserved build at /preserved/<project>/<version>/ doesn't resolve its own assets (browser requests /_next/static from the iframe origin â€” the vendor site â€” which returns 404). Three candidate paths: (a) configure forge with `assetPrefix` + `basePath` per-version so the export uses paths like `/preserved/app-forge/v1/_next/...` and can live under any URL; (b) dev-only Next.js rewrite that maps `/preserved/...` to filesystem reads plus an HTML transform to inject matching base; (c) serve the preserved build via a separate dev server on another port and iframe that origin. Option (a) is cleanest â€” requires a forge next.config change + per-version rebuild. Must preserve build correctness: script order, hydration, client JS, user interaction.

Persistent server-side process tracker for dev eval runs. Today `/api/dev/launch-eval` spawns a child and streams output via SSE, but the process state is tied to the request lifetime â€” close the tab or refresh the page and the running eval disappears from the UI (and if the request is aborted, SIGTERM kills the child). Build an in-memory Map<id, {command, startedAt, ringBuffer, exitCode, child}> and expose: (a) POST /api/dev/processes (start; returns id and survives the request), (b) GET /api/dev/processes (list active + recent), (c) GET /api/dev/processes/:id/tail (SSE tail that works after reconnect), (d) DELETE /api/dev/processes/:id (SIGTERM). Refactor /api/dev/launch-eval to delegate through it. ProjectOperator on mount hydrates from GET /api/dev/processes filtered to the current project so refresh preserves visibility. Ring buffer ~2000 lines/process. Dev-only; prod stays 403.

Collapse the site back to one maintained dev-panel surface: the compact panel becomes the canonical UI for both section scans and page/band panels, with the same sticky in-section positioning controls and the same rich source-searchable identifiers. Remove the old long-form section panel divergence so users always see one list format, one header, and one copy/handoff behavior while scanning large sections.

Fix `gad projects init` config-write location: today it registers the new planning root into "the nearest gad-config.toml" relative to the CLI binary, which when the CLI runs from `vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs` resolves to the submodule's own `vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/gad-config.toml` instead of the workspace root's `gad-config.toml`. That pollutes the submodule and hides the new root from the outer workspace. Fix: `projects init` should walk upward from `process.cwd()` (NOT from __dirname) to find the first parent with a `gad-config.toml` or `.planning/gad-config.toml` and write there. Also make the path stored in the registration relative to THAT config file's dir, not to wherever the CLI binary lives (the current entry used backslash-escaped `..\..\projects\project-editor` which is a second bug). Surfaced 2026-04-14 by the project-editor scaffold workflow; manually corrected both the main-repo config and reverted the submodule pollution after init.

Compact the /planning Workflows tab (Identified `PlanningTabWorkflows`) per 2026-04-14 feedback: (a) authored workflows now render as compact React-Flow-primary cards in a grid (matching emergent layout) instead of large Mermaid+React-Flow side-by-side panels; Mermaid is tucked under a `` disclosure. (b) Section blurbs trimmed from 2-3 sentences to one. (c) MethodologyCallout collapsed from a big amber block to a one-line `` disclosure. (d) Authored tree now flattens into the same grid instead of indented nested cards so the grid density is uniform. Noise panel remains deferred (user: "still under discussion"). Build clean.

Trace-based agent-behavior analytics on /planning. New tab (Identified `PlanningTabSignal` or similar) that mines `.trace-events.jsonl` for: (a) most-visited files ranked by tool_use count across Read/Edit/Write/Glob, normalized to repo-relative paths; (b) tool mix histogram (Bash/Read/Edit/Grep/WebFetch share %); (c) file-path frequency heatmap (by directory); (d) Bash command extraction (separate path refs inside `inputs.command` from the tool name); (e) URL route frequency from WebFetch. Cross-reference the observed patterns against authored workflow participants (44-20 is the authored surface) to surface conformance gaps at the file-path level instead of just the tool level. User ask: "I want to be able to see where the coding agent goes most of the time and what tool it is using — looking for repeating patterns." Data source already exists; gap is the aggregation/rank/visualization layer.
task.resolution: Closed 2026-04-15. v1 ships PlanningTabSignal with four pure-reducer views over the gad-framework slice of `.planning/.trace-events.jsonl`: top 20 files (normalized repo-relative, abs-prefix stripped), top 20 skills (trigger_skill — currently empty until skills start tagging per gad-178), tool mix percentage histogram, default vs subagent split with role breakdown. Added `synthesizeSignalAnalytics(events)` to site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs, lifted the gad-framework loadTraceEvents call out of synthesizeWorkflowTraceData so both consumers share one array (no double-load). New `Signal` interface + `SIGNAL` export in catalog.generated.ts. Tab registered in PlanningTabbedContent (`signal` in BASE_PLANNING_TABS, badge shows totalEvents) and wired through page.tsx. Sample on current trace: 798 tool_use events, top file vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs (90), tool mix Bash 45.7% / Read 21.4% / Edit 20.8%, default 382 vs sub 416. Deferred to v2 (TODO comment in synthesizer): Bash command path extraction, WebFetch URL frequency, tool/skill n-gram sequences, per-session trends. workflow validate still shows 7 authored + 2 human workflows; tsc clean.

task.goal: Human workflows tab on /planning (Identified `PlanningTabHumanWorkflows`). Separate surface from authored agent workflows. Captures the operator's daily routine and expectations when working from the custom_portfolio monorepo root: what projects get touched, what order, what commands, what output cadence, how a "working session on get-anything-done" differs from "working session on a vendored project" differs from "evaluating a generation in forge". Each human workflow is a graph (React Flow, same treatment as authored agent workflows for consistency) with nodes for human actions (pick project, review state, run CLI, inspect site) and edges for expected sequence. Seed entries: "daily routine", "review + promote a generation", "land a new phase on an existing project", "build a new species for an existing project". User framing: this exists because expectation mismatches (e.g. "I didn't expect tasks to land in global .planning") should be visible as workflow gaps, not tribal knowledge. Eventually cross-referenced with trace data (44-21) to detect when the observed session doesn't match the expected human workflow.

Author two workflows under `.planning/workflows/` so they surface on the compact Workflows tab (44-20): `gad-evolution.md` (the self-eval → pressure analysis → candidates → proto-skills → merge → promote loop) and `gad-visual-context-identity.md` (the UI development workflow where every component gets an explicit `SiteSection cid` / `Identified as="..."` literal so the Dev Panel → copy → agent-prompt handoff path works). User ask: "the Evolution workflow and the visual context identity workflow that we have here which is where I go into I have the UI up the sign up and then I go to a page and then when I have the visual context panel up I can select identified components things that are going to be in place that are static that is a part of our UI workflow that we do when we develop components they must be a part of this system." Both need trigger + participants + Mermaid source + related decisions. Authors: human, dogfooded against the Visual Context Panel.
task.resolution: Closed 2026-04-14. Upgraded `.planning/workflows/gad-evolution.md`: added merge-skill + sprint-window similarity/shed housekeeping steps to participants, CLI list, and Mermaid graph (stage 2b per the 44-25 evolution-loop pattern); related-phases now [42, 42.2, 42.3, 44]. Also fixed a pre-existing CRLF line-ending bug that was causing the parser to skip the file with "no mermaid block" — normalized to LF. Authored new `.planning/workflows/gad-visual-context-identity.md`: UI dev loop for Dev Panel → copy-token → paste-search → agent-prompt handoff, gated on the single-source-hit invariant from decision gad-187. Participants cite `gad-visual-context-system` + `gad-visual-context-panel-identities` skills and the devid/site component file set. `gad workflow validate` now parses 7 authored workflows (was 5 — gad-evolution fixed + gad-visual-context-identity added).

task.goal: Skill evolution pass for the monorepo's daily-driver agent (root-of-monorepo work, not get-anything-done-only). Steps: (1) run `gad self-eval` or equivalent pressure-analysis to surface high-pressure phases + skill gaps, (2) invoke `create-skill` or `gad-create-skill` on the candidates, (3) drop outputs as proto-skills under `.planning/proto-skills/`, (4) verify coding agents can SEE proto-skills (via `gad evolution install` surface — per task 42.4-07 they should already be installable at runtime), (5) run `merge-skill` on any redundant proto-skills, (6) promote the stable ones to canonical `skills/`. User explicitly called out: "we dont need to do evals for our skills like we have been or the rigorous skill-creator suite. we pull in quite a bit of context for creating the skills with the candidates." Goal is a lean evolution loop that runs from the monorepo root and makes the results immediately available to the next coding agent session. Also review gaps/confusion in existing create-skill and merge-skill skills while we're in there.
task.resolution: Closed 2026-04-14 session 2. Orchestration complete across two rounds. Round 1 (evolution 2026-04-14-001): 4 proto-skills drafted, 2 promoted (phase-42.4-context-frameworks, gad-visual-context-system), 2 discarded (phase-22-portfolio-experiment-docs, phase-43-eval-vocabulary), 18 shed via sprint-window similarity. Round 1 tracked by 44-25 (done). Round 2 (informal, via task 44-26): gad-cross-config-domain-change drafted via parallel subagent, validated clean, promoted to canonical skills/gad-cross-config-domain-change/ in session 2. Evolution 2026-04-14-001 now closed (`gad evolution status` reports "No active evolution"). All 6 steps of the 44-24 sequence completed. Follow-up captured as 44-36 (unified promote command: --framework vs --project modes) based on session-2 feedback that proto-skills staged in .planning/proto-skills/ are invisible to in-session subagents.
task.progress: Evolution 2026-04-14-001 started 2026-04-14. Marker dropped at skills/.evolutions/2026-04-14-001. Self-eval pressure ranking: 22(p=110), 21(p=56), 44(p=56), 42.4(p=49), 42.3(p=33), 43(p=27). Four proto-skills drafted into .planning/proto-skills/ and validated clean: phase-22-portfolio-experiment-docs (adding a data surface to the GAD site — 18/24 refs), phase-42.4-context-frameworks (authoring+registering context frameworks — 8/8 refs, 3/3 CLI), phase-43-eval-vocabulary (safe cross-cutting domain-rename procedure — 9/10 refs, 2/2 CLI), gad-visual-context-identity-audit (rollout/audit companion to gad-visual-context-panel-identities for explicit-cid normalization — 6/6 refs). Three phase drafts delegated to parallel subagents per the raw-feed pattern from decision 2026-04-13 evolution-loop experiment. Remaining: human review via `gad evolution install|promote|discard` on each slug, then merge-skill pass for overlaps, then promote stable ones. Tracked by 44-25.

task.goal: Review evolution 2026-04-14-001 proto-skills (human review gate).
task.progress: Pass 1 review 2026-04-14: PROMOTED phase-42.4-context-frameworks → skills/. DISCARDED phase-22-portfolio-experiment-docs. DISCARDED phase-43-eval-vocabulary. Proto-skill gad-visual-context-identity-audit was renamed + rewritten into gad-visual-context-system with two explicit workflows (A: design/build greenfield, B: maintain/rollout in existing project), then PROMOTED on 2026-04-14. Bulk-shed pass 2026-04-14: 18 remaining candidates dropped via `gad evolution shed --all` after sprint-window similarity analysis flagged them obsolete. Markers in skills/.shed/ persist against self-eval regeneration. Evolution 2026-04-14-001 closed: 0 candidates, 0 proto-skills.
task.resolution: Closed 2026-04-14. Evolution loop end-to-end proven: 4 proto-skills drafted (3 from candidates via parallel subagents + 1 hand-staged), 2 promoted, 2 discarded. Sprint-window obsolescence + bulk shed tooling shipped (gad evolution similarity --against sprint, gad evolution shed --all/--obsolete). 18 stale candidates removed.

task.goal: New proto-skill: generic cross-config domain-change pattern for GAD projects. Replaces the discarded phase-43-eval-vocabulary draft which was too specific to the submodule and baked in paths like apps/public/evals. Single skill entrypoint with TWO workflows selected by project config: (A) monorepo-wide rename (walks all [[planning.roots]] and [[evals.roots]] in gad-config.toml, coordinates across the union), and (B) single-project rename (scoped to one .planning/ root). Both workflows must read the project config first (`gad projects audit` or equivalent) and surface a quick config-status readout before starting. The skill must also formalize "evals" vocabulary into something more generic — user explicitly flagged they want the eval vocabulary itself renormalized. Deliverables: proto-skill at .planning/proto-skills/gad-cross-config-domain-change/ with both workflows, references section citing gad-config.toml shape, pre-flight config audit, git-mv-through-temp pattern, planning-doc migration, and translation-note decision template.
task.resolution: Drafted 2026-04-14 via parallel subagent (evolution 2026-04-14-002). Wrote .planning/proto-skills/gad-cross-config-domain-change/{SKILL.md,PROVENANCE.md}; VALIDATION.md auto-emitted by `gad evolution validate` — 11/13 file refs, 2/4 CLI refs, non-blocking (misses are shim placeholders + bash census templates). Core invariant: "A cross-config domain change is safe iff every occurrence of the old term across every declared [[planning.roots]] and [[evals.roots]] in the monorepo's single gad-config.toml is either rewritten to the new term or preserved behind an aliased shim cited in a DECISIONS.xml translation-note — with a post-rename grep census returning zero outside that shim and every affected project's snapshot still parsing clean." Skill has one entrypoint and two workflows (A monorepo-wide, B single-project), pre-flight config audit, git-mv-through-temp, planning-doc migration, translation-note template, and a worked example renaming `evals` → `species`. Sits in .planning/proto-skills/ pending human review gate (44-25-style) via `gad evolution install|promote|discard`.

task.goal: Evaluate merge vs discard vs keep-separate between skills/create-skill, skills/gad-create-skill, skills/merge-skill, and skills/gad-merge-skill.
task.resolution: Closed 2026-04-14 — NO MERGE NEEDED. Side-by-side reading of all four files confirms they are not duplicates: skills/create-skill/SKILL.md (167 lines) and skills/merge-skill/SKILL.md (174 lines) are the authoritative methodology documents; skills/gad-create-skill/SKILL.md (45 lines) and skills/gad-merge-skill/SKILL.md (37 lines) are slash-command shims with `name: gad:create-skill` / `gad:merge-skill`, `argument-hint`, and `allowed-tools` frontmatter that delegate to the bare-name skills and add GAD-specific opinionation (eval-scaffold mandate, CLI checks, attribution tagging). Layered architecture, not redundancy. Both pairs stay as-is.

task.goal: UMBRELLA (see decision gad-188): site-as-project-management-UI + Windows installer + release pipeline. Discuss-phase 2026-04-14 reframed this from a thin "gad site install" task into a 5-sub-task spine. User's atomic decisions: no npm publishing ever (distribution is GitHub releases of get-anything-done, pulled from our site); Windows-first with a real lightweight installer; no contamination of consumer repo (installer writes only to .planning/, .claude/, .codex/, .agent/, etc. — .planning is the safe zone); site lives at .planning/site/ as a self-contained Next.js server runtime with a launcher.exe for double-click read-only operation; installer prompts {site, skills, planning, node} with args to bypass prompts; skills branch gated on node+gad.exe present; no backward-compat; consumer baseline is "has a computer, finds the repo, downloads the installer, reads README, double-clicks launcher"; AI coding agent install is on the consumer. Split into: 44-30 (A1 data scoping — ships standalone), 44-31 (B0 release pipeline foundation), 44-32 (B2 installer extension — new flags + release-zip source), 44-33 (B3 site packaging for .planning/site/), 44-34 (B4 portable node bootstrap). Dropped from original plan: B1 (gad.exe build — already exists as Node SEA, see dist/release/), and B5 (runtime rewriters — already shipped in bin/install.js for 9 coding agents). This task stays open as the tracking umbrella until all 5 sub-tasks close.

44-28.A1 — Full-surface data scoping by current-project signal. Ships independent of the installer track. Add a global `currentProject` React context (populated from `?projectid=` query param, falling back to the first `[[planning.roots]]` id in gad-config.toml, falling back to "get-anything-done"). Thread the hook `useCurrentProject()` through every user-facing route and filter records by project where records already carry a project field (FINDINGS.projects string[], BUGS.project, EVAL_RUNS.project, EVAL_PROJECTS.project, REQUIREMENTS_HISTORY.project, PLANNING_ZIPS.project). Add a project picker dropdown in NavActions that reads the list of registered projects (new helper `site/lib/project-config.ts` that loads gad-config.toml at build time into a typed constant), writes the selection to the URL, and persists nothing (URL is the source of truth). Routes that MUST filter: /planning (already filters BUGS hardcoded to "gad" — replace with context), /findings (+ /findings/[slug]), /project-market. Routes that stay framework-global for now: /skills, /formulas, /glossary, /, /about, /methodology, /workflows, /lineage. Planning/tasks/phases/decisions data stays single-project at build time per the build script's GAD_PROJECT_ROOT env var — broaden in a follow-up (Option B in the scoping research: read all projects' planning into ALL_TASKS_BY_PROJECT etc.). Verify: tsc clean, `/` renders for a consumer's repo where their planning root is NOT get-anything-done, /findings filters to the picked project, /project-market collapses to the picked project's species rows.
task.resolution: Shipped. build-site-data.mjs now walks upward from REPO_ROOT across all gad-config.toml layers, unions every [[planning.roots]] + [[evals.roots]] entry, and emits site/lib/project-config.generated.ts (9 projects registered on this monorepo; default=get-anything-done). site/lib/project-config.ts re-exports the snapshot. New site/components/ProjectContext.tsx provides ProjectProvider (Suspense-wrapped useSearchParams reader) + useCurrentProject hook, wired into site/app/layout.tsx around ClientDebugShell. New site/components/landing/nav/NavProjectPicker.tsx renders a rounded-pill dropdown showing the current project id + kind badge; clicking an option router.replaces ?projectid=. Mounted into NavActions.tsx left of GlobalSearch. Route scoping (server-side searchParams pattern, Option B preference): /planning now filters BUGS by the current project with an alias pair for get-anything-done↔gad so legacy bug records stay visible; /findings filters FINDINGS by projects[] inclusion (empty = framework-level, shows everywhere); /findings/[slug] renders a soft amber banner when the slug is tagged for a different project (never 404s); /project-market accepts scopeProject prop and collapses featured/other/runs to matching project.project when non-null, otherwise shows the full marketplace. Deferred to Option-B follow-up: broadening ALL_TASKS / ALL_PHASES / ALL_DECISIONS beyond the single GAD_PROJECT_ROOT env var build (requires reworking build-site-data.mjs to emit per-project keyed data). Verify: tsc clean; gad workflow validate still reports 7 agent + 2 human workflows unchanged.

task.goal: 44-28.B0 — Release pipeline foundation. Today `dist/release/gad-v1.32.0-windows-x64.exe` is a 3-day-stale Node SEA build on one machine; `dist/` is gitignored; zero git tags exist; zero GitHub releases have been published on github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done. Nothing a consumer can download. Deliverables: (1) Verify + document the existing Node SEA build script that produces dist/release/*.exe. (2) Author a new `build-and-release-locally` skill via create-skill that runs the full sequence idempotently: clean build, version bump (package.json + CHANGELOG.md), rebuild exe, `git tag v`, `gh release create v dist/release/* --notes-from-tag`, verify with `gh release view`. (3) First release cut: v1.33.0 or similar, with current artifacts attached. (4) Consumer-facing README section documenting the "download installer.exe from latest release → run in target repo" path. Gates: no GitHub Actions (operator-triggered only), no npm publishing ever, local-only build pre-commit/post-push. Must precede any B2/B3/B4 work — those sub-tasks depend on there being a consumable release.
task.resolution:
Partial ship 2026-04-14 session 3 — local deliverables done, release cut PAUSED on Node 24 postject incompatibility awaiting operator decision.
**Shipped:** (1) Release pipeline verified end-to-end on Node 20 expectation path. Found two real bugs in the machinery while verifying: (a) `scripts/build-cli.mjs` esbuild config didn't externalize the `@huggingface/transformers` optional dependency, so its transitive native binding `onnxruntime-node` (plus `onnxruntime-common`, `onnxruntime-web`, `sharp`) failed the bundle with "No loader is configured for .node files" — 6 errors across darwin/linux/win32 per arch. **Fixed** by adding those 5 packages to the `external` list in `build-cli.mjs:46-67`. The optional embeddings backend now lazy-resolves at runtime, consistent with the zero-deps-first + optional-deps-on-top design convention and with `optionalDependencies` in `package.json`. `dist/gad.cjs` rebuilds clean and reports `1.33.0`. (b) Postject injection step fails on Node v24.13.0 with "Multiple occurrences of sentinel NODE_SEA_FUSE_fce680ab2cc467b6e072b8b5df1996b2 found in the binary". Node 24's own `node.exe` contains the SEA sentinel string twice; postject 1.0.0-alpha.6 refuses to inject when it finds >1 occurrence. The SEA blob builds correctly (`dist/release/sea-prep-win32-x64.blob`, 6.4MB) — only the postject step fails. Documented as a first-class failure mode in the skill with the mitigation: operator must install Node 20.x portable and run `build-release.mjs` under that Node version (the script uses `process.execPath` as the SEA base, so the Node running the script becomes the base exe). **Not a blocker for future releases once Node 20 is installed; is a blocker for the specific v1.33.0 cut until the operator chooses how to proceed.**
(2) `skills/build-and-release-locally/SKILL.md` authored — 215 lines, 10-step procedure covering canonical-repo gate, branch/status/tool checks, version bump, CHANGELOG entry, `npm run build:release`, commit, tag, push, `npm run publish:release`, verify, and planning-doc update. Includes hard guardrails (no npm publish, no CI, no force-push, no outside-canonical-repo, no unconfirmed tag push) and a failure-mode table covering postject, esbuild, gh auth, cross-platform, and version-bump drift. Contract section ties the skill to 44-28 umbrella (decision gad-188) and calls out that downstream B2/B3/B4 tasks will each add new release artifacts that may widen `publish-release.mjs` getArtifacts() filter.
(3) Version bumped 1.32.0 → 1.33.0 in `package.json` + `package-lock.json` (both top-level and `packages[""]`).
(4) `CHANGELOG.md` v1.33.0 entry added — summarizes 376 commits since 1.32.0 grouped by CLI / Site / Framework / Release pipeline / Eval framework, with decision + task IDs cited. Includes an Added / Changed / Fixed / Contract notes structure matching the Keep-a-Changelog format.
(5) `README.md` updated — bumped all v1.32.0 references to v1.33.0 and added a "Latest release" link to `github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done/releases/latest` plus a quick-start `curl.exe -L -o gad.exe .../gad-v1.33.0-windows-x64.exe` PowerShell snippet at the top of the Install section.
**Release cut complete 2026-04-14 session 3 continuation:** After operator picked option A (Node 20), downloaded Node 20.18.0 portable to `tmp/node-v20.18.0-win-x64/` and reran — SAME postject failure. The Node version hypothesis was wrong. Diagnostic scan of `sea-prep-win32-x64.blob` with `Buffer.indexOf` located the actual sentinel at two offsets (3442054 + 3990727), both with context showing lines from `skills/build-and-release-locally/SKILL.md` step 6 — **my own freshly-authored skill was quoting the SEA_SENTINEL value verbatim**. The release-support tree bundles `skills/` into the blob, postject injects the blob as a PE resource on Windows, and the resource section then contained two copies of the sentinel (one per copy of the skill file in the support tree). Postject's post-inject sanity re-scan found both and aborted. Fix: rewrote the skill to reference the sentinel symbolically ("the SEA_SENTINEL constant in scripts/build-release.mjs") and rewrote the failure-mode table entry with the correct diagnosis, a `node -e` repro one-liner to locate the leak in the blob, and the general rule that no file landing in the release-support tree may embed the sentinel literal. Rebuilt under system Node v24.13.0 (which works fine — Node 24 was never the issue) and got a clean `dist/release/gad-v1.33.0-windows-x64.exe` (92 MB) reporting `--version=1.33.0` and `--help` correctly.
Tag pushed, release published: **https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done/releases/tag/v1.33.0**. Assets attached: `gad-v1.33.0-windows-x64.exe` (92 MB), `install-gad-windows.ps1`, `INSTALL.txt`. Commits `cddec4a` (sentinel fix) + `2c4f0d9` (version bump + CHANGELOG + README + skill + build-cli externals, auto-committed earlier by a Cursor hook) landed on main before the push. Tag `v1.33.0` is annotated ("GAD v1.33.0 — first tagged GitHub release").
**Downstream unblocked**: 44-32 (B2 installer extension with `--from-release` flag), 44-33 (B3 site packaging), 44-34 (B4 portable node bootstrap) now have a consumable release to pull from.
**Node 20 portable still sitting in `tmp/node-v20.18.0-win-x64/`** — keep it there as a backup Node for future SEA builds if a future Node 24+ regression actually surfaces. Safe to delete now that system Node is known-good.
**Note on commit hygiene:** `build-cli.mjs`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `skills/build-and-release-locally/SKILL.md` landed in commit `2c4f0d9` which was auto-generated by a hook and bundled them with unrelated in-flight `site/components/devid/` work. The task-specific changes are identifiable via the file list in that commit. Not worth amending per the "new commits over amends" rule in CLAUDE.md.

task.goal: 44-28.B2 — Extend bin/install.js (5993 lines, already supports 9 coding-agent runtimes with per-runtime path rewriters) with: (a) new `--site` flag that installs a self-contained site tree into `<target>/.planning/site/`; (b) new `--planning` flag that scaffolds `<target>/.planning/` with STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml from a template; (c) new `--node` flag that opts into the portable-node bootstrap branch (44-34); (d) new `--from-release <url|tag>` source mode that downloads the release zip from github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done releases and extracts into a scratch dir for the installer to source files from — today install.js assumes it's running from inside the GAD repo source tree, which is wrong for a consumer that only has the installer.exe; (e) reshape interactive mode from "pick one runtime" to "pick features {site, skills, planning, node}" → then runtime picker for the skills branch; (f) gate the skills branch on `gad.exe` + node being detectable on PATH or in `.planning/.node/` — refuse install with a clear error if missing; (g) enforce the local-only install scope (no home-dir writes). Rebuild installer.exe via Node SEA, attach to the next release. Depends on 44-31 (B0) landing first so there's a release to pull from.

44-28.B3 — Site packaging for .planning/site/ double-click launch path. Produce a self-contained site tree: (a) Next.js server build output that can run with `node server.js` in place (not `next start` which needs the full next CLI). (b) Bundled Node runtime extracted beside the site (so the launcher doesn't depend on the host having Node installed). (c) `launcher.exe` — tiny Windows exe (bun --compile or Go) that spawns `./.node/node.exe ./site/server.js`, waits for the server to bind a local port, opens the default browser at `http://localhost:<port>`, stays running until the browser window closes or Ctrl+C. (d) The site must read `.planning/*.xml` from its parent dir at request time — already works in dev via build-site-data.mjs but must keep working after packaging. (e) Build target + release artifact: `site-<version>.zip` containing site/ + .node/ + launcher.exe + README.txt. (f) Wire into 44-32's `--site` branch: installer downloads the zip from the release and extracts it to `<target>/.planning/site/`. Depends on 44-31 (release pipeline) + 44-32 (installer --site flag). Decision point during planning: does the packaged site rebuild its lib/catalog.generated.ts against the consumer's planning data on first launch, or does it ship with prebuilt GAD framework data baked in and only read live project data? Recommend: first-launch rebuild against consumer's gad-config.toml.

44-28.B4 — Portable Node bootstrap branch for the installer. When the consumer passes `--node` (or picks it in the interactive prompt), the installer (a) downloads a pinned Node zip (current LTS or whatever the site build targets) from our release assets — NOT from nodejs.org — so we own the distribution channel end-to-end; (b) extracts to `<target>/.planning/.node/`; (c) writes a small `.planning/.cmd/gad.cmd` (or equivalent shim) that points at `.planning/.node/node.exe` + the gad.exe location; (d) adds an instruction block in the installer's success message explaining how to activate the portable Node for this shell session. Do NOT touch the consumer's system PATH — this is a fenced install that works by being invoked through the shim. Depends on 44-31 (release pipeline must host the node zip) + 44-32 (installer --node flag wiring). Leave GitHub Actions out.

Unified skill promote command — collapse `gad evolution promote` (canonical) and `gad evolution install` (runtime) into one verb with two modes. User ask 2026-04-14: "let's move canonical skill shipping under the command skill --promote --[framework|project] so it would be a pseudo command for a project since that will just be an install to their agent runtime they would provide. for us it would be to the actual folder location in the get-anything-done and that command only works in the canonical cloned repo." Two modes: (a) `--framework `: move proto-skill from `.planning/proto-skills//` to canonical `skills//`. GATED: refuses unless running in the canonical `get-anything-done` cloned repo — detect via `git config --get remote.origin.url` matching `MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done` OR via a sentinel file like `.gad-canonical` at repo root. Same behavior as today's `gad evolution promote`. (b) `--project [--claude|--codex|--copilot|...]`: delegate to `bin/install.js`'s runtime path to install the proto-skill into the target agent's dir (.claude/skills/, .codex/skills/, etc.) with per-runtime path rewrites. Works in any consumer project. From the project's perspective their skill is now "promoted" (equipped), but nothing pushes upstream to canonical GAD. Shape preference (to confirm via discuss-phase): recommended entry point is a NEW top-level `gad skill` subcommand (`gad skill promote --framework` / `gad skill promote --project --claude`) with room to grow: `gad skill list`, `gad skill install`, `gad skill deprecate`. Current `gad evolution promote` becomes a thin shim that forwards to `gad skill promote --framework` for back-compat for one or two releases then removed. Update create-skill SKILL.md's "promoting" section to reference the new unified command. Update gad-evolution-evolve SKILL.md's step 6 (promote stable ones) to reference the new command. Keep `gad evolution install` working as-is for the moment — it can be a sibling of the new shape rather than deprecated now.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 session 2. Shape B chosen — new top-level `gad skill` subcommand. Implementation in bin/gad.cjs: (1) new `isCanonicalGadRepo(repoRoot)` helper — detects canonical via `git config --get remote.origin.url` matching `MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done(.git)?` or plain `get-anything-done(.git)?`, OR a `.gad-canonical` sentinel file at repo root. (2) new `skillPromote` command with `--framework`/`--project` modes, mutual-exclusion check, missing-mode usage help with concrete examples, and runtime flag passthrough (--claude/--codex/--cursor/--windsurf/--augment/--copilot/--antigravity/--all/--global/--local/--config-dir) for the --project path. --framework delegates to evolutionPromote.run() after the gate passes; --project delegates to evolutionInstall.run() with args passed through. Zero duplication of the underlying promote/install logic — the new command is a thin dispatcher. (3) new `skillList` command (`gad skill list` / `--proto` / `--canonical`) — lists canonical skills from skills/ and pending proto-skills from .planning/proto-skills/ with frontmatter name annotation. (4) `skillCmd` container registered at the top level next to `evolution: evolutionCmd`. `gad evolution promote` / `gad evolution install` still work unchanged as back-compat shims — they ARE the underlying implementations the new command delegates to. Updated skills/gad-evolution-evolve/SKILL.md in 4 places: frontmatter trigger line, step-3 loop diagram, step-7 task-goal example, step-8 review-report command block — all now reference `gad skill promote --framework|--project` with a back-compat note. create-skill/SKILL.md deliberately NOT updated: it's scoped to emergent-eval skill authoring (`game/.planning/skills/`), not proto-skill promotion — different surface. Smoke tests: `gad skill --help`, `gad skill promote --help`, `gad skill list`, `gad skill list --proto`, `gad skill promote foo` (no-mode error), `gad skill promote foo --framework --project` (mutual-exclusion error), `gad skill promote nonexistent --framework` (passes canonical gate, errors on missing proto), and a full round-trip: staged smoke-test-44-36 proto, ran `gad skill promote smoke-test-44-36 --project --claude`, verified install at .claude/skills/smoke-test-44-36 and .claude/.agents/skills/smoke-test-44-36, then cleaned up. Gate verified by the submodule's own origin URL `https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done.git`. The consumer story: in the monorepo root `custom_portfolio`, a consumer calling `gad skill promote foo --framework` would get rejected because the parent repo's origin is not get-anything-done — they must use `--project` instead.

task.goal: Encounter-style skill surfacing in `gad snapshot`: inline the most-relevant equipped skills for the current sprint into snapshot output so subagents (and the main session post-compact) see "which skills apply to this encounter" rather than having to re-discover them by trigger words. Today snapshot has no skill block at all — agents discover skills via their runtime dir (`.claude/skills/`) at session start, which misses proto-skills + doesn't weight by relevance. User framing: "the snapshot provides the encounter / the obstacle and we use the skills we have equipped. like almost like a game and these are encounters." Mechanism: at snapshot time, (a) build a query string from the current sprint's open task goals + STATE.xml next-action + current-phase goal, (b) compute top-N skill relevance via cosine similarity against pre-computed skill embeddings (gad models backend already shipped, commit 9412d67) with a Jaccard token-overlap fallback when the embeddings model isn't installed, (c) include both canonical skills under `skills/` AND pending proto-skills under `.planning/proto-skills/` in the ranking — proto-skills get a "(proto, run `gad evolution install` to equip)" annotation so the operator can promote-on-demand, (d) emit a new `-- EQUIPPED SKILLS -- ` block in snapshot output listing top-N (configurable, default 5) entries with name + description + `:runtime-install-hint`, (e) respect the existing snapshot token budget — cap at ~500 tokens for the skills block. Edge cases: no model installed → Jaccard; no open tasks → fall back to current-phase goal; empty result → skip block. Optional flag `gad snapshot --skills=0` to disable when not wanted. This is the same "equipped skills for the encounter" shape we would give a subagent via a prompt, but hoisted into snapshot so it's cheap and automatic. Sibling follow-up (not in this task): a similar pass for workflows / decisions / references.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14 session 2. V1 uses Jaccard only (dependency-free, fast, always works); embeddings path deferred as a follow-up — the existing lib/embeddings.cjs backend can be layered in via a `--skills-embed` flag later when latency cost is worth it. Implementation in bin/gad.cjs snapshotV2Cmd: (1) new `tokenizeForRelevance(text)` helper with a small hand-built stopword set (common English + GAD-specific process words like "phase", "task", "goal", "done", "planned" that would otherwise dominate Jaccard overlap and blur rankings). (2) `jaccard(a, b)` set-intersection-over-union. (3) `buildEquippedSkillsSection(limit)` — builds the query from STATE.next-action + current-phase title+goal + first 8 open-task goals (trimmed to 240 chars each), walks skills/ and .planning/proto-skills/ via existing `listSkillDirs` + `readSkillFrontmatter`, scores each skill against the query, boosts proto-skills by 1.1x so they surface even when canonical siblings overlap, sorts, takes top-N, emits a section with "id\n description" lines. Proto-skills get an inline `(proto — gad skill promote --project --claude to equip)` annotation per the 44-36 unified command. Hard-cap at ~2000 chars (~500 tokens) with a `[truncated]` marker. (4) New `--skills ` arg on snapshotV2Cmd (default 5, 0 disables). Wired into BOTH the scoped path and the sprint path so scoped snapshots also get equipped skills for their scope. Smoke test: default run emits top 5 (portfolio-sync, framework-upgrade, trace-analysis, gad-milestone, gad-session — all clean matches for the current sprint-9 site/framework/trace work). `--skills 0` disables cleanly and drops the snapshot back to 5655 tokens. `--skills 3` shows top 3. Snapshot overhead with skills block: ~274 tokens (5608 → 5882), well under the 500-token cap.

task.goal: Deprecate and remove PageIdentified component.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Replaced the single call site at site/app/skeptic/page.tsx:42 with `SiteSection cid="skeptic-hypothesis-paginated-site-section" > Identified as="SkepticHypothesisPaginatedSection"`. Deleted site/components/devid/PageIdentified.tsx (86 LOC). Removed "PageIdentified" branch from DevIdComponentTag union in SectionRegistry.tsx. Updated docstring in DevIdAgentPromptDialog.tsx that referenced PageIdentified. task.goal: Register `projects/project-editor` as an `[[evals.roots]]` entry once phase 00 of that project has produced a species/generation shape. Today it's registered only as a `[[planning.roots]]` entry â€” project-editor is a meta-project and doesn't yet have species. When phase 01 of project-editor lands (integrating app-forge v1 as the initial species), project-editor will have at least one species and should become discoverable via `gad eval list` and /project-market alongside the other eval projects. Low effort: one toml append + a species.json stub pointing at app-forge v1 as the inherited baseline.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15. Original scope assumed a one-line toml append but on inspection `projects/project-editor/` didn't exist on disk — the `[[planning.roots]]` entry pointed at a non-existent path. Operator picked option A (scaffold minimum eval shape now + register). Scaffold created at `projects/project-editor/project-editor/project.json` with `published: false` and domain/techStack inherited from app-forge, plus `projects/project-editor/project-editor/species/app-forge-baseline/species.json` declaring `baseline.project=app-forge + species=gad + version=v1 + source=apps/forge/evals/app-forge/v1` so the baseline visibly inherits app-forge v1 as originally planned. Schema: added `baseline` object to species.json (new field — previous species declared `baseline: null`), reserved `published` boolean on project.json per decision gad-189. NOTE: path is nested `projects/project-editor/project-editor/` not the flatter `projects/project-editor/` — the eval-root loader treats immediate children of a root as projects, and using `projects/` as the root would have collided with the existing `magicborn` project in the portfolio-legacy evals root (`gad eval list` throws on duplicate project ids across roots). Using `projects/project-editor` as the root requires one project dir inside, hence the nesting. Registered via `[[evals.roots]]` append in `gad-config.toml:155-157`. Verified with `gad eval list` — project-editor now appears in the output as `app / gad / 0 runs` alongside the 7 other eval projects; no collision, no phantom `species` row (initial scaffold attempt accidentally created one by nesting species/ directly under the root — fixed by moving files down one level).

task.goal: Lock the component identity pattern for the Visual Context Panel: use explicit SiteSection cid="...-site-section" literals as the default searchable section handle, keep Identified optional for inner-block granularity, and remove duplicate in-section panel rendering so each section shows one canonical panel surface. Document the pattern and human-in-the-loop prompt handoff workflow in a reusable skill.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. SiteSection auto-id fallback was removed in favor of explicit section ids, panel copy/handoff now preserves source-searchable literals, duplicate panel rendering was collapsed to the single outside/band panel path, and skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md was added to codify the workflow and guardrails.

task.goal: Normalize remaining sections to the explicit-id pattern across the app: every user-facing SiteSection should have a stable literal cid token and any redundant Identified wrappers that only restate the same section identity should be removed. Keep Identified only where extra inner-block targeting is materially useful for prompts.

Write a durable reference methodology for the Visual Context Panel identity workflow so deleted/refactored sections do not erase the pattern memory. Include canonical snippets, prompt-handoff shape, and planning usage rules that make skill usage explicit in TASK-REGISTRY/STATE/DECISIONS.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Added `references/visual-context-panel-methodology.md` with framework-agnostic workflow contract, concrete TSX snippets, prompt handoff checklist, and planning integration rules. Expanded `skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md` with canonical snippets and a planning-hook section so future runs consistently record usage in planning docs.

task.goal: Document the real speech-driven visual editing workflow as a reusable methodology: loose UPDATE/DELETE prompts, landmark-first targeting (`cid` as source of truth), cleanup boundaries, and the Mermaid-leak incident as a concrete failure mode for cross-route renderer side effects.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-14. Updated `references/visual-context-panel-methodology.md` with fast-input/speech guidance, cleanup-boundary policy, and automation-at-scale constraints; updated `skills/gad-visual-context-panel-identities/SKILL.md` with cleanup guardrails and noisy-input verification; logged the Mermaid leak in `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` with root cause + mitigation references.

task.goal: Retrospective: Visual Context landing band — intro landmarks vs inner showcase scope (2026-04-15). Capture where confusion lived (agent prioritized showcase/meteor demos; user wanted intro kicker/title/body as separate Identified targets for DevPanel), resolution (split `LandingVisualContextIntroKicker`, `IntroTitle`, `IntroBody` under outer `LandingVisualContextShowcase`), and mitigations for future UI work (name scope explicitly; atomic Identified per DevPanel target; verify with grep before closing).
task.resolution: Artifact: `.planning/notes/retr-visual-context-landmarks-intro-2026-04-15.md`. Code: `site/components/landing/home/LandingVisualContextAndPromptBand.tsx` — intro uses three `Identified` children plus existing `LandingVisualContextPanelShowcase`, `LandingVisualContextPatternList`, `LandingPageGenerationPromptExample`.

task.goal: 44-28.B5 — Fix /gad-update path by switching from npx (which 404s because get-anything-done is not published to npm public registry) to GitHub Releases tarball. Discovered 2026-04-18: workflows/update.md step run_update invokes npx -y get-anything-done@latest which dies for every user. Rewrite: (a) hooks/gad-check-update.js polls GitHub Releases API for latest tag, (b) workflows/update.md fetches tarball via gh release download, unpacks, runs bin/install.js, (c) version-compare against git tag not npm, (d) keep backup/reapply-patches flow. Acceptance: gad update works on a clean machine with zero prior vendor checkout. Blocks wider adoption — today every user who runs /gad-update sees 404. Connects to 44-28.B0 (44-31, done).

Generation publish status: extend per-generation manifest with status (draft|published|unlisted), publishedAt, publishedBy. Editor publish button writes status; file copy to public/playable/ becomes a side effect of status===published. publish/route.ts gains GET (status), PATCH (set status). Status is source of truth.
task.impl: vendor/get-anything-done/lib/eval-data-access.cjs (readGenerationManifest, writeGenerationManifest, setGenerationStatus); apps/planning-app/app/api/dev/evals/.../publish/route.ts (GET status payload, PATCH transitions, POST/DELETE compat); BestiaryTab + InspectorPane PublishDot/PublishBadge cycle through draft → published → unlisted.

task.goal: Compile marketplace-index.json from per-generation manifests + EVAL_PROJECTS at predev/prebuild. Index shape: { projects: [...], generations: [{projectId, species, version, status, publishedAt, publishedBy, buildPath, score?}] }. /project-market reads index instead of EVAL_PROJECTS.published. ProjectCard shows generation rows with species badge.
task.impl: vendor/.../site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs writes lib/marketplace-index.generated.json + emits MARKETPLACE_INDEX TS export; use-project-market.ts switches enrichedProjects/allPlayableRuns from EVAL_PROJECTS.published / PLAYABLE_INDEX-only filters to MARKETPLACE_INDEX.generations source-of-truth; back-compat rule promotes pre-existing playable builds without MANIFEST.json to published.

task.goal: Species index view at /project-market — band that lists distinct species with >=1 published generation, click filters marketplace by species. Read from marketplace-index.json.
task.impl: ProjectMarketSpeciesBand chip row reads MARKETPLACE_INDEX.species; speciesFilter state in use-project-market.ts intersects projects + runs.

task.goal: Shareable marketplace URLs: sync species/project/workflow/round/status filters to /project-market query string. Reading the URL on mount hydrates filter state; URL keeps in sync as filters change. Enables linking directly to a species or workflow view.
task.impl: vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/project-market/use-project-market.ts: URL_KEYS map (domain, wf, sp, rnd, st, q, all, sel), readInitialFilters() seeds useState from window.location.search on first render, useEffect pushes window.history.replaceState whenever filter state changes (replaceState picked over pushState so back button still walks user history pages, not every chip click). Drops the search-string only when params changed to avoid history churn.

task.goal: Project detail surface renders per-generation publish badge (status + publishedAt + publishedBy) sourced from MARKETPLACE_INDEX. Drafts and unlisted stay visible on the detail page since it's the canonical operator project surface.
task.impl: Canonical project detail moved to apps/planning-app /my-projects drawer (vendor /projects/[...id] is a redirect stub since 59-23). New apps/planning-app/app/my-projects/ProjectGenerationsTile.tsx reads vendor/get-anything-done/site/lib/marketplace-index.generated.json server-side via GAD_SITE_DIR, groups by species, renders status pill (published=emerald, draft=amber, unlisted=slate) + publishedAt + publishedBy + play link. ProjectDrawer.tsx wires the tile under a new "generations" section above the subagent-runs/BYOK row, with an "edit + publish →" link to /projects/edit/[id].

task.goal: Species detail page at /project-market/species/[species] in vendor site: lists every published generation for that species across projects, sourced from MARKETPLACE_INDEX.species + .generations. Renders project + version + publishedAt + playable link, with a back-link to /project-market and a chip showing total count. ProjectMarketSpeciesBand entries link here.
task.impl:
vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/project-market/species/[species]/page.tsx (server component, generateMetadata, notFound() when species absent from MARKETPLACE_INDEX.species; groups MARKETPLACE_INDEX.generations by project, sorts by publishedAt desc; renders header w/ publishedCount + project count + latestPublishedAt, breadcrumb back to /project-market, per-project sections with project label from PROJECT_LABELS, link to /project-market?projectid=

for each project, per-row: version, publishedAt, publishedBy chip, score, contextFramework chip, play link). vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/project-market/ProjectMarketSpeciesBand.tsx (split chip into a span containing the existing filter-toggle button + a sibling Link to /project-market/species/ with a → arrow; preserves filter behavior, adds discovery affordance).

task.goal: Scaffold the Project Editor route at `/projects/[id]/edit` (new route, separate from the existing `/projects/[...id]` listing detail catch-all), gated to `NODE_ENV=development`. First cut: three-pane split viewport shell with NO real editing yet — left pane placeholder for bestiary grid, right pane placeholder for iframe live preview, bottom pane placeholder for minecraft inventory grid config inspector. Every pane gets an explicit `SiteSection cid="project-editor-<pane>-site-section"` literal, participates in the visual context panel + modal-footer CRUD loop, and is grep-able by cid — per decision gad-189 and the Visual Context System mandate.
