Renders against `projects/project-editor/project-editor/` as the dogfood target. Does NOT implement edit affordances or the dev bridge — this task is the shell only. Must include a visible "Project Editor (dev mode)" dev-panel badge so the operator can see the gate status. Acceptance: route renders in dev with empty panes + cids; `/projects/[...id]` listing detail unchanged; tsc clean; at least one prompt from the visual-context-modal-footer round-trips against a pane cid (proves the Visual Context System is wired before any editor feature work starts).

Visual Context System reference research for 44.5-01 (discuss-phase deliverable, not a code task). Before PLAN.md is written for 44.5-01, enumerate the exact set of existing Visual Context System components, skills, decisions, and file paths that the Project Editor route will consume: (a) the `SiteSection cid` pattern source (site/components/...), (b) the visual-context-panel component + its modal footer CRUD form, (c) the `gad-visual-context-panel-identities` skill, (d) decisions gad-186/187, (e) how the panel participates in portal modals (decision point from phase 42 work), (f) how dev-id band scan discovers new sections, (g) the global search hook that indexes cids, (h) any missing pieces that would need to be built before the editor can meet the mandatory Visual Context coverage requirement. Produce a short reference index (not a full plan) used as the "references" section of 44.5-01's KICKOFF.md. This closes the user's "make sure what references will be needed for implementation is understood" instruction from 2026-04-15.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-15 as `.planning/research/44.5-01a-visual-context-system-references.md`. Operator flagged the two anchor tokens (`cid="visual-context-modal-footer"` + `stableCid="visual-context-panel"`) by name — both located in `site/components/devid/dev-modal-footer-constants.ts` and `site/components/devid/dev-panel-constants.ts` respectively, and applied via `DevIdModalContextFooter.tsx` (383 LOC) and `DevPanel.tsx` (833 LOC, 2-liter variant). Full reference index enumerates 20 VCS component files (~2558 LOC total) + 2 constants files + 2 canonical skills (gad-visual-context-system 213 LOC, gad-visual-context-panel-identities 115 LOC) + 4 decisions (gad-186/187/189/180-182) + 1 memory entry, plus a "what's already available vs what needs to be built" matrix for phase 44.5. Key operator clarification locked into the doc + updated memory: **clipboard is the universal CRUD transport** — UPDATE emits locked markdown prefix + editable body via `buildUpdateLockedPrefix`, DELETE emits fire-and-forget block via `buildDeletePrompt`, copy-cid emits raw searchHint, speech capture via `useDictatedPromptCopy` + `speechRecognition.ts`. VCS never invokes agents directly; operator always pastes into their chosen agent (Claude Code/Cursor/Codex/etc). Six new-infra items identified for phase 44.5 execution: (1-3) constants files for new editor chromes (inventory-grid modal, recipe-edit dialog, species-json field modal), (4) deterministic cid derivation helpers for inventory slots / bestiary cards / diff rows (tied to tasks 44.5-05/07/09), (5) iframe postMessage bridge for inner-build cid targeting in 44.5-10 (stretch), (6) VCS coverage regression test to prevent editor drift. Research doc is the "references" section input for the upcoming 44.5-01 KICKOFF.md — planning can now proceed with a complete picture of what exists vs what needs to be built.

task.goal: Draft/published model — add `published: false` boolean to `project.json` schema (default draft) + reserve `published` on `species.json` without enforcing yet. Update `/project-market` page component to filter `FEATURED_PROJECTS` / marketplace lists to `published === true`; add a "no published projects yet — browse drafts in the editor" empty state with a CTA that routes to the Project Editor. Update `build-site-data.mjs` or equivalent data generator to read the new field and surface it on every project record. `/projects/[...id]` listing detail and `gad eval list` remain unfiltered per decision gad-189. Operator-facing flip is a single checkbox in the editor (44.5-01c). Schema migration: touch every existing `project.json` to add `"published": false` explicitly — do not rely on missing-field-means-draft, because the difference between "not yet touched" and "explicitly draft" matters for later auditing. Acceptance: `/project-market` renders the empty state with the editor CTA until at least one project flips published.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Schema migration: 8 project.json files updated with explicit `"published": false`. build-site-data.mjs emits published field per species row. EvalProjectMeta interface gains `published: boolean`. Marketplace (use-project-market.ts) filters to published===true. Empty state CTA links to /projects/edit. eval-data-access.cjs createProject defaults published=false, listProjectSummaries includes published. eval-data-runtime.ts maps published field. Listing detail and CLI unfiltered.

task.goal: "Open in Editor" CTA on the existing `/projects/[...id]` listing detail page. Adds a single button/link in the listing header that routes to the new `/projects/[id]/edit` editor surface, visible only when `NODE_ENV=development` or the dev-panel equivalent flag. Button gets its own cid. Also adds the reverse nav — an "Exit to listing" link in the editor shell that routes back to the listing detail for the same project. Both links participate in the visual context panel.

Update `project-skills-scope-section-site-section` data source on the listing detail (`/projects/[...id]`). The panel stays in place but its current data source (project-level skill catalog) changes to brood/generation-aggregated skill usage pulled from the project's accumulated broods. The aggregation walks every species's generations under the project and sums skill invocations from each generation's preserved trace/attribution data. `build-site-data.mjs` gains a `projectBroodSkillAggregation` helper that produces the input record. Panel UI unchanged beyond the numbers — still source-searchable via the existing cid, still grep-able, still wired to the visual context panel. Acceptance: panel on the listing shows non-zero numbers for app-forge after aggregating app-forge v1's preserved trace, and shows zero + empty state for project-editor (baseline species inherits, no generations yet).

Dev-server command bridge: Node child-process bridge behind a dev-only API route that rejects all requests unless NODE_ENV=development. First cut is permissive: accepts any `gad *` and any `npx *` command so we can iterate on the editor surface without fighting the allow-list. Hardening (explicit allow-list, argument sanitization, per-command timeout tuning) is deferred to a follow-up task after the editor's happy path is proven. Stream stdout/stderr back to the browser via Server-Sent Events or similar. Explicitly rejects production requests at module load time so the wide-open dev surface can never ship in prod.

Hardening pass for the dev-server command bridge: after 44.5-02 proves the editor works, replace the permissive “any gad/npx” acceptance with an explicit allow-list (`gad evolution status|validate|promote|discard`, `gad snapshot`, `gad state`, `gad tasks`, `npx` commands scoped to the eval repo, `pnpm|npm|yarn` install/build/test), add argument sanitization, per-command timeout tuning, and structured error responses. Keep the NODE_ENV=development gate as the primary safety â€” the allow-list is defense in depth.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. UNSAFE_ARG_RE blocks shell metacharacters (;|&$`(){}\\><!”'\n\r\0). TIMEOUT_MAP: eval/evolution=120s, snapshot=60s, default=30s. Structured error SSE: {error, exitCode, stderr} on non-zero exit. species added to allowlist.

task.goal: Live-edit eval folder from the editor: file-system adapter that reads/writes requirements.md, gad.json, per-species overrides, and manifest entries for the currently-selected project, guarded by the same dev-only gate as 44.5-02. No schema validation in this task â€” focus on the round-trip (read file â†’ edit in browser â†’ write file â†’ trigger rebuild).
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Added /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/files route (GET read, PUT write). Allowlisted patterns: project.json, species/*/species.json, species/*/REQUIREMENTS.md (writable); generation TRACE/RUN/PROMPT/EXEC (read-only). Path validation prevents traversal. createRequire bypasses Turbopack.

task.goal: Species/population/generation/brood capture surfaces inside the editor: inspector panes showing the currently-edited project's species rows, each species's brood (accumulated generations), and the cross-species view for the same project. Reuses phase 44 marketplace components but flips affordances from browse to "active editing" with visible row-level mutation indicators. Every row gets a cid. Per decision gad-189 the vocabulary is locked — inspector pane labels read "Species", "Brood", "Generation", "Recipe" and must not drift.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. InspectorPane upgraded: project fields (name, domain, techStack, description, tagline) are inline-editable via EditableField component + PUT /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]. Species fields (workflow, description) editable via PUT .../species/[name]. REQUIREMENTS.md editor with create/edit/save via /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/files. BestiaryTab gains "+ species" create form (name + workflow selector, POST .../species). All wired through the data layer API routes.

task.goal: Minecraft-style inventory grid primitive for config editing. Reusable React component that renders an object (gad.json / species.json / recipe config) as a grid of slots where each slot represents one config key. Features: drag-and-drop between slots, typed slot variants (string/number/boolean/enum/nested-object → opens sub-grid), hover tooltip showing current value + source path, click-to-edit inline or modal editor for complex values, visual "crafting" animation when a recipe combination is recognized. Every slot has a deterministic cid derived from the config key path so slots are individually grep-able (`inventory-slot-gad-json-sprint-size-site-section`). Primary surface for 44.5-03 file-system round-trip. Prototype first in Storybook-equivalent isolation before wiring to real data.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. InventoryGrid onEdit wired in InspectorPane: scoringWeights saves to project.json scoring field via PUT /api/dev/evals/projects/[id], constraints saves to species.json via PUT .../species/[name]. Type coercion (coerceValue) parses numbers/booleans back from string input. setByPath/getByPath handle nested dot-path updates. Full raw species.json and project.json grids added to inspector with live save.

task.goal: Recipe CRUD surface inside the Project Editor. Recipes are reusable species templates owned by the project (per decision gad-189). This task: (a) define the `recipe.json` schema — name, description, base species slug, default gad.json overrides, list of required inputs, list of expected outputs; (b) store recipes at `projects/<project>/<project-editor-slug>/recipes/<recipe-slug>/recipe.json` alongside species/; (c) editor UI: list of recipes in the Project Editor shell, create/edit/delete buttons (each with a cid), a form backed by the inventory-grid primitive from 44.5-05; (d) "run recipe" action that produces a new species in the project's species/ directory and triggers an eval generation via the command bridge; (e) a recipe-level cid so the modal footer CRUD can target recipes. No inheritance-from-recipe runtime yet — first cut is "recipe authors a species, species runs normally".

Bestiary view — read-only UI for a project's accumulated broods. Renders as a grid of bestiary-entry cards, one per species, each card showing: species name, generation count, newest generation thumbnail, animated trait bars for the 6-8 key traits (44.5-08 dependency), click-to-expand inline panel showing all generations in the species's brood with a visual diff tree (44.5-09 dependency) highlighting which traits changed between adjacent generations. Card cids are deterministic on species slug; generation-row cids are deterministic on species+version. The bestiary is the primary left-pane in the editor's split viewport for 44.5-01. Acceptance: app-forge's bestiary shows one species (gad) with one generation (v1); project-editor's bestiary shows one species (app-forge-baseline) with its inherited-baseline badge and zero generations of its own.

Animated trait bar + radar chart primitives. (a) Trait bar: horizontal bar per trait, animates on mount from 0 to current value, color-coded by trait category, tooltip shows trait source (species.json field or derived metric). Reusable as both a standalone component and a row inside a bestiary card. (b) Radar chart: 6-8 axes showing the species's trait vector, with the ability to overlay multiple species for comparison. Prototype first — operator has not seen one yet and will give feedback after live. Both primitives get cids and a modal-footer-editable representation. Prototype in isolation before wiring to real species data.

Visual diff tree primitive. Renders a side-by-side (or overlaid) tree view of two config objects (typically generation N vs generation N+1) where changed leaves glow with a color gradient indicating change direction (+/- for numerics, added/removed/modified for strings/objects). Used inside bestiary-card expansion (44.5-07) to surface what changed between generations in a species's brood. Every diff-row has a cid derived from the config key path for grep-ability. Accepts arbitrary JSON — schema-agnostic by design so it works for gad.json, species.json, recipe.json, and future types.

iframe live-preview primitive for the editor's right pane. Takes a generation identifier (project/species/version) and renders the preserved static build from `apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/` in a sandboxed iframe. Includes: device-frame chrome selector (desktop/tablet/mobile), reload button, "open in new tab" link, URL path input for SPA routes, and a fallback screenshot when the build isn't live-servable. Gets a cid. Integrates with the Visual Context System such that the modal footer can target either the iframe wrapper or (via postMessage when the inner build opts in) the selected element inside the iframe. Primary right-pane content for 44.5-01's split viewport.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. PreviewFrame.tsx: device toggle (Desktop/Tablet 768px/Mobile 375px), toolbar (reload, new tab, URL display, close), load-error fallback, data-cid="project-editor-preview-frame-site-section". Wired into ProjectEditor renderCanvas() replacing both artifact preview and generation preview iframes.

task.goal: Scaffold the DNA Editor pane inside the Project Editor left-pane region (sibling to the Bestiary pane) using the `scaffold-visual-context-surface` skill promoted 2026-04-15. Follow its 6-step checklist: (1) dev gate via `NODE_ENV === 'development'`, (2) enumerate panes + cid assignments (pane shell `dna-editor-pane-site-section`, four state groups `dna-editor-state-{dna,expressed,mutation,shed}-site-section`, row prototype `dna-editor-gene-<slug>-site-section`), (3) emit cids as literal strings (no computed cids — include a prototype literal in a comment for the row family), (4) register identities with the Visual Context Panel, (5) round-trip one modal-footer CRUD prompt against a real cid BEFORE any feature wiring, (6) only then begin list rendering / action wiring. Ships with empty state groups — the list population is 44.5-12's scope. Dev-panel badge "DNA Editor (dev mode)" visible. First real application of the `scaffold-visual-context-surface` skill; success criterion is that the skill's checklist guides the scaffolding without re-derivation. Per decision gad-198.

DNA Editor list population — read the four gene states into the scaffolded 44.5-11 pane. **DNA**: enumerate `.claude/skills/` (or the per-runtime skills dir) in the current project, read SKILL.md frontmatter for name/description/status. **Expressed**: enumerate `.gad-try/<slug>/` sandboxes in cwd, read PROVENANCE.md for source/kind/staged_on and ENTRY.md for the handoff prompt. **Mutations**: enumerate `.planning/proto-skills/<slug>/`, read PROVENANCE status + SKILL.md name + VALIDATION.md verdict. **Shed**: enumerate shed-score data (when 42.2-33 battery output is available) and manually-flagged entries. Each row renders with state-appropriate metadata badges and a cid derived from the slug (literal-prototype comment for grep-ability). No actions wired yet — pure read + render. File-system adapter extends 44.5-03's round-trip primitive. Per decision gad-198.

DNA Editor action row wiring via the 44.5-02 dev bridge. Each gene state gets state-appropriate buttons that spawn the corresponding CLI command and stream output via SSE: **Try** (run `gad try <ref>` for a URL/slug/path input form), **Promote** (run `gad evolution promote <slug>` on a mutation), **Shed** (run `gad evolution shed <slug>` or `discard`), **Cleanup** (run `gad try cleanup <slug>` on an expressed trial), **Copy prompt** (read ENTRY.md + use the existing clipboard helper from 42.2-40.a), **Preview** (load the trial artifact into the right-pane iframe — 44.5-14's scope). Every button is a `SiteSection` with a deterministic cid (`dna-editor-action-<verb>-<slug>-site-section`). Depends on 44.5-11 (scaffold), 44.5-12 (list population), 44.5-02 (dev bridge). Per decision gad-198.

Iframe preview integration for DNA Editor trial artifacts. Extends the 44.5-10 iframe live-preview primitive to detect and render non-generation artifacts: auto-detect `graph.html` (graphify trials), generic `index.html` (codebase maps, game builds), or a SKILL.md-declared `outputs:` path. When an operator clicks Preview on an expressed trial, the right pane loads the detected artifact in the sandboxed iframe. Integrates with the Visual Context System — the iframe wrapper gets a cid derived from `<slug>-preview-iframe-site-section`. Falls back to a "no viewable artifact" placeholder when no HTML output is detected. Depends on 44.5-13 (Preview button) and 44.5-10 (iframe primitive). Per decision gad-198.

DNA Editor command+K shortcuts. Integrates with 42.2-43's command+K global search to add gene-lifecycle shortcuts: typing "try <ref>" triggers the same flow as the Try button; "promote <slug>" runs evolution promote; "shed <slug>" runs evolution shed; "preview <slug>" loads the trial artifact into the iframe. Autocompletes slug names from the current DNA Editor's enumerated genes. Depends on 44.5-13 (action wiring) and 42.2-43 (command+K infrastructure). Per decision gad-198.

VCS context panel dismiss/expand — add X dismiss button to band dev panels, localStorage-backed dismissed set, "Reset N dismissed" in status badge. Addresses panel noise on dense pages like the editor.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. DevIdProvider gains dismissedBandCids Set + toggleBandDismiss + resetDismissedBands. SiteSection gates BandDevPanel on !dismissed. DevPanel renders X button in band mode. DevIdStatusBadge shows reset control. localStorage key: vcs-dismissed-panels.

task.goal: VCS speech capture + landmark neutral prompt — add mic icon and landmark icon per panel list item row. Mic captures speech and injects "landmark identifier: {cid} --- {transcribed text}" as freeform prompt snippet. Landmark copies "landmark: {cid}" to clipboard. Neutral prompt with no CRUD verb for spatial/layout references.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. DevPanelListItem gains onLandmarkCopy + onMicToggle + listening props. DevPanel wires per-row dictation via rowDictationCidRef + third useDictatedPromptCopy instance. finalizeRowDictation builds landmark identifier snippet and submits via submitVcHandoffUpdateSnippet. Both section and band render paths wired.

task.goal: Editor pane height fix — left pane and inspector pane did not extend full viewport height. Switched from SiteSection wrappers to plain div elements with h-full + flex layout for proper height propagation in the flex container.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. ProjectEditor.tsx: left pane and right pane changed from SiteSection to plain divs with data-cid, h-full, flex flex-col, overflow-y-auto. Tab bar gets shrink-0, content area gets flex-1 overflow-y-auto.

task.goal: Build lib/eval-data-access.cjs — unified CRUD module for eval projects, species, and generations (decision gad-203). Filesystem-as-database: JSON files on disk are the database. Single shared Node module provides read (delegating to eval-loader.cjs for merge semantics) and write (create/update/archive for projects and species) operations. REST API routes at /api/dev/evals/projects/*, /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/*, and /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/[name]/generations. CLI commands: gad projects create/edit/archive, gad species list/create/edit/clone/archive. Editor server components rewired from static eval-data.generated.ts imports to runtime eval-data-access.cjs via eval-data-runtime.ts adapter. All type-safe, zero new type errors.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Files: lib/eval-data-access.cjs (unified CRUD, ~450 LOC), site/app/api/dev/evals/ (5 route files), site/app/projects/edit/eval-data-runtime.ts (server-side adapter mapping CRUD shapes to EvalProjectMeta/EvalRunRecord), bin/gad.cjs (projects create/edit/archive + species list/create/edit/clone/archive commands). Editor pages rewired: edit/page.tsx and edit/[id]/page.tsx now load data live from filesystem instead of prebuild-generated TS. Verified: all CRUD operations tested (create, update, clone, archive), CLI tested end-to-end, tsc clean (only pre-existing DevId errors).

task.goal: Verb collapse documentation — write `references/skill-lifecycle.md` explaining decision gad-198's two-verb model (**express** = `gad try` for epigenetic temporary expression; **integrate** = `gad evolution promote` for permanent DNA integration) against the three states (**expressed** in `.gad-try/`, **mutation** in `.planning/proto-skills/`, **integrated** in `.claude/skills/` or canonical `skills/`). Map every existing CLI command to its lifecycle role: `gad try`, `gad try cleanup`, `gad evolution install`, `gad evolution promote`, `gad evolution shed`, `gad evolution discard`, `gad skill promote-folder`, `gad install` (local), `gad install --global`. Deprecate `gad install --global` as the default first-encounter path for new skills (route new users through `gad try` + `gad evolution promote` instead). Backward compatible — no CLI changes, docs only. Update `references/skill-shape.md` §1 and AGENTS.md loop section to cite the new lifecycle model. Per decision gad-198.
Project detail tab shell — add tabbed navigation (Overview, Planning, Evolution, System) to /projects/[...id] with URL search param ?tab= routing.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Tab shell was scaffolded earlier — this task closes it by (1) routing hardcoded tab labels through the 45-04 VOCAB catalogue (`VOCAB.tabs.overview/planning/evolution/system`), giving the four top-level labels one source of truth and eliminating a 45-15-audit blind spot; and (2) persisting the Planning sub-tab (tasks/decisions/roadmap/requirements/notes) in the URL via a new `?sub=` search param with the same default-hides pattern as `?tab=` — tasks is the default and omits the param; any other sub-tab writes `?sub=` via `router.replace({ scroll: false })`. The sub-tab buttons now gain `data-cid="project-planning-subtab-"` so the Visual Context System can address each one. Decisions/roadmap sub-panes still show "coming soon" placeholders pointing at the specific follow-up tasks (45-07, 45-08) that will wire them. Files touched: `site/app/projects/[...id]/ProjectDetailTabs.tsx`, `site/app/projects/[...id]/ProjectDetailTabContent.tsx`. Typecheck + build clean.

task.goal: Per-project data adapter — server-side loader that reads .planning/ XML files for a given projectId and returns tasks, decisions, phases, requirements, notes as typed props.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Adapter `site/app/projects/[...id]/project-planning-data.ts` was partially scaffolded earlier (tasks/decisions/phases/requirements/state via the CJS readers). This task closes it by adding notes support: (1) new `PlanningNote` exported type `{ id, title, updatedAt, snippet, relPath }`; (2) `notes: PlanningNote[]` added to the `ProjectPlanningData` interface and populated by `loadProjectPlanningData()` for each project; (3) new `loadProjectNotes(projectId)` standalone export; (4) `collectNotes()` walker that recurses `.planning/notes/` and `.planning/docs/`, reads `.md` / `.mdx`, derives title (first H1 or filename), snippet (first paragraph, 220-char cap, trailing ellipsis), and updatedAt (mtime ISO), sorted newest-first. Logic mirrors the existing `site/app/api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/planning/route.ts` scanner so the API route can migrate to this in a later pass (not in scope here). Smoke-tested with the live project: `get-anything-done` → 389 tasks / 221 decisions / 51 phases / 17 notes / state.currentPhase=42.4. Typecheck + build clean. Unblocks 45-06..11 migrations (tab components can now consume the adapter directly instead of fetching the API route).

task.goal: Per-project API routes for planning data — GET /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/planning and /api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/system returning JSON.
Vocabulary constants file — single-source mapping of internal terms to display terms per decisions gad-166, gad-169, gad-189. Exports VOCAB object used by all components.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Rewrote `site/lib/vocabulary.ts` from a minimal VOCAB stub into a full single-source catalogue. Structure: (1) typed `ENTITIES` record with `{singular, plural, article}` for project/recipe/species/generation/brood/bestiary/dna/finding (decision gad-189 vocabulary + gad-169 whitepaper framing — Article is the singular for finding); (2) `VOCAB.conditions` mapping gad/gsd/bare/custom (gad-166 — explicitly no "GAD+emergent"); (3) `VOCAB.publication` with draft/published (gad-189 default-draft model); (4) `VOCAB.sections` and `VOCAB.tabs` for UI labels; (5) `VOCAB.verbs` for spawn/breed/evolve/publish/preserve (gad-219 — verbs kept distinct); (6) `VOCAB.renamed` table mapping legacy keys (evalProject, run, version, round) to canonical `EntityKey`; (7) four tiny helpers: `entityLabel`, `pluralize(key, count)`, `withArticle(key)` ("a Species" / "an Article"), `countLabel(key, count)` ("3 Generations"), `canonicalizeLegacyKey(key)` returning `EntityKey | null`; (8) `BANNED_TERMS` array with the specific product framings banned (eval project, eval-project, eval_project, GAD+emergent, gad-emergent, gad+emergent) — deliberately NOT banning bare "emergent" because it is a legitimate workflow descriptor. TS compile clean; smoke-tested helpers via ts-transpile + require in a throwaway Node eval: 18/18 checks pass (pluralization, articles, count labels, legacy canonicalization, condition/publication/verb lookups, banned-term membership). No consumers exist yet — unblocks 45-06..11 tab migrations and the 45-15 audit.

task.goal: Art of War epigraph registry — curated quotes mapped to section contexts (hero, planning, evolution, system, findings, scoring) with original + adapted variants.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. Expanded `site/lib/epigraphs.ts` from a minimal 10-entry stub into a typed registry of 22 Sun Tzu epigraphs across 8 sections: hero (3), planning (3), evolution (3), system (3), findings (3), scoring (3), bestiary (2), marketplace (2). Each entry carries `original` (verbatim Sun Tzu) and `adapted` (rewritten through our evolutionary/build vocabulary — species, generation, brood, codebase, tokens, gauges, pressure). Added `EpigraphSection` string-literal type for compile-time section-key safety and four helpers: (1) `getAllEpigraphs()` — full list; (2) `getEpigraph(section)` — first match, backwards-compat; (3) `getEpigraphsFor(section)` — all for a section; (4) `pickEpigraph(section, seed?)` — deterministic pick via a small non-crypto hash (same seed → same epigraph so a page can stabilise its choice per session/build). `countBySection()` returns coverage counts for rotation reporting. Smoke-tested via transpile+require: 22 total, coverage correct per section, deterministic seed stable, unknown section returns null. Build clean. No consumers wired yet — 45-16 (hero copy + epigraphs on section dividers) is the visible-rendering task that will drop in a shared `` component using these helpers.

task.goal: Migrate PlanningTasksTab to project-scoped — accept projectId prop, fetch per-project data from Wave 1 adapter, wire into Planning tab.
Migrate PlanningDecisionsTab to project-scoped — accept projectId prop, render per-project decisions under Planning tab.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. `ProjectPlanningDecisionsTab` was already written as a project-scoped component (accepts `projectId`, fetches `/api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/planning`, newest-first ordering, expand/collapse past 30 items, `data-cid="project-planning-decisions-site-section"`). This task closes the migration by replacing the "coming soon" placeholder in `ProjectDetailTabContent.tsx` — the `planningSubTab === "decisions"` branch now renders the component. Build clean. Users hitting `/projects/[...id]?tab=planning&sub=decisions` now get live decisions data (221 for get-anything-done) instead of a placeholder.

task.goal: Migrate PlanningRoadmapTab + GanttSection to project-scoped — roadmap phases and Gantt timeline for one project.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16. `ProjectPlanningRoadmapTab` was already written as a project-scoped component (accepts `projectId`, fetches phases + tasks from the planning API, renders phase list + Gantt-style timeline with per-status color bands). This task closes the migration by replacing the "coming soon" placeholder in `ProjectDetailTabContent.tsx` — the `planningSubTab === "roadmap"` branch now renders the component. Build clean. Note: roadmap is demoted in the gauge bank (gad-229) but remains a first-class planning sub-tab — this task is about VIEWING roadmap data, not gauging it.

task.goal: Migrate PlanningRequirementsTab + PlanningNotesTab to project-scoped rendering under Planning tab.
Migrate Evolution tab components — PlanningWorkflowsTab + PlanningSkillCandidatesTab become per-project Evolution tab showing workflow patterns and proto-skills.
Migrate System tab components — PlanningSystemStatCards + PlanningSystemRuntimeActivityPanel become per-project System tab fed by self-eval.json and trace data via command bridge.
Rewire /planning route — replace monolithic planning page with cross-project dashboard or redirect to /projects. Content now lives per-project.
Landing page composition — rewrite project detail Overview tab as marketing landing: workflow visualizations, human review scores, stats bar, findings as whitepaper cards, VCS showcase.
task.resolution: Shipped 2026-04-16 — pragmatic cut: stats bar + section reorder, with workflow visualization and VCS showcase deferred to a 45-13 follow-up to avoid scope explosion mid-flight. (1) New component `site/app/projects/[...id]/ProjectStatsBar.tsx`: 4-cell grid across generations (runs count + "preserved builds" sub), species (count + "recipes in brood"), best human review (max `humanReviewNormalized.aggregate_score` / `humanReview.score` across runs, normalized to X.X / 5 or X.X / 10 depending on source scale), latest generation (version + relative date — "3d ago", "2w ago", etc). Icons from lucide (GitBranch/Dna/Star/Clock), rendered as bordered cards with accent icons. Uses the 45-04 `countLabel()` helper so "1 Generation" / "3 Generations" pluralize correctly. Each cell has `data-cid="project-stats-bar-cell-"` for VCS. (2) Overview composition reordered in `/projects/[...id]/page.tsx`: Hero → StatsBar → Runs → Findings (moved up from below Requirements — visitors see outcomes before inputs) → Requirements → Skills scope → Emergent lineage (workflow-gated) → Bugs → Scoring weights. ProjectFindingsSection is already card/article-style per `ProjectFindingArticle`, so "whitepaper cards" requirement is satisfied by promoting its position. (3) Deferred scope captured in an inline comment on the composition JSX and as `2026-04-16-45-13-followup-workflow-vcs-showcase.md` todo: workflow-graph visualization component + VCS showcase panel each deserve their own task, not inline with the composition rewrite. Typecheck + build clean.

task.goal: Type ramp + color palette refresh — clear hierarchy across project/species/generation levels, growth gradients, DNA base pair dots, phylogeny timeline. Dark theme + accent system preserved.
task.impl: vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/globals.css: added taxonomy-level tokens (--level-project warm amber inheriting accent identity; --level-species muted teal/seafoam; --level-generation soft violet) so a scanner can identify entity scale without reading labels. Added --gradient-growth (cool→warm, "upward") and --gradient-evolution alias for the existing accent gradient as forward-prep for score ramps + phylogeny edges. Added --dna-{a,t,g,c} base-pair palette (warm/cool by canonical pairing) and a `.dna-dot` utility with `.dna-{a,t,g,c}` variants for upcoming evolution band components. Added type ramp utilities in the components layer (.text-display, .text-section-title, .text-card-title, .text-meta, .text-meta-strong, .text-data) that formalize the sizes/weights/tracking previously sprinkled across cards/chips/section headers. Added .chip-level + chip-level-{project,species,generation} helpers that color-mix off the level token so a single token swap re-themes every chip. Applied to two high-visibility surfaces: vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/project-market/species/[species]/page.tsx (species h1 carries species-level color + chip; project group h2 uses text-section-title + project chip; generation rows lead with generation chip + .text-data version) and vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/project-market/ProjectMarketSpeciesBand.tsx (active state saturates --level-species via color-mix; idle hover hints species hue without competing with the project grid). All level-tinted surfaces leave existing semantic colors intact (emerald for "best score" positive signal, amber featured ribbon, destructive errors). Vendor site build green.

task.goal: Vocabulary audit — systematic grep + replace of banned terms in all public-facing site/ components. eval project→Species, run→Generation, GAD+emergent→remove.
task.notes: 2026-04-16d done. Live user-facing copy rebranded across: nav-shared, PlayableTeaser home, docs/quick-start.md (v1.33 + canonical CLI), /downloads metadata + Section 3, TemplatesIntro + TemplatesEvalSection, Footer.tsx (blurb + stale jump-link repair for gad-196 drift), playable-shared descriptions (3), round-results-shared description, ProjectMarketHeader title + prose, build-site-data.mjs llms.txt blurb, app/layout.tsx metadata + openGraph. /how-it-works verified clean. Residual "eval" in site/ is in generated data files (eval-data.generated.ts, catalog.generated.ts, llms-full.txt) sourced from historical decision/task text — these are durable historical record and intentionally preserved.

task.goal: Hero copy rewrite + Art of War epigraphs — evolutionary language in hero section, epigraphs on section dividers, playful evolutionary metaphors throughout.
task.resolution:
Shipped 2026-04-16. Three deliverables: (1) New reusable `site/components/site/SectionEpigraph.tsx` — renders the adapted Sun Tzu line as emphasized italic with the verbatim original shown beneath as "After Sun Tzu, The Art of War: ...". Quote icon, bordered muted band, center or left alignment. Null-renders when the section has no epigraphs so it's safe to drop anywhere. Exported through `@/components/site`. Consumes the 45-05 registry via `pickEpigraph(section, seed)` with seed-based deterministic picks. (2) Landing page `site/app/page.tsx`: six section dividers between the existing bands (hero/system/planning/evolution/findings/marketplace) with stable seeds so the picks don't flip on refresh. (3) Project Overview tab `site/app/projects/[...id]/page.tsx`: four dividers (hero/bestiary/planning/scoring) with seeds namespaced by `project.id` so different projects see a different rotation. (4) Hero copy polish on `LandingEvolutionBand` — fixed the "permanent roto skills" typo → "permanent proto-skills"; added evolutionary framing ("species mutate, generations compete, and the survivors promote..."); kept the "context under pressure" tagline intact because it tested well as-is. Avoided a full hero rewrite to not regress approved tone. Smoke test: `curl http://localhost:3000/` returns 6 distinct `section-epigraph-
` data-cid attributes. Typecheck + build clean.

task.goal: Logo + favicon refresh — evolutionary-themed design (helix, phylogeny tree, or similar). Update favicon, OG image, metadata references.
Marketplace integration verification — /project-market cards link to new project detail Overview tab, published/draft filter works, landing page content visible.
task.resolution:
Shipped 2026-04-16 as a verification pass. All three bullets confirmed: (1) `ProjectCard.tsx:148` links to `/projects/${project.id}` which hits the new tab-shell (45-01). (2) Published filter is active at `use-project-market.ts:44-48`: `EVAL_PROJECTS.filter((p) => p.published === true)` — since no project has `published: true` yet, the marketplace correctly renders the `ProjectMarketEmptyCatalog` ("No published projects yet") empty state. Live probe: `curl http://localhost:3000/project-market` returns the empty state and has no `View project` card links, as expected. (3) Landing page content on `/projects/` is visible: live probe of `/projects/app-forge/gad` renders all four stats-bar cells, 4 distinct `section-epigraph-
` data-cids (hero/bestiary/planning/scoring), and tab-shell buttons. Minor fix landed alongside: `ProjectStatsBar` previously derived its data-cid from `sub.split(" ")[0]` which collided when two cells started with "no" ("no preserved yet" / "no review yet"). Added an explicit `cidKey` prop so each cell has a stable, unique identifier regardless of subtext — `project-stats-bar-cell-{generations|species|review|latest}`. Typecheck + build path clean.

task.goal: Dead import cleanup — remove unused /planning components superseded by project-scoped versions. Audit site/app/planning/*.tsx for orphaned files.
Full build + VCS audit — npm run build passes, all new components have data-cid attributes, VCS grep confirms every new section is discoverable.
README + external docs copy pass — update all external-facing docs to evolutionary vocabulary, drop GAD+emergent framing, treat findings as articles/whitepapers.
Consolidate gad workspace commands into gad projects (sync/add/ignore). Deprecate workspace as alias with notice. Update all error messages referencing workspace. Decision gad-208.

Trim landing site nav to 9 public routes. Remove dead route directories (glossary, formulas, insights, bugs, phases, tasks, decisions, eval-guide, standards, data, agents/[id], commands/[id], runs/[project]/[version]). Create /how-it-works stub. Decision gad-207.
Build SpriteAnimation React component — CSS steps() based sprite sheet renderer. Props: src, frameWidth, frameHeight, frameCount, fps, loop, direction, paused. Decision gad-211.
Add species-level .planning/ support to gad projects sync. When discover=true, detect species/<name>/.planning/ dirs as sub-roots. Decision gad-209.
Define project-level assets/ directory convention and generation data salvage pipeline. Extract valuable data from completed generation runs to species level. Decision gad-210.
Expand /downloads page: three sections — installer+CLI, VCS component registry (npx-installable showcase with live demos), planning packs. Decision gad-207.

Build /how-it-works content: GAD loop diagram, species/generation/DNA model explainer, scoring methodology, standards references. Absorbs content from removed /eval-guide, /formulas, /standards routes.
Verify build passes after route removal + nav trim. Fix broken imports from deleted routes. Full type-check + VCS audit.

Teachings reader routes — /teachings index (card grid by category, difficulty + date + tags) and /teachings/[slug] detail (marked markdown render, frontmatter stripped, backrefs panel for implementation/decisions/phases/related). Server components read teachings/index.json + per-tip md bodies from teachings/static/**. Decision gad-264 promoted this into active phase 46 scope. Acceptance: curl /teachings returns 200 with tip titles visible; curl /teachings/<slug> returns 200 with rendered body. Verified against all 6 indexed tips.

Fix gad tip reindex ENOENT on installed binary. teachings-reader.cjs now resolves TEACHINGS_DIR by walking up from cwd looking for a gad-config.toml repo root and preferring vendor/get-anything-done/teachings/ (or direct teachings/) on that root before falling back to the bundled runtime location. GAD_TEACHINGS_DIR env override added for testing. Source CLI verified — 'node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs tip reindex' now writes to the user repo's teachings/ instead of the read-only runtime bundle. Installed binary picks up the fix on next reinstall (deferred: current gad.exe is still held open by this session).

Submodule audit — mb-cli-framework, grime-time-site, repub-builder, claw-code, magicborn, get-anything-done. Scope each: last commit upstream vs. ours, production usage vs. dead references, whether still needed as submodule or should be pulled in-tree / deleted. Initial scan 2026-04-18: mb-cli-framework has 103 live references (packages/magicborn-cli tests + docs + vendor-registry + ink-tui) — KEEP. grime-time-site / repub-builder / claw-code status unknown, user suspects unused now that PayloadCMS pivot was dropped. Deliverable: references/submodule-audit-2026-04.md with KEEP/INLINE/DROP recommendation per submodule. Follow-up tasks for any DROP + gitmodules cleanup.

Catalog all 91 vendor SKILL.md files into DEV/PROD/META lanes per gad-247 + todo 2026-04-17-skill-lane-taxonomy. Output: .planning/notes/skill-lane-taxonomy-2026-04-18.md (counts: 60 dev, 22 meta, 3 prod, 6 dual-tag). Recommends META as third lane. Answers todo open questions (debug=dev+meta, verify-phase=dev, verify-work=dev+prod, create-skill=meta, third lane=yes). Provides phase 55 frontmatter schema + 5-wave migration order. Closes audit todo; phase 55 frontmatter rollout itself is a separate task.

Clerk skeleton in apps/planning-app: install @clerk/nextjs, mount middleware that protects /api/dev/* and /projects/edit/*, render UserButton in header, expose helper that resolves operator id (Clerk userId or 'solo-operator' fallback). Stamp publishedBy on the publish PATCH route from auth(). Solo-operator fallback when CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY/CLERK_SECRET_KEY are unset so local dev keeps working without Clerk creds.
task.impl: apps/planning-app/{middleware.ts (clerkMiddleware + createRouteMatcher protect for /api/dev/*, /projects/edit/*; passthrough when CLERK_SECRET_KEY+NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY unset), lib/operator-id.ts (auth().userId > x-operator-id header > OPERATOR_ID env > "solo-operator"; isClerkEnabled() server flag), components/auth/{AuthProvider,AuthHeaderSlot}.tsx (conditional ClerkProvider + UserButton/SignInButton or solo-operator pill), AppShell.tsx (header slot wired). publish/route.ts PATCH/POST/DELETE stamp publishedBy from getOperatorId() unless body overrides.

task.goal: Project Editor agent workspace at /project-editor + /project-editor/[project] in apps/planning-app: index lists registered projects with latest published generation chip, detail page renders ProjectTree (file browser via /api/dev/tree), forge-view iframe (latest preserved build via /api/dev/forge-view + /api/dev/forge-asset), and SSE-streamed agent launcher (/api/dev/spawn) with confirm dialog for claude-code/codex/gad-eval-run. Cross-links to /projects/edit/[id] (bestiary/BYOK) + /my-projects drawer. Middleware extends Clerk protection to /project-editor/* since spawn writes to disk.
task.impl: apps/planning-app/lib/project-editor/projects.ts (listProjectSummaries joins gad-config.toml roots with marketplace index for latestGeneration chip), apps/planning-app/lib/dev/useSseSpawn.ts (local replacement for the deleted vendor hook — POST + EventSource-style SSE parser, dispatches stdout/stderr/system/exit), apps/planning-app/app/project-editor/{page.tsx (index, restored from other-agent commit, rewired to @/lib/project-editor/projects), [project]/page.tsx (detail — agent buttons with confirm dialog, ProjectTree, forge-view iframe, terminal pane)}, apps/planning-app/components/ProjectTree.tsx (restored, hits /api/dev/tree), apps/planning-app/app/api/dev/forge-asset/[project]/[gen]/[...slug]/route.ts (recreated — serves files from apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/<gen>/ with realpath traversal guard), apps/planning-app/middleware.ts (matcher extended with /project-editor/(.*)), apps/planning-app/components/AppShell.tsx (nav adds Project Editor entry). Drive-by fixes: app/api/dev/projects/[id]/write/route.ts had wrong relative path to lib/projects-data; app/api/dev/gene-states/route.ts had implicit-any callback param.

task.goal: Subagent attribution: spawn route resolves operator via getOperatorId() and injects OPERATOR_ID + GAD_OPERATOR_ID + GAD_AGENT_ID + GAD_AGENT_ROLE + GAD_PROJECT_ID into child env, plus /api/dev/whoami returns the same payload for client-side surfaces. Subagent CLIs (claude-code, codex, gad eval run) can echo identity via env, and a callback into /api/dev/publish from inside a spawned child can pass x-operator-id header so MANIFEST.json publishedBy carries the human operator even when the actual write came from an agent runtime.
task.impl: apps/planning-app/app/api/dev/spawn/route.ts (await getOperatorId(); compute agentId = `${command}:${projectId}:${Date.now().toString(36)}`; build attributionEnv with OPERATOR_ID + GAD_OPERATOR_ID + GAD_AGENT_ID + GAD_AGENT_ROLE + GAD_PROJECT_ID; merge into spawn() env after FORCE_COLOR; SSE start event now includes operator/agentId/agentRole so the terminal pane can echo identity in its header line). apps/planning-app/app/api/dev/whoami/route.ts NEW (GET returns { operatorId, clerkEnabled, soloOperator } via getOperatorId() + isClerkEnabled(); dev-gated 403 outside NODE_ENV=development). Effect: publish PATCH from a CLI inside a spawned child reads OPERATOR_ID env, passes it as x-operator-id header to /api/dev/.../publish, and getOperatorId() returns the human operator (Clerk userId or solo-operator) instead of the runtime user. Server-rendered surfaces (/my-published) call getOperatorId() directly; client-side surfaces call /api/dev/whoami once per mount.

task.goal: /my-published view in apps/planning-app: server reads marketplace-index.generated.json and filters generations where publishedBy === getOperatorId(). Solo-operator mode shows everything publishedBy 'solo-operator'; Clerk mode shows the signed-in user's generations only. Each row links to /projects/edit/[id] (revert/manage) and to the public playable URL. Empty state explains how to publish.
task.impl: apps/planning-app/app/my-published/page.tsx NEW (async server component; loads marketplace-index.generated.json from GAD_SITE_DIR; resolves operatorId via getOperatorId(); filters generations where publishedBy === operatorId; sorts by publishedAt desc; groups by project; renders header with operator+clerk badges, totals row (count/projects/species), per-project sections with manage + public-marketplace cross-links, per-row version + species chip linking to /project-market/species/<s> + publishedAt + score + contextFramework + play link). apps/planning-app/components/AppShell.tsx adds "My Published" nav entry between Project Editor and Planning. apps/planning-app/middleware.ts protects /my-published when Clerk is enabled (matcher list extended). Empty state explains the publish flow when no generations stamp the current operator yet.

task.goal: Hot-rebuild marketplace-index.generated.json on publish so /my-published and the public marketplace surface state changes without a full vendor-site predev. Avoid re-scanning every TRACE.json — mutate the existing index in place, preserving fields the publish PATCH can't recompute (score, date, contextFramework).
task.impl: apps/planning-app/lib/marketplace/rebuild-index.ts NEW (upsertGenerationInIndex({ projectId, species, version, status, publishedAt, publishedBy, hasBuild }): loads marketplace-index.generated.json from GAD_SITE_DIR, computes generationId, finds existing row, applies rules — published+hasBuild = upsert preserving score/date/contextFramework, draft/unlisted/no-build = remove; recomputes species summary [species, projects[], publishedCount, latestPublishedAt]; sorts generations by publishedAt desc; bumps generated_at; writes back. Returns { ok, reason: upserted|removed|skipped-no-build|no-index|write-failed, generationsCount, speciesCount }). apps/planning-app/app/api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/[name]/generations/[version]/publish/route.ts: applyStatus() now calls upsertGenerationInIndex() after da.setGenerationStatus(); failure swallowed (manifest is source of truth, predev still rebuilds canonical index).

task.goal: Per-project best/latest score chips on /project-market/species/[species] detail page. Operators evaluating a species's portfolio against one project want to see the high-water mark and the most recent scored attempt at a glance, not just the count of generations.
task.impl: vendor/get-anything-done/site/app/project-market/species/[species]/page.tsx: per project group, filter to scored runs (g.score !== null), pick max-by-score as bestScore and first scored as latestWithScore (gens already sorted by publishedAt desc). Render emerald-tinted "best 0.XX · vN" chip and a neutral "latest 0.XX · vN" chip when latest != best, plus the existing generation count. Tooltips explain semantics. Chips skipped entirely when no run is scored.

task.goal: gad publish CLI command with operator attribution. Subagents spawned via /api/dev/spawn inherit OPERATOR_ID/GAD_AGENT_ID env vars (task 51-03) but had no CLI consumer that actually threaded them as request headers. Provide one so an agent runtime calling `gad publish escape-the-dungeon --species gad --version v7` from inside a spawned shell stamps MANIFEST.publishedBy with the human operator, not the runtime.
task.impl: vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs: new gadOperatorAttribution() helper reads OPERATOR_ID/GAD_OPERATOR_ID/GAD_AGENT_ID/GAD_AGENT_ROLE from env and returns request headers. New publishCmd (positional project; --species --version --status --url --json) PATCHes the planning-app publish endpoint with attribution headers; falls back to GAD_PLANNING_APP_URL or http://localhost:3002. Wired into main subCommands as `publish`. Help output includes operator + planning-app URL guidance.

task.goal: gad publish list — read-only inspection of the marketplace index from the CLI. Operators want to confirm what their identity has actually shipped without opening /my-published in a browser, and agents want a way to assert published state in scripts without spinning up planning-app.
task.impl: vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs: restructured publishCmd into parent with subCommands {set, list}. publishSetCmd is the prior PATCH behavior (renamed). publishListCmd reads vendor/get-anything-done/site/lib/marketplace-index.generated.json directly from disk, applies --mine (default, filters by gadOperatorAttribution operator) | --all | --project | --species filters, prints a padded table or --json. Added injectPublishDefault() so `gad publish` (bare) routes to `publish list` and `gad publish <projectid> ...` routes to `publish set` to preserve ergonomics. Help output for the parent describes both subcommands.

task.goal: UI publish actions need to be distinguishable from CLI/agent publishes in the audit trail. Without a marker, server logs show `operator=<id>` for both a Bestiary tab click and a `gad publish` call from an agent, hiding intent.
task.impl: apps/planning-app/app/projects/edit/[id]/BestiaryTab.tsx + InspectorPane.tsx: publish PATCH calls now stamp `x-gad-agent-id: ui-click:<surface>:<ts36>` and `x-gad-agent-role: ui-click` headers (surface=bestiary|inspector). Server route apps/planning-app/app/api/dev/evals/projects/[id]/species/[name]/generations/[version]/publish/route.ts reads both headers and emits a single `[publish] <path> → <status> · operator=<id> · role=<role> · agent=<agentId>` console.info per PATCH so dev-server logs visibly distinguish UI vs CLI/agent publishes. Headers are best-effort; missing values just produce a shorter log line.

task.goal: Scaffold apps/planning-app/ as a new Next 15 workspace entry. Minimal shell: layout.tsx with dev-only auth stub, home page redirecting to /my-projects, tailwind config mirroring the landing app, tsconfig paths. Runs on a distinct dev port (3002) so it coexists with the landing app during migration.

Extract shared UI primitives into a consumable location. Candidates: MarketingShell (rename to AppShell with slot-based navigation), SectionEpigraph, SiteSection, SiteProse. Options: (a) packages/ui-shared workspace package, (b) direct imports from vendor/get-anything-done/site/components/site/ with a re-export barrel. Pick (b) initially — faster, defer package extraction until a third consumer emerges.
task.implementation: Pivot from option (b). Landing primitives too context-coupled (DevIdProvider, BandDevPanel, landing data modules) for re-export barrel. Authored minimal local primitives in apps/planning-app/: components/AppShell.tsx (header/nav/footer chrome with nav to /my-projects, /planning, /teachings), components/SectionHeading.tsx (kicker + title + description), lib/cn.ts (clsx+tailwind-merge). Added clsx + tailwind-merge deps. Layout + my-projects page wired. Todo 2026-04-17-shared-ui-primitive-extraction.md captures when packages/ui-shared extraction becomes worth doing (third consumer lands).

task.goal: Port /planning route from landing app to planning-app. All existing tabs (Tasks, Decisions, Requirements, Notes, Workflows, SkillCandidates, System) move intact. Data source stays the same — planning CLI readers via gad binary or direct XML parse. Landing app's /planning stays for one release cycle with a deprecation banner.
task.implementation: apps/planning-app/app/planning/{page.tsx,PlanningTabbedContent.tsx,TasksTab.tsx,DecisionsTab.tsx,RoadmapTab.tsx,RequirementsTab.tsx,NotesTab.tsx,SystemTab.tsx,EvolutionTab.tsx}; apps/planning-app/lib/planning-data.ts (server-side loader — walks up from cwd for monorepo root, delegates to vendor/get-anything-done/lib/*-reader.cjs via createRequire acquired through process.getBuiltinModule('node:module') to bypass Next/Turbopack static analysis of CJS readers); apps/planning-app/app/globals.css (add accent / accent-foreground tokens needed by ported tab UI). Outer tabs: Planning/Evolution/System. Planning subtabs: Tasks/Decisions/Roadmap/Requirements/Notes. Evolution subtabs: Workflows/SkillCandidates/ProtoSkills — all derived client-side from planning payload. Default projectId=get-anything-done; multi-project scoping deferred to 59-04.

task.goal: Port /projects routes + build the /my-projects dashboard. Dashboard shows: list of owned projects with phase/next-action snippet, per-project drawer (current phase, open tasks, recent decisions, subagent-run history, BYOK env status), action buttons (trigger daily subagent, view last report, open planning XML). Consumes gad CLI readers. Lives on planning-app only — landing gets a redirect banner.
task.resolution: Read-only dashboard live at /my-projects. Parses [[planning.roots]] from gad-config.toml (dep-free regex), calls existing CJS readers (task-registry-reader, decisions-reader, state-reader) via the process.getBuiltinModule('node:module').createRequire dance shared with 59-03's planning-data.ts. 10 projects render. Drawer at /my-projects?projectid=<id> shows full STATE.xml render, last 5 decisions, open tasks grouped by phase, subagent-run empty-state reading .planning/subagent-runs/<id>/*.json (ready for 59-07 writer + 59-09 flesh-out), BYOK tile fixed to 'coming in phase 60' per gad-265 Q3 option C. planning-data.ts extended: resolveProjectBaseDir now falls back to gad-config.toml [[planning.roots]] for arbitrary project ids; /planning page accepts ?projectid= alongside legacy ?project=. /projects/<id> detail route is not in this drop — drawer covers the same data; landing /projects port can lift from ProjectDrawer when 59-08 deprecates it. Degraded-card rendering for missing .planning (none triggered today — all 10 roots healthy). pnpm --filter @portfolio/planning-app build passes.

task.goal: Implement 'gad planning serve' CLI subcommand. Spawns planning-app (next dev in dev / next start in prod) on port 3002 with reuse detection — if something is already listening on the port and responds with a planning-app health endpoint, attach rather than spawn. Logs go to ~/.gad/logs/planning-app-<date>.jsonl. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT.
task.implementation: vendor/get-anything-done/lib/planning-serve.cjs (new — resolveWorkspaceRoot, pure decideReuseAction, probeHealth, spawnPlanningApp, log helpers); vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs (planningServeCmd added to existing planningCmd subCommands alongside hydrate); apps/planning-app/app/api/health/route.ts (new — returns {app:"gad-planning-app",version,port,uptimeSeconds} with Content-Type:application/json); vendor/get-anything-done/tests/planning-serve.test.cjs (new — 12 tests of decideReuseAction). Shells out to `pnpm --filter @portfolio/planning-app dev|start` per decision gad-265 Q1 (monorepo-only for phase 59). Port resolution: --port > GAD_PLANNING_PORT env > 3002. Log file: ~/.gad/logs/planning-app-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl (daily rotation by filename). SIGINT/SIGBREAK forwarded to child, 10s grace, then SIGKILL. --no-browser accepted silently (serve never opens one; flag kept for symmetry with task 59-06 gad start). dist/gad.cjs rebuilt via npm run build:cli; SEA binary reinstall deferred (current gad.exe session-locked). Known gap: pnpm must be on PATH — child.on('error') surfaces actionable error if not.

task.goal: Implement 'gad start' CLI subcommand (decision gad-262). Runs 'gad planning serve' if not running, waits for health, opens browser to http://localhost:3002/my-projects. Aliased as 'gad dashboard'. Idempotent. --no-browser flag skips the browser open for editor/iframe workflows.
task.implementation: vendor/get-anything-done/lib/start-cmd.cjs (new — resolveBrowserOpener for win32/darwin/linux, resolveGadExecPath self-invoker, spawnPlanningServeDetached via child.unref, waitForHealth polling with 30s default timeout + 500ms poll, runStart orchestrator); vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs (+~50 lines registering startCmd with --port + --no-browser flags and 'dashboard' alias). Reuses planning-serve.cjs probeHealth + decideReuseAction for health-check parity. Idempotent — second invocation attaches via probe/attach path.

task.goal: Daily-subagent dispatch hook. 'gad start' (or an explicit 'gad start --dispatch-subagents') reads each project config for daily-subagent: true and, if today's run has not yet been recorded in .planning/subagent-runs/<projectid>/, spawns the configured subagent runtime with the project snapshot + task context. Writes run record on completion (consumes subagent-run-history todo schema).
task.notes: Prompt-emit-only (option Y): writes .planning/subagent-runs/<projectid>/<today>-<taskid>.{prompt.md,json}. Config flag is `dailySubagent = true` (lowerCamelCase, matches existing `planningDir`/`discover` keys) on [[planning.roots]]. llm-from-scratch is the first project flagged. Skip-already-ran uses filename prefix match on <today>-*.json (fail-safe: corrupt files count as handled). Local-date, not UTC. Real runtime dispatch deferred — see todo 2026-04-17-subagent-real-dispatch-runtime.md (future task 59-07a); scoped-spawn integration point flagged inline in subagent-dispatch.cjs. `gad start --dispatch-subagents` wires the dispatch loop after browser-open. New top-level command family `gad subagents dispatch [--projectid X]`. 15 tests, 15/15 passing.

task.goal: Deprecate landing-site /planning and /projects routes with redirect stubs — server-rendered page that says 'this view moved to gad planning serve' with copy-pasteable install+run commands. Keep for one release cycle then remove. Update all internal cross-links to /my-projects.
task.notes: Shared PlanningAppRedirectStub (MarketingShell + SiteSection) renders 3-step install/serve/open UX: npx install → gad planning serve → localhost:3002/<targetPath>. /planning stub targets /planning; /projects/[...id] stub preserves [...id] params and targets /my-projects?projectid=<id> (59-04 resolution punted dedicated /projects/:id route on planning-app — drawer holds parity). Nav "Projects" link repointed /projects → /project-market (marketplace is the surviving browse-all surface on landing). llms.txt generator in build-site-data.mjs relabels Planning entry as "moved" with planning-app run instructions. /projects/edit* routes untouched (phase 44.5 Project Editor scope). Dead inner components under /projects/[...id]/ (Hero, Runs, Findings…) deferred to task 45-19 (Dead import cleanup). tsc --noEmit clean; next build completes, /planning now static-prerendered, /projects/[...id] stays dynamic for param-aware metadata. Internal-link audit `grep -rEn "href=[\"']/(planning|projects/)" site/app site/components` → only legitimate remaining hit is /projects/edit (out of scope). Ref.tsx left alone — refs like /planning?tab=tasks#10-01 now land on the deprecation stub and surface the run instructions instead of deep-linking; acceptable for one release cycle per gad-261.

task.goal: Render subagent-run history on /my-projects project drawer. Reads .planning/subagent-runs/<projectid>/*.json (schema from todo 2026-04-17-subagent-run-history.md). Timeline view — date, task id, status, one-line outcome, link to full report body, link to teaching tip produced. Empty state with a 'trigger a run' button.
task.implementation: apps/planning-app/app/my-projects/SubagentRunHistory.tsx fleshed out from 59-04's stub. Timeline now groups runs by day (descending), each row shows HH:MM + task id + outcome + model chip + status pill (done=emerald, failed=rose, timeout=amber, in-progress=sky, unknown=muted). When a run has any of reportBody/teachingTipId/nextTaskId/commitSha, the row becomes an expandable native `` element with a caret rotation; the open panel renders a `` grid of duration (computed from startedAt/endedAt with s/m/h formatting), short-sha commit (7 chars), next task id, teaching tip id, source filename — plus a scrollable `` report body with max-h-64. Empty state keeps the "watching .planning/subagent-runs/X/" messaging and ADDS a `Trigger a run` block showing `gad subagents dispatch --projectid <id>` in a select-all code block with the dailySubagent gad-config.toml hint. Component stays server-side only — `` is native HTML, no client JS required. Every rendered element carries a data-cid for VCS (subagent-runs-empty-<id>, subagent-runs-list-<id>, subagent-run-<project>-<runId>, subagent-run-...-report, subagent-runs-dispatch-cmd-<id>). Verified via production build (Next 16.0.8 turbopack — compile 10.7s, tsc clean, all routes generate) + `next start` on :3003: fixture run record rendered every field (duration 14m13s, short sha d3cfce1, teaching-tip, report); empty state for claw-cli rendered dispatch command + "No subagent runs recorded" copy. Fixture deleted before commit.

task.goal: CLI efficiency gap cluster: land task-registry writer + gad tasks add + gad tasks promote + gad tasks --stalled + gad next. Closes the hand-editing loop that forced agents to edit TASK-REGISTRY.xml by hand to register new tasks (root cause of the 45-todo backlog in vendor/get-anything-done/.planning/todos/ never becoming real tasks). New lib/task-registry-writer.cjs: pure addTaskToXml + atomic appendTaskToFile, fs-injectable for tests, stable error codes (TASK_ID_EXISTS, PHASE_NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_FAILED, MALFORMED_XML, WRITE_FAILED). CLI subcommands under tasksV2Cmd: add (register by id/phase/goal), promote (lift a .planning/todos/*.md file into a task — filename derives id, first prose line becomes goal, todo moves to todos/promoted/ unless --keep). tasks list gains --stalled flag (in-progress tasks with no agent/skill/runtime attribution). New top-level gad next command: cross-project priority hotlist (tiers: active → stalled → next planned → idle). Tests: 16/16 in tests/task-registry-writer.test.cjs, all fs-injected, no real XML parser, no real disk. Also: operator-local gsd-* hooks deleted (gsd-check-update, gsd-context-monitor, gsd-prompt-guard, gsd-statusline, gsd-workflow-guard) + stale gsd-update-check.json cache + gsd-file-manifest.json — they were orphaned and surfacing /gsd:update prompts. Settings.json already points at gad-* siblings.
task.implementation: lib/task-registry-writer.cjs (~190 lines) provides escapeXml, buildTaskElement, addTaskToXml (pure, throws TaskWriterError), appendTaskToFile (atomic tmp+rename, fsImpl-injectable). bin/gad.cjs adds tasksAddCmd + tasksPromoteCmd under tasksV2Cmd.subCommands, extends tasksListCmd with --stalled flag (heuristic = in-progress without agent/skill/runtime attribution), adds top-level nextCmd with 4 tiers (active, stalled, next, idle) + --limit per project. Dogfooded by registering this task via `node bin/gad.cjs tasks add 59-10 --projectid get-anything-done --phase 59 --goal "..."` — first task in repo history that was added without hand-editing XML. Tests in tests/task-registry-writer.test.cjs (16 cases across 5 describe blocks): escapeXml coverage, buildTaskElement (required-fields, optional fields, goal-body escape), addTaskToXml happy path (phase scoping, closing-tag indent preservation, empty-phase insertion), failure modes (PHASE_NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_FAILED for missing/invalid fields, TASK_ID_EXISTS on global collision), appendTaskToFile with fs fake (happy path tmp cleanup, id-conflict leaves file untouched, rename-error cleanup). Gsd hook kill: removed 5 orphaned legacy hooks + 2 cache/manifest files at ~/.claude/hooks/ (user-local — not versioned in this repo).

task.goal:
Project Editor + dev API rehome from vendor/get-anything-done/site/ to apps/planning-app/. Pulls the entire `/projects/edit/[id]` tree (19 .tsx files: ProjectEditor, BestiaryTab, RecipesTab, ByokTab, InspectorPane, ProjectCanvas, DiffTree, GenerationRunner, RadarChart, TraitBar, DnaEditor, DnaActionRow, EditableField, InventoryGrid, LiveDataPanel, CommandPalette, PreviewFrame, page.tsx, use-command-bridge.ts) plus the supporting eval-data-runtime.ts loader and 8 of 16 `/api/dev/*` route trees actually used by the editor (command-bridge, env-defaults, evals/projects/*, gene-states, graph, live, scopes, secrets/*) into apps/planning-app. Vendor site keeps only marketing/landing surfaces.
Implementation: (1) tsconfig path `@gad-site/*` → `../../vendor/get-anything-done/site/*` so the editor still imports `eval-data` + `eval-data.generated` from the vendor site without copying the 19k-line generated file. (2) New apps/planning-app/lib/gad-paths.ts exports GAD_ROOT/GAD_LIB/GAD_BIN/GAD_SITE_DIR resolved via `import.meta.url` walk-up — replaces the original `process.cwd() + ".."` pattern that assumed cwd was the site dir. (3) scripts/rewrite-gad-paths.mjs (one-shot) sweeps all 19 copied files and substitutes the path-resolution patterns. (4) SiteSection ported into apps/planning-app/components/site/ as a stripped-down version (no dev-id band Visual Context Panel, no useDevId/BandDevPanel deps) so the public landing site can keep evolving its dev-id machinery without churning planning-app. (5) next.config.mjs sets outputFileTracingRoot to repo root + experimental.externalDir=true so Next traces the @gad-site imports cleanly. (6) Editor + dev API routes deleted from vendor site in the same change. Build verifies: pnpm --filter @portfolio/planning-app build succeeds with all 8 dev API routes + the editor route registered.
Vendor site `/projects/edit` and `/api/dev/*` routes are deleted in the same change. Captures decision-gad-269 boundary: vendor site = marketing/landing only; planning-app = operator surfaces (BYOK, evals, dev tooling).

Write references/byok-design.md resolving the open questions in phase 60 goal + decision gad-260: (a) crypto primitive — libsodium sealed-box vs AES-256-GCM with scrypt KDF, recommend one, (b) master-key UX — OS keychain-first (Windows Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, Linux secret-service) with passphrase fallback when keychain unavailable, (c) on-disk format — JSON envelope with version, salt, nonce, per-key metadata (last-rotated, scope, provider), (d) rotation model — in-place vs additive, (e) site-side strategy — browser-only WebCrypto decrypt vs server-side per-user KMS.
task.implementation: vendor/get-anything-done/references/byok-design.md (926 lines, 15 sections + references). Codifies gad-266 sign-off (A-H) plus 6 defense-in-depth additions: AAD bound to projectId|keyName|version (slot-swap prevention); gitignore write-failure fails closed; 'env get' reads not audit-logged (privacy); keychain stores derived key bytes not passphrase; test override hook; env-fallback kept only during migration cutover. 8 open follow-ups in §15, one filed as a todo (non-Windows keychain adapters), rest flagged for natural task-kickoff read-through.

task.goal: Implement lib/secrets-store.cjs + OS-keychain wrappers. .gad/secrets/<projectid>.enc format from 60-01. Three keychain adapters: windows-credential-manager (via PowerShell or node-ffi), macos-keychain (security CLI), linux-secret-service (via dbus-next). Unit tests with a mock keychain adapter. .gad/ added to repo-level .gitignore across all projects that register a planning root.
task.implementation: vendor/get-anything-done/lib/secrets-store.cjs (public API: get/set/list/rotate/revoke/decryptAll + test hooks), lib/secrets-store-errors.cjs (SecretsStoreError + 12 named codes per byok-design §12), lib/kdf.cjs (PBKDF2-SHA-256 600k iter, 32-byte output, zeroBuffer helper), lib/envelope.cjs (AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt with AAD=projectId|keyName|version, schemaVersion-1 JSON envelope serializer + verifier slot that disambiguates PASSPHRASE_INVALID from BAG_CORRUPT), lib/keychain/index.cjs (platform dispatcher + _setAdapterForTest hook), lib/keychain/windows.cjs (real DPAPI-via-PowerShell adapter writing ConvertFrom-SecureString blobs to %LOCALAPPDATA%\gad\keychain\), lib/keychain/macos.cjs + linux.cjs (stubs throwing KEYCHAIN_UNAVAILABLE), lib/keychain/passphrase-fallback.cjs (raw-mode TTY reader, no echo, Ctrl-C cancels), tests/secrets-store.test.cjs (20 tests covering round-trip, list metadata-only, rotation graceDays=0/7, revoke, AAD slot-swap BAG_CORRUPT, ciphertext mutation BAG_CORRUPT, wrong-passphrase PASSPHRASE_INVALID, gitignore fail-closed GITIGNORE_WRITE_FAILED + no .enc written, audit log, mock keychain round-trip, stale keychain fall-through, graceDays range validation). 20/20 pass. Windows DPAPI roundtrip smoke-tested on live box. Does NOT wire CLI (task 60-03).

task.goal: CLI family: 'gad env get <KEY> --projectid', 'gad env set <KEY> --projectid' (reads value from prompt, not argv, to avoid shell history), 'gad env list --projectid' (keys + metadata, never values), 'gad env rotate <KEY> --projectid', 'gad env revoke <KEY> --projectid'. All operations require master-key unlock.
task.implementation: vendor/get-anything-done/lib/env-cli.cjs (~330 lines — factory createEnvCli({store, readPassphrase, stdin, stdout, stderr, exit, confirm}) with get/set/list/rotate/revoke handlers; messageFor() maps every §12 error code to operator-facing remediation text; renderListTable() emits compact columnar output with values elided; TTY-echoless value prompt reuses passphrase-fallback helper, non-TTY path reads one line from stdin so `echo val | gad env set K --projectid P` composes; list() swallows "no envelope" KEY_NOT_FOUND and renders "0 keys set"). vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs wiring: 5 defineCommand subcommands under a top-level `envCmd` (registered in main subCommands between `data` and `eval`). tests/env-cli.test.cjs (30 tests — get happy/KEY_NOT_FOUND/passphrase-thread, set TTY/piped/empty/GITIGNORE_WRITE_FAILED, list table/json/empty, rotate defaults/graceDays-0/out-of-range, revoke --force/declined/accepted, every §12 error code → operator message, end-to-end PASSPHRASE_INVALID surface, table-render width+ISO-truncation). 30/30 pass; no regression in secrets-store tests (20/20 still green). Smoke: `gad env --help`, `gad env set --help`, `gad env list --projectid get-anything-done` all clean.

task.goal: Scoped spawn wrapper in lib/scoped-spawn.cjs. Given projectId + child command, decrypts that project's env bag, merges into child process.env ONLY for the child, never mutates parent process.env. Subagent dispatch (phase 59 task 59-07) uses this wrapper. Subagent sees OPENAI_API_KEY etc. without the main session leaking it.
task.implementation: vendor/get-anything-done/lib/scoped-spawn.cjs (~235 lines — factory createScopedSpawner({store, childProcess, now}) with spawn({projectId, command, args, options, keyNames?, requireBag?}) handler; default scopedSpawn bound to real secrets-store + child_process; ScopedSpawnError class with stable codes PROJECT_NOT_FOUND / DECRYPT_FAILED / SPAWN_FAILED + .cause preservation; merge strategy per gad-266 G spreads `{...process.env, ...projectEnv, GAD_PROJECT_ID: projectId}` so project bag wins over parent env on collision and GAD_PROJECT_ID injection wins over both; --exclusive deferred per byok-design §15 follow-up #4 with inline pointer; isMissingEnvelope() distinguishes KEY_NOT_FOUND-for-missing-envelope (route to PROJECT_NOT_FOUND / empty bag) from KEY_NOT_FOUND-for-missing-key (route to DECRYPT_FAILED); post-spawn references nulled out + documented V8-immutable-string spec-limit; caller options.env silently overridden — scoped-spawn owns env). tests/scoped-spawn.test.cjs (16 tests across 11 suites — happy path single key, multiple keys via keyNames, keyNames-unspecified → list+fetch-all, parent process.env deep-equal snapshot before/after, collision gad-266 G project-overrides-parent, PROJECT_NOT_FOUND default-proceed, PROJECT_NOT_FOUND requireBag-throws, DECRYPT_FAILED rewrap of PASSPHRASE_INVALID + BAG_CORRUPT with .cause, SPAWN_FAILED synchronous-throw with .cause, GAD_PROJECT_ID injection wins over bag value, options passthrough cwd/stdio/shell, createScopedSpawner dep-shape validation, TypeError on empty projectId/command). 16/16 pass. Smoke: `node -e "require('...').createScopedSpawner"` returns function. No CLI wiring (that is 59-07 daily-subagent dispatch).

task.goal: Key lifecycle UX: rotation flow (generate new, re-encrypt, zero-out old buffer), revocation flow (remove from bag + record revocation event in audit log), audit log at .gad/secrets/<projectid>.audit.jsonl (append-only, gitignored).
task.notes: Delivered library-only layer at lib/secrets-lifecycle.cjs wrapping store.rotate/revoke with richer audit events (eventId, eventType, actor, details, reason). purgeExpired uses option B — direct envelope read/mutate/write with no re-encryption (each AEAD slot stays intact; purge only deletes the version entry from plaintext JSON metadata). Fail-closed ordering: store op runs first, audit append second; AuditWriteError surfaces fs failures without rolling back committed state (at-least-once audit semantics, documented in source). Envelope writes are atomic (tmp + rename). Current version is guarded from purge even when retiresAt is in the past (malformed-envelope defense). LIFECYCLE_EVENT_TYPES frozen at ['rotate','revoke','purge','bag-created']. 17 tests passing in tests/secrets-lifecycle.test.cjs. Follow-up todos: CLI wiring (gad env audit, auto-purge on startup) is a separate task; consider exposing a store-level writeEnvelope helper if future tasks need mutation without duplicating envelope.cjs plumbing.

task.goal: CLI wiring for the lib/secrets-lifecycle.cjs layer delivered in 60-05. Two new subcommands: `gad env audit <projectid> [--since ISO] [--limit N] [--json]` (newest-first audit-log view, wraps secretsLifecycle.auditLog) and `gad env purge <projectid> [--as-of ISO] [--dry-run] [--json]` (manual grace-period purge, wraps secretsLifecycle.purgeExpired). Plus a best-effort auto-purge preflight in `gad start` — scans all projects with an existing .gad/secrets/<id>.enc envelope, calls purgeExpired on each, logs one-line summary to stderr, NEVER fails start on purge error (swallow + log). Library extension in lib/env-cli.cjs adds auditCmd + purgeCmd following the same dep-injection + guarded error pattern. Tests in tests/env-cli.test.cjs (audit + purge describe blocks) with lifecycle mocks matching the existing store-mock shape.
task.implementation: lib/secrets-lifecycle.cjs gains `previewPurge({projectId,asOf})` (read-only counterpart to purgeExpired — same {purgedCount,byKey} shape, no envelope write, no audit append). lib/env-cli.cjs gains `auditCmd({projectid,since,limit,json})` and `purgeCmd({projectid,asOf,dryRun,json})` following the existing guarded + dep-injection pattern; createEnvCli now accepts a `lifecycle` dep. renderAuditTable + summarizeAuditDetails formatters added for the text-mode view (TS EVENT KEY ACTOR DETAILS columns, rotate/revoke/purge summaries). bin/gad.cjs adds envAuditCmd + envPurgeCmd citty definitions under envCmd.subCommands (description string refreshed to include audit+purge). New lib/startup-purge.cjs exposes listProjectEnvelopes + purgeExpiredAllProjects + renderStartupPurgeSummary — wired into startCmd.run as a best-effort preflight that swallows all errors and prints a single stderr line. Tests: tests/env-cli.test.cjs +10 cases across audit (6) and purge (7) plus renderAuditTable (2); tests/secrets-lifecycle.test.cjs +4 cases under previewPurge; tests/startup-purge.test.cjs new file with 11 cases across listProjectEnvelopes (4) + purgeExpiredAllProjects (4) + renderStartupPurgeSummary (4). 78/78 relevant tests pass. Monorepo consumes via `node vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs env {audit,purge}` until next `gad install`; installed gad.exe (v1.33.0, pre-ea1ad32) does not yet surface the new subcommands. Follow-up: operator-level todo filed at custom_portfolio/.planning/todos/2026-04-18-gad-tasks-add-cli-gap.md for the missing `gad tasks add` CLI (had to hand-edit XML to register this task).

task.goal: Migrate llm-from-scratch OPENAI_API_KEY from global process.env into its project bag. Subagent wrapper for llm-from-scratch uses scoped-spawn + 'gad env get' to decrypt at dispatch time. Documents the expected migration in references/byok-design.md §Migration.
task.notes: Delivered library-only at lib/openai-client.cjs: createOpenAIClient({store,fetchImpl,env,logger}) + createApiKeyResolver + OpenAIClientError. Resolution policy: bag-first via store.get, env-fallback with deprecation warn that names `gad env set OPENAI_API_KEY --projectid <id>`. Security-critical errors (PASSPHRASE_INVALID, BAG_CORRUPT, KEYCHAIN_LOCKED) propagate and NEVER silently fall back to env. Legacy plain-Error shapes with /no envelope for project/i or /KEY_NOT_FOUND/i also route to fall-through (defensive — matches 60-04 pattern).
